title: FLAT: Feedforward Latent Triangle Splatting for Geometrically Accurate Scene Generation
figure: First method to decode triangle splats directly from video diffusion latents via feedforward processing
abstract: FLAT introduces a novel approach for generating explorable 3D scenes from single images by decoding triangle splats from video diffusion latents. It improves geometric accuracy and visual quality compared to existing methods by utilizing a ray-centered rotation parameterization and a product window function for better gradient flow. The method supports real-time rendering and provides a systematic

title: FLUX3D: High-Fidelity 3D Gaussian Generation with Diffusion-Aligned Sparse Representation
figure: First scalable image-to-3D Gaussian Splatting framework that improves representation learning and cross-modal alignment via diffusion.
abstract: FLUX3D addresses the challenges in high-fidelity 3D Gaussian generation from images by enhancing representation learning and aligning 2D image features with 3D voxel latents. It introduces a novel diffusion framework that significantly improves the quality of generated 3D Gaussian assets, outperforming existing methods in fidelity.

title: MM-TRELLIS: Point-Cloud Guided Multi-Modal 3D Vehicle Generation in Autonomous Driving
figure: First multi-modal 3D vehicle generation that integrates LiDAR and image sensors via native 3D generative models
abstract: MM-TRELLIS is a multi-modal approach for generating realistic 3D vehicle models from autonomous driving scenes. It utilizes both multi-view images and LiDAR point clouds to enhance geometric accuracy and cross-view consistency. The method outperforms existing techniques in high-fidelity vehicle generation, with code available for public use.

title: 3DCarGen: Scalable 3D Car Generation via 3D-consistent Multi-view Synthesis
figure: First scalable framework for 3D car generation via multi-view synthesis.
abstract: 3DCarGen is a framework for generating high-quality 3D car models from single images, addressing geometric inconsistencies in multi-view diffusion methods. It synthesizes multiple 3D-consistent images and employs a feed-forward model for reconstruction, achieving robust fidelity and consistency in 3D vehicle assets for autonomous driving simulations.

title: Inclusive Interactive Collisions for Multi-View Consistent Compositional 3D Generation
figure: First method to generate multi-view consistent compositional 3D assets via Inclusive Interactive Collisions.
abstract: I2C-3D addresses challenges in generating multi-object compositional 3D assets by ensuring reasonable interactions among Gaussian primitives and enhancing multi-view consistency through a novel optimization-based approach. The method improves generation quality and supports flexible 3D editing, outperforming existing techniques.

title: Sat2City v2: Native 3D City Asset Generation from a Single Satellite Image
figure: First framework for generating explicit 3D city assets from a single satellite image via a structured-latent 3D foundation model.
abstract: Sat2City v2 enhances the generation of 3D city assets from satellite images by utilizing a pretrained structured-latent 3D model. It addresses previous limitations by fine-tuning a geometry flow and enabling controllable texturing, resulting in improved performance in generating textured mesh assets. The work is supported by a new dataset of satellite-mesh pairs.

title: Arbor: Explicit Geometric Conditioning for Controllable 3D Asset Generation
figure: First method to use explicit geometric conditioning for controllable 3D asset generation via text and image prompts.
abstract: Arbor introduces a novel control interface for 3D asset generation that allows for explicit geometric constraints, improving the ability to generate objects that fit specific spatial requirements. By converting constraint meshes into tokens, Arbor enhances the generation process while maintaining object quality and variation.

title: MeshFlow: Mesh Generation with Equivariant Flow Matching
figure: First mesh generation method that respects symmetries via equivariant flow matching.
abstract: MeshFlow directly generates triangle meshes as triangle soups, addressing the challenges of mesh representation symmetries. It employs equivariant optimal-transport flow matching models and modifies the Diffusion Transformer architecture for efficient mesh generation. The method achieves high-quality meshes with significant speed improvements during inference. Project page is available.

title: JanusMesh: Fast and Zero-Shot 3D Visual Illusion Generation via Cross-Space Denoising
figure: First fast and zero-shot framework for generating text-driven 3D visual illusions via cross-space denoising.
abstract: JanusMesh introduces a novel framework for generating 3D visual illusions that change semantics based on viewing angles. It employs a two-stage process involving cross-space denoising for voxel alignment and view-conditioned texture synthesis. The method achieves high realism and efficiency, outperforming existing techniques in geometric integrity and semantic recognizability.

title: TriFlow: Generating Artist-Like 3D Mesh Topology via Nearest-Vertex Vector Fields
figure: First generative model for producing artist-like 3D mesh topology via nearest-vertex vector fields.
abstract: TriFlow introduces a novel method for generating compact 3D meshes with artist-like topology from input geometry conditions like signed distance fields. It utilizes a nearest-vertex vector field to represent mesh topology and employs a latent flow-matching model for synthesis. The approach enhances topology quality and generalization, achieving significant improvements over existing methods.

title: PointDiffusion: Diffusion-Based Scene Completion in the Point Cloud Domain
figure: First stable scene-scale LiDAR completion model using multi-token Gaussian VAE via diffusion.
abstract: PointDiffusion addresses the challenge of reconstructing dense 3D scenes from sparse LiDAR point clouds by introducing a multi-token Gaussian VAE with cross-attention pooling. This model enables scaffold-free single-step diffusion completion, significantly reducing inference latency and improving accuracy compared to existing methods.

title: MUSE: Agentic 3D Scene Authoring via Memory-Grounded Incremental Requirement Satisfaction
figure: First memory-grounded framework for controllable 3D scene authoring via incremental requirement satisfaction
abstract: MUSE is a memory-grounded multi-agent framework for controllable 3D scene generation and editing. It improves scene authoring by unifying construction and editing processes, allowing for requirement-level state tracking and preservation-aware editing. The framework demonstrates significant improvements in success rates for both construction and editing tasks, supported by human evaluations.

title: World Tracing: Generative Pixel-Aligned Geometry Beyond the Visible
figure: First generative pixel-aligned geometry representation that predicts 3D points beyond the visible surface via diffusion.
abstract: World Tracing introduces a generative pixel-aligned geometry representation that predicts 3D points aligned with input pixels while completing geometry beyond the visible surface. It employs a diffusion transformer to achieve strong performance in visible-surface reconstruction and complete geometry generation across various benchmarks, enabling text-driven 3D scene editing and integration with te

title: ISAP-3D: Identity-Slot Aligned Part-Aware 3D Generation
figure: First part-aware 3D generation framework that aligns identity and slots for stable geometry synthesis.
abstract: ISAP-3D introduces a framework for part-aware 3D generation that addresses structural ambiguity by aligning part identities with generation slots. This method enhances stability and robustness in generating structured objects by employing identity-conditioned layout prediction and geometry synthesis. A new part-level dataset is also introduced to support this approach.

title: 3D-CBM: A Framework for Concept-Based Interpretability in Generative 3D Modeling
figure: First framework integrating Concept Bottleneck Models into 3D generative modeling for interpretability.
abstract: This paper presents a framework that integrates Concept Bottleneck Models into 3D generative architectures to enhance interpretability in deep geometric learning. It addresses the semantic gap in 3D content creation, focusing on mapping raw geometric inputs into interpretable primitives. The framework shows promise in concept prediction accuracy and allows for interactive error correction, paving

title: P3D-Bench: Benchmarking MLLMs for Parametric 3D Generation and Structural Reasoning
figure: First benchmark for evaluating parametric 3D generation via multimodal large language models.
abstract: P3D-Bench introduces a benchmark for evaluating parametric 3D generation from text and images, focusing on geometric precision and structural reasoning. It assesses models on their ability to generate executable 3D programs that align with specified designs, covering tasks like Text-to-3D and Image-to-3D. The evaluation reveals challenges in part-level modeling and assembly coherence.

title: ABot-Earth 0.5: Generative 3D Earth Model
figure: First generative model for synthesizing 3D environments from satellite imagery via 3D Gaussian Splatting.
abstract: ABot-Earth 0.5 is a generative framework that synthesizes seamless 3D environments from satellite imagery using a novel 3D Gaussian Splatting model. It generates realistic geometry and textures, enabling rapid scene synthesis and real-time visualization, while addressing the sim-to-real domain gap for applications like UAV navigation.

title: EditSSC: Toward Editable Semantic Occupancy Scenes with Unconditional Diffusion Models
figure: First method for editable 3D semantic scene generation using unconditional diffusion models.
abstract: EditSSC introduces a method for 3D semantic scene generation that utilizes 2D Bird's Eye View representations and latent diffusion networks. It allows for editing capabilities without retraining, outperforming existing baselines on unconditional generation tasks. The approach demonstrates the effectiveness of repurposing 2D architectures for 3D scene generation and editing.

title: CP4D: Compositional Physics-aware 4D Scene Generation
figure: First 4D scene generation that integrates static environments with dynamic objects via physical dynamics.
abstract: CP4D introduces a novel approach to 4D scene generation that combines static 3D environments with dynamic, physically plausible objects. It employs a three-stage pipeline to generate high-fidelity representations, synthesize realistic object trajectories, and compose coherent 4D scenes, achieving high visual fidelity and physical plausibility.

title: Rethinking 3D Shape Generation: Diffusion over Superquadrics
figure: First diffusion model that generates 3D shapes using superquadrics for improved efficiency.
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to 3D shape generation using diffusion models over superquadrics, significantly reducing computational demands while maintaining high fidelity. By representing shapes with a compact set of parameters, the method enhances scalability and supports advanced capabilities like part-level editing, achieving efficient generation times.

title: Neural Field Tokenizations with Hierarchy and Spatial Locality Priors
figure: First framework to learn general-purpose tokenized representations of continuous signals via locality and hierarchy priors.
abstract: LH-NeF introduces a framework for learning tokenized representations of continuous signals, including 3D shapes, using locality and hierarchy as priors. This approach replaces memory-intensive meta-learning with a single forward pass, significantly reducing memory usage and supporting larger batch sizes. The method achieves competitive performance across various modalities, including images and 3D

title: EvoGS: Constructing Continuous-Layered Gaussian Splatting with Evolution Tree for Scalable 3D Streaming
figure: First continuous-layering representation for scalable 3D streaming via Evolution Tree
abstract: EvoGS introduces a continuous-layering representation for 3D Gaussian Splatting, organized as an Evolution Tree. This method generates finer details through parent-child refinement, reducing splat redundancy and improving transmission efficiency. It significantly enhances real-time adaptive streaming quality.

title: 3DMorph: Single-Image-Guided Local 3D Shape Editing and Morphing
figure: First training-free framework for single-image-guided local 3D shape editing and morphing.
abstract: 3DMorph addresses the challenge of intuitive local shape editing in 3D by allowing users to modify shapes based on 2D images. The method localizes relevant 3D regions and applies 2D edits while preserving unmodified areas, enabling intermediate shape generation. A new benchmark, Delta3D, is introduced to evaluate editing quality, showing that 3DMorph outperforms existing methods.

title: GuideCAD: A Lightweight Multimodal Framework for 3D CAD Model Generation via Prefix Embedding
figure: First lightweight multimodal framework for 3D CAD model generation via prefix embedding.
abstract: GuideCAD is a multimodal framework designed for efficient 3D CAD model generation, utilizing a mapping network to convert image embeddings into prefix embeddings. This allows a pretrained language model to integrate visual and textual information, enabling a transformer-based decoder to predict construction sequences for generating 3D CAD models. The method shows improved efficiency and quality co

title: ARAPDiffusion: ARAP Regularization for Diffusion-Based Deformable Shape Space Learning
figure: First latent diffusion model that incorporates ARAP regularization for shape generation.
abstract: ARAPDiffusion presents a latent diffusion model that learns the continuous shape space of deformable shapes by integrating ARAP deformation as regularization. This approach enhances both the encoder/decoder and the diffusion model, allowing for effective shape generation with limited training data. The method shows improved performance in generating shapes from both unconditional and conditional i

title: HomeWorld: A Unified Floorplan-to-Furnished Framework for Generating Controllable, Densely Interactive Whole-Home Scenes
figure: First unified framework for generating controllable whole-home scenes via hierarchical decomposition
abstract: HomeWorld introduces a hierarchical framework for generating indoor scenes, addressing challenges in layout coherence and realism. It utilizes a large dataset for floorplan generation and employs image generation models for furniture and object layout, enhancing diversity and design appeal. The method includes a 3D generative model for asset replacement and aims to support embodied AI applications

title: Global-Local Monte Carlo Tree Search in Vision-Language Models for Text-to-3D Indoor Scene Generation
figure: First text-to-3D indoor scene generation using global-local Monte Carlo tree search with diffusion models.
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of text-to-3D indoor scene generation using large vision-language models. It proposes a global-local tree search approach to optimize object placement in scenes, leveraging a new hierarchical scene representation and a PRM-guided MCTS method. The method aims to improve realism in generated scenes and introduces a diverse dataset for evaluation.

title: Controllable Dynamic 3D Shape Generation via 3D Trajectories and Text
figure: First controllable dynamic 3D shape generation via 3D trajectories and text.
abstract: T2Mo is a feed-forward framework that generates controllable dynamic 3D shapes based on 3D trajectories and text. It addresses the challenges of language ambiguity by using trajectories for spatial guidance, ensuring object motions adhere to specified paths while reflecting text semantics. The method includes a shape-grounded trajectory embedding for robust handling of various trajectory configura

title: MeshWeaver: Sparse-Voxel-Guided Surface Weaving for Autoregressive Mesh Generation
figure: First autoregressive mesh generation framework that incorporates sparse-voxel guidance for improved geometric fidelity.
abstract: MeshWeaver is an autoregressive framework for mesh generation that addresses limitations in tokenization efficiency and geometry awareness. It predicts the next vertex in a surface weaving process, utilizing a multi-level sparse-voxel encoder to enhance the generative model with geometric context. This approach allows for high-quality mesh generation with improved fidelity and efficiency.

title: MeshFlow: Efficient Artistic Mesh Generation via MeshVAE and Flow-based Diffusion Transformer
figure: First efficient artistic mesh generator via VAE and flow-based diffusion.
abstract: MeshFlow introduces a novel method for generating artistic 3D meshes, overcoming the limitations of traditional auto-regressive methods. By utilizing a Variational Autoencoder for continuous vertex representation and a Rectified Flow transformer for parallel generation, it achieves significant speed and accuracy improvements in mesh generation.

title: SymTRELLIS: Symmetry-Enforced Voxel Latents for 3D Generation
figure: First method to enforce symmetry in flow-based 3D generation via learned linear operators on voxel latents
abstract: SymTRELLIS introduces a method for enforcing symmetry in single-view 3D generative models during flow-based generation. By approximating latent-space actions of spatial transformations, it allows for symmetry enforcement without retraining the underlying model. The approach significantly reduces symmetry errors while maintaining reconstruction accuracy, demonstrating its effectiveness on a benchma

title: MORPHOS: Autoregressive 4D Generation with Temporal Structured Latents
figure: First autoregressive framework for dynamic 3D asset generation from videos using Temporal Structured Latents.
abstract: MORPHOS is an autoregressive framework that generates dynamic 3D assets from videos using various representations like meshes and 3D Gaussians. It introduces Temporal Structured Latents to maintain temporal consistency and handle topological changes, achieving state-of-the-art performance in appearance and competitive results in geometry.

title: PhyScene3D: Physically Consistent Interactive 3D Tabletop Scene Generation
figure: First framework for physically consistent interactive 3D tabletop scene generation via Human-Mimetic Constructive Process
abstract: PhyScene3D addresses the challenge of generating physically consistent 3D tabletop scenes for interactive robotic learning. It reformulates scene generation into a sequential process using a Cognitive Topological Reasoning Chain and integrates a differentiable Signed Distance Field with Test-Time Optimization to ensure physical feasibility while maintaining semantic intent. The method shows signif

title: Learning Neural Deformation Representation for 4D Dynamic Shape Generation
figure: First method for 4D dynamic shape generation via neural deformation representation.
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach for generating 4D dynamic shapes by introducing a new neural deformation representation that separates motion and shape latent spaces. The method improves upon previous works by enhancing temporal consistency and rendering speed, demonstrating superior performance in various generation tasks.

title: SuperVoxelGPT: Adaptive and Ordered 3D Tokenization for Autoregressive Shape Generation
figure: First adaptive and ordered 3D tokenization for autoregressive shape generation via SuperVoxelVAE.
abstract: SuperVoxelGPT introduces a novel framework for 3D shape generation that utilizes adaptive and ordered supervoxel tokenization to improve the efficiency and quality of autoregressive models. By predicting a geometric saliency distribution and employing centroidal Voronoi tessellation, it optimizes token sequences, achieving significant reductions in length and improvements in generation speed and q

title: GAP3D: Generative Alignment of VLM Latents to Patch-Level Embeddings for 3D Generation
figure: First modular diffusion-based approach aligning VLM latents to patch-level embeddings for 3D asset generation.
abstract: GAP3D introduces a modular, diffusion-based method that aligns vision-language model latents to patch-level features of an image encoder, facilitating 3D asset generation without extensive 3D data. It shows zero-shot capabilities for multimodal prompts and aims to bridge the representation gap between VLM and image-encoder features.

title: Helix4D: Complex 4D Mesh Generation
figure: First dynamic mesh generation framework for complex 4D scenes via video conditioning
abstract: Helix4D is a dynamic mesh generation framework that adapts the Trellis2 model for video-conditioned 4D generation. It addresses challenges in complex topology changes and transparent materials by employing a sliding-window cross-frame attention mechanism and a novel 4D temporal encoding. Extensive experiments demonstrate its effectiveness in generating high-quality dynamic meshes.

title: CodecSplat: Ultra-Compact Latent Coding for Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting
figure: First ultra-compact latent coding framework for feed-forward 3D Gaussian splatting.
abstract: CodecSplat introduces a novel framework for compressing 3D Gaussian splatting by encoding intermediate features into a compact bitstream, enhancing storage and transmission efficiency while maintaining high-quality scene representation.

title: BrickAnything: Geometry-Conditioned Buildable Brick Generation with Structure-Aware Tokenization
figure: First geometry-conditioned framework for generating buildable brick structures via autoregressive modeling
abstract: BrickAnything is an autoregressive framework that generates physically buildable brick structures from diverse 3D representations. It uses point clouds and a structure-aware tree tokenization to ensure the generated sequences adhere to assembly constraints, improving the consistency and validity of the output. The method enhances buildability objectives and demonstrates effective performance in pr

title: Metric--Phase Fields: Decoupling Distance and Sign for Thin-Structure Reconstruction from Unoriented Point Clouds
figure: First decoupled implicit representation for thin-structure reconstruction from unoriented point clouds via Metric-Phase Fields.
abstract: Metric-Phase Fields (MPFs) introduce a novel implicit representation that separates metric proximity from topological phase, enabling better reconstruction of thin structures from unoriented point clouds. The method combines unsigned metric fields with smooth phase fields to improve surface extraction and training stability, outperforming traditional SDF and UDF methods. Experiments show MPFs effe

title: Fishbone: From One 3D Asset to a Million Controllable Edits
figure: First unified rib-spine representation for controllable parametric mesh deformation
abstract: Fishbone introduces a rib-spine representation for generating diverse 3D assets through controllable mesh deformation. It allows real-time adjustments of local and global shape features, enhancing applications in 3D generation, interactive editing, and robot learning. The method is efficient and versatile, addressing challenges in creating varied 3D assets.

title: AnySurf: Any Surface Generation with Directed Edge
figure: First unified framework for generating open, closed, and hybrid 3D surfaces with accurate face orientation.
abstract: AnySurf is a framework designed for generating various types of 3D surfaces, including open and closed forms, with a focus on accurate face orientation. It addresses limitations in existing methods that struggle with open surfaces and proposes enhancements to improve mesh quality. The framework is built on a directed-edge representation and includes a new dataset for industrial garments and access

title: AssetGen: Deployable 3D Asset Generation at Interactive Speed
figure: First interactive 3D asset generator that produces high-quality meshes from a single reference image in under 30 seconds.
abstract: AssetGen is a 3D generator that prioritizes user experience and deployability, producing high-quality meshes and textures from a reference image in 30 seconds. It employs a coarse-to-refine framework for geometry generation and optimizes the pipeline for speed, achieving competitive visual quality against commercial solutions.

title: PhysX-Omni: Unified Simulation-Ready Physical 3D Generation for Rigid, Deformable, and Articulated Objects
figure: First unified framework for simulation-ready physical 3D generation across diverse asset types.
abstract: PhysX-Omni introduces a unified framework for generating simulation-ready physical 3D assets, addressing limitations of existing methods that focus on single asset categories. It features a novel geometry representation for Vision-Language Models and the PhysXVerse dataset for diverse categories. The framework shows strong performance in generation and understanding, with potential applications in

title: Stream3D: Sequential Multi-View 3D Generation via Evidential Memory
figure: First training-free streaming mechanism for view-conditioned 3D generation via evidential memory.
abstract: Stream3D introduces a novel approach to 3D generation from streaming monocular views, addressing temporal inconsistencies in generated outputs. By utilizing a compact evidential memory to cache informative historical frames, it maintains performance without retraining the underlying generator. Evaluations show it surpasses existing methods in both photometric and geometric metrics.

title: ROAR-3D: Routing Arbitrary Views for High-Fidelity 3D Generation
figure: First method that upgrades single-view models for arbitrary multi-view 3D generation via a token-wise view router
abstract: ROAR-3D enhances single-image-to-3D generative models by allowing them to accept multiple unposed images, improving geometry generation quality. It uses a token-wise view router to establish 2D-to-3D correspondences and a dual-stream attention design to maintain primary-view behavior while enriching geometry. This method achieves state-of-the-art results in multi-view 3D generation with minimal ad

title: CAdam: Context-Adaptive Moment Estimation for 3D Gaussian Densification in Generative Distillation
figure: First context-adaptive moment estimation for 3D Gaussian densification in generative distillation.
abstract: CAdam introduces a novel framework for 3D Gaussian densification in generative distillation, addressing limitations of standard methods. By leveraging gradient moments and a context-aware mechanism, it effectively separates geometric signals from noise, improving memory efficiency and reducing Gaussian count while maintaining perceptual quality across various objectives.

title: Structural Energy Guidance for View-Consistent Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First method to improve multi-view consistency in text-to-3D generation via structural energy guidance.
abstract: This paper addresses the Janus problem in text-to-3D generation by proposing Structural Energy-Guided Sampling (SEGS), which enhances multi-view consistency without retraining. By constructing a structural energy in the PCA subspace of U-Net features, SEGS effectively reduces viewpoint artifacts while maintaining appearance fidelity, demonstrating improvements across various baselines.

title: Efficient 3D Content Reconstruction and Generation
figure: First efficient 3D content generation system that combines multi-view diffusion with feed-forward reconstruction.
abstract: This work presents Instant3D, a system for automatic 3D content creation that synthesizes assets from text or images. It leverages multi-view diffusion and feed-forward techniques to generate high-quality 3D models quickly, while also addressing 3D reconstruction through a fast structure-from-motion pipeline. The approach aims to streamline asset creation for various applications, including gaming

title: QuadLink: Autoregressive Quad-Dominant Mesh Generation via Point-Relation Learning
figure: First quad-dominant mesh generator that links points into structured faces via a hybrid model
abstract: QuadLink is a framework for generating quad-dominant meshes from point clouds, addressing the limitations of existing methods that produce only triangular or isotropic meshes. It employs a centroid-conditioned vertex linking model to create sparse and anisotropic meshes with coherent edge flow, supporting hybrid polygonal topology. The method shows improved geometric fidelity and topological quali

title: EVA01: Unified Native 3D Understanding and Generation via Mixture-of-Transformers
figure: First unified framework for native 3D understanding and generation via Mixture-of-Transformers.
abstract: EVA01 integrates 3D meshes into Multimodal Large Language Models, enabling native understanding, generation, and editing of 3D shapes. It features a Mixture-of-Transformers architecture that aligns semantic and geometric spaces, achieving state-of-the-art text-to-3D generation and robust geometric editing. The framework enhances the integration of 2D models with 3D tasks.

title: WorldAct: Activating Monolithic 3D Worlds into Interactive-Ready Object-Centric Scenes
figure: First framework that converts static 3D worlds into editable and interaction-ready object-centric scenes via multimodal agent guidance.
abstract: WorldAct transforms static generated 3D worlds into editable and interactive scenes by decomposing scenes, identifying actionable objects, and reconstructing object-level meshes. This allows for object-level editing and manipulation while maintaining scene coherence, enhancing interaction scenarios compared to original outputs.

title: Sat3DGen: Comprehensive Street-Level 3D Scene Generation from Single Satellite Image
figure: First geometry-first methodology for street-level 3D scene generation from satellite images via feed-forward framework
abstract: Sat3DGen addresses the challenges of generating street-level 3D scenes from satellite images by integrating geometric constraints with a perspective-view training strategy. This approach significantly improves both 3D accuracy and photorealism, as validated by a new benchmark. The method shows versatility in various applications, including semantic-map-to-3D synthesis and large-scale meshing. Code

title: Real2Sim: A Physics-driven and Editable Gaussian Splatting Framework for Autonomous Driving Scenes
figure: First framework that combines 4D Gaussian Splatting with a differentiable Material Point Method for editable driving scene generation.
abstract: Real2Sim is a framework that integrates 4D Gaussian Splatting with a physics-aware solver to generate dynamic driving scenes. It allows for instance-level editing and simulates realistic interactions, addressing challenges in traditional simulation methods. The framework shows potential for scalable data generation in various autonomous driving tasks.

title: GTA: Advancing Image-to-3D World Generation via Geometry Then Appearance Video Diffusion
figure: First image-to-3D world generation method that follows a Geometry-Then-Appearance paradigm via video diffusion.
abstract: GTA is a novel image-to-3D world generation method that improves structural fidelity by generating coarse geometric structures followed by fine-grained appearance synthesis. It employs a two-stage framework with dedicated video diffusion models and introduces strategies for enhancing cross-view consistency. Extensive experiments show its superiority in fidelity and visual quality, making it a vers

title: PG-3DGS: Optimizing 3D Gaussian Splatting to Satisfy Physics Objectives
figure: First 3D Gaussian Splatting method that incorporates physics objectives for shape optimization.
abstract: PG-3DGS combines differentiable physics simulation with 3D Gaussian representations to generate geometries that are visually accurate and physically functional. The framework optimizes shapes to meet physical objectives, resulting in objects that exhibit meaningful behaviors, such as teapots that pour and airplanes that generate lift. Experiments demonstrate improved physical functionality without

title: Pixal3D: Pixel-Aligned 3D Generation from Images
figure: First pixel-aligned 3D generation from images via a new conditioning scheme
abstract: Pixal3D introduces a pixel-aligned 3D generation paradigm that enhances fidelity in image-to-3D synthesis by establishing direct pixel-to-3D correspondence. It generates 3D assets in a way that is consistent with the input view, improving quality and scalability. The method also extends to multi-view generation, enabling high-fidelity, object-separated 3D scenes from images.

title: Beyond Spatial Compression: Interface-Centric Generative States for Open-World 3D Structure
figure: First interface-centric generative states for open-world 3D structure via tokenization.
abstract: The paper introduces interface-centric generative states for 3D representation, addressing issues in current tokenizers that treat representation as mere spatial compression. The proposed C2LT-3D method enhances structural robustness in open-world assets by exposing local geometry and component relationships, allowing for better assembly-level reasoning and validation during decoding.

title: AdaptSplat: Adapting Vision Foundation Models for Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting
figure: First lightweight adapter for feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting that improves geometric fidelity.
abstract: AdaptSplat introduces a lightweight adapter for feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting, enhancing performance without complex designs. It effectively integrates high-frequency structural priors from vision models, improving the accuracy of Gaussian primitives on complex surfaces. The method achieves state-of-the-art reconstruction performance and generalizes well across domains.

title: Probability-Flow Distillation: Exact Wasserstein Gradient Flow for High-Fidelity 3D Generation
figure: First method to achieve high-fidelity 3D generation via Wasserstein gradient flow.
abstract: This paper introduces Probability-Flow Distillation (PFD), an extension of Score Distillation Sampling that addresses mode collapse in text-to-3D generation. By employing a Wasserstein gradient flow, PFD enhances the sampling capacity and produces high-fidelity 3D assets with improved quality compared to previous methods.

title: MeshFIM: Local Low-Poly Mesh Editing via Fill-in-the-Middle Autoregressive Generation
figure: First autoregressive model for local low-poly mesh editing via Fill-in-the-Middle generation.
abstract: MeshFIM introduces a Fill-in-the-Middle framework for autoregressive generation of low-poly meshes, allowing targeted regeneration of local regions while preserving surrounding context. It addresses mesh-specific challenges and demonstrates applications in interactive editing and defect repair, outperforming existing methods in mesh refinement and generation.

title: Generative 3D Gaussians with Learned Density Control
figure: First 3D Gaussian representation with adaptive density control via latent diffusion.
abstract: The paper introduces Density-Sampled Gaussians (DeG), a flexible 3D representation that optimizes Gaussian centers based on a learnable density function. It addresses challenges in applying diffusion to unordered latents and proposes a new mechanism for robust sequence modeling. The method achieves state-of-the-art results in single-image-to-3D generation, combining adaptive primitives with stable

title: DVD: Discrete Voxel Diffusion for 3D Generation and Editing
figure: First discrete voxel diffusion framework for 3D generation and editing.
abstract: DVD introduces a discrete diffusion framework for generating and editing sparse voxels in 3D generative pipelines. It treats voxel occupancy as a discrete variable, enhancing interpretability and quality in voxel generation. The method includes uncertainty estimation and a fine-tuning strategy for efficient voxel inpainting and editing. Code is available at the provided GitHub link.

title: Structured 3D Latents Are Surprisingly Powerful: Unleashing Generalizable Style with 2D Diffusion
figure: First method to leverage 2D diffusion for style-controllable 3D asset generation.
abstract: DiLAST introduces a novel approach for 3D asset generation that utilizes a pretrained 2D diffusion model to enhance style transfer capabilities. By optimizing structured 3D latent representations, the method effectively generates diverse 3D objects with out-of-distribution styles, demonstrating significant improvements in style controllability and generalization.

title: MOC-3D: Manifold-Order Consistency for Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First text-to-3D generation method that addresses macro-topological inconsistency and micro-geometric discontinuity via manifold-order consistency.
abstract: MOC-3D is a text-to-3D generation method that improves structural consistency and detail continuity by addressing challenges in existing Score Distillation Sampling methods. It utilizes a Semantic View-Order Constraint Module and a Manifold-based Feature Continuity Module to enhance the generation of 3D objects, making it suitable for applications in the Metaverse and Virtual Reality.

title: Map2World: Segment Map Conditioned Text to 3D World Generation
figure: First framework for 3D world generation conditioned on segment maps via a detail enhancer network
abstract: Map2World introduces a framework for generating 3D worlds based on user-defined segment maps, addressing issues of scale consistency and flexibility. It includes a detail enhancer network to add fine details while maintaining scene coherence. The method shows improved user-controllability and performance over existing approaches, allowing for complex 3D world generation.

title: REVIVE 3D: Refinement via Encoded Voluminous Inflated prior for Volume Enhancement
figure: First pipeline that generates voluminous 3D assets from flat images via a two-stage process
abstract: REVIVE 3D is a two-stage pipeline designed to generate voluminous 3D assets from flat images. It constructs an Inflated Prior to recover global volume and adds part-aware details. The second stage involves 3D Latent Refinement, which enhances the generated volume using Gaussian noise and geometric cues. The framework also supports image-conditioned 3D editing and introduces metrics for assessing v

title: ProcFunc: Function-Oriented Abstractions for Procedural 3D Generation in Python
figure: First library for procedural 3D generation in Python with easy-to-use functions
abstract: ProcFunc is a library designed for Blender-based procedural 3D generation in Python, facilitating the creation and execution of procedural generation code. It enables the generation of diverse training data and includes a procedural generator for indoor rooms, showcasing efficiency and diversity in 3D synthetic data generation.

title: Prox-E: Fine-Grained 3D Shape Editing via Primitive-Based Abstractions
figure: First fine-grained 3D shape editing method via primitive-based abstractions.
abstract: Prox-E introduces a training-free framework for fine-grained 3D shape editing using geometric primitives. It abstracts a 3D shape into a set of primitives, which are then edited by a vision-language model to guide a 3D generative model for localized modifications while preserving the overall identity of the shape. The method outperforms existing 2D-based and training-based 3D editing approaches.

title: GenAssets: Generating in-the-wild 3D Assets in Latent Space
figure: First 3D latent diffusion model that generates high-quality 3D assets from in-the-wild data.
abstract: GenAssets introduces a 3D latent diffusion model that generates high-quality 3D assets for traffic participants using in-the-wild LiDAR and camera data. The method employs a "reconstruct-then-generate" approach, leveraging occlusion-aware neural rendering to create a latent space for objects, followed by a diffusion model for asset generation. This approach enhances diversity and scalability in co

title: Sculpt4D: Generating 4D Shapes via Sparse-Attention Diffusion Transformers
figure: First framework for generating 4D shapes via sparse-attention diffusion transformers
abstract: Sculpt4D introduces a novel 4D generative framework that enhances temporal modeling within a pretrained 3D Diffusion Transformer. It employs a Block Sparse Attention mechanism to maintain object identity while effectively capturing motion dynamics, achieving high-fidelity 4D synthesis and reducing computational demands.

title: Seed3D 2.0: Advancing High-Fidelity Simulation-Ready 3D Content Generation
figure: First high-fidelity simulation-ready 3D content generation system with part-aware decomposition via VAE.
abstract: Seed3D 2.0 enhances 3D content generation with improved fidelity and simulation capabilities. It features a two-stage pipeline for geometry and a unified model for texture generation, allowing for coherent scene construction and part-level interactions. A human preference study shows it outperforms recent commercial models in textured 3D asset generation. The system is available online.

title: GSCompleter: A Distillation-Free Plugin for Metric-Aware 3D Gaussian Splatting Completion in Seconds
figure: First distillation-free plugin for metric-aware 3D Gaussian splatting completion via a Generate-then-Register workflow
abstract: GSCompleter introduces a novel approach to 3D Gaussian Splatting scene completion, moving away from the traditional distillation method to a more efficient Generate-then-Register workflow. It synthesizes 2D images and converts them into 3D Gaussian primitives, improving completion quality and efficiency while achieving state-of-the-art results across benchmarks.

title: Dream-Cubed: Controllable Generative Modeling in Minecraft by Training on Billions of Cubes
figure: First large-scale study of 3D diffusion models for voxel generation in Minecraft.
abstract: Dream-Cubed introduces a dataset of Minecraft worlds at voxel resolution and models for generating interactive 3D environments using cubes. It explores 3D diffusion models for voxel generation, enabling efficient and semantically grounded creation while supporting user workflows like inpainting. The study includes a human preference evaluation and releases the dataset, code, and pretrained models

title: Asset Harvester: Extracting 3D Assets from Autonomous Driving Logs for Simulation
figure: First system to convert sparse driving logs into complete 3D assets via image-to-3D model.
abstract: Asset Harvester is an image-to-3D model that transforms sparse object observations from autonomous driving logs into complete, simulation-ready 3D assets. It combines object-centric training, geometry-aware preprocessing, and a robust training recipe to enable scalable conversion of real-world data into reusable 3D models.

title: UniMesh: Unifying 3D Mesh Understanding and Generation
figure: First unified framework for 3D mesh understanding and generation via diffusion.
abstract: UniMesh proposes a unified framework that integrates 3D generation and understanding in a single architecture. It features a novel Mesh Head for bridging image generation with implicit shape decoders and introduces Chain of Mesh for user-driven semantic mesh editing. The framework also includes a self-reflection mechanism for diagnosing high-level task failures. Experimental results show competiti

title: Repurposing 3D Generative Model for Autoregressive Layout Generation
figure: First framework that repurposes 3D generative models for autoregressive layout generation in native 3D space.
abstract: LaviGen is a framework that generates 3D layouts directly in 3D space using an autoregressive process. It models geometric relations and physical constraints among objects, producing coherent 3D scenes. The adapted 3D diffusion model enhances efficiency and spatial accuracy, achieving superior performance on the LayoutVLM benchmark.

title: Beyond Prompts: Unconditional 3D Inversion for Out-of-Distribution Shapes
figure: First unconditional 3D shape editing framework that bypasses latent sinks via geometric representation decoupling
abstract: This paper explores the limitations of text-driven inversion in 3D generative models, particularly when they become insensitive to prompt modifications. It presents a framework that leverages the model's unconditional generative prior to enable robust editing of out-of-distribution 3D shapes, enhancing the model's geometric expressivity while addressing current pipeline limitations.

title: HY-World 2.0: A Multi-Modal World Model for Reconstructing, Generating, and Simulating 3D Worlds
figure: First multi-modal world model that generates 3D Gaussian Splatting scenes from diverse inputs.
abstract: HY-World 2.0 is a multi-modal framework that generates navigable 3D world representations from text prompts, single-view, and multi-view images. It enhances panorama fidelity and scene understanding through a four-stage method, achieving state-of-the-art performance in 3D world generation and reconstruction.

title: Rethinking Image-to-3D Generation with Sparse Queries: Efficiency, Capacity, and Input-View Bias
figure: First efficient image-to-3D generation framework using sparse queries via a learned expansion operator
abstract: SparseGen is a novel framework for efficient image-to-3D generation that reduces input-view bias and improves speed. It uses a compact set of learned 3D anchor queries and a learned expansion operator to decode queries into 3D Gaussian primitives. The model is trained without 3D supervision, achieving efficiency in representation and inference while maintaining multi-view fidelity.

title: ReConText3D: Replay-based Continual Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First continual learning framework for text-to-3D generation via replay-based methods
abstract: ReConText3D introduces a continual learning framework for text-to-3D generation, addressing catastrophic forgetting in existing models. It allows generative models to learn new 3D categories incrementally while preserving previously learned assets. The method uses a compact replay memory for effective knowledge retention and is evaluated on the Toys4K-CL benchmark, showing superior performance acr

title: Beyond Voxel 3D Editing: Learning from 3D Masks and Self-Constructed Data
figure: First 3D editing framework that enhances image-to-3D generation with self-constructed datasets and annotation-free masking.
abstract: The BVE framework addresses limitations in 3D editing by utilizing a self-constructed dataset and enhancing an image-to-3D generative model. It introduces a novel 3D masking strategy to maintain local invariance during edits, allowing for high-quality, text-aligned 3D asset generation while preserving original visual characteristics.

title: VVGT: Visual Volume-Grounded Transformer
figure: First volumetric visualization framework that maps data to 3D Gaussian Splatting via a dual-transformer network.
abstract: VVGT is a feed-forward framework that enhances volumetric visualization by mapping volumetric data to a 3D Gaussian Splatting representation. It eliminates the need for per-scene optimization, allowing for scalable and interactive visualization. The method employs a dual-transformer network and integrates multi-view observations, achieving high-quality results and strong generalization across data

title: Any 3D Scene is Worth 1K Tokens: 3D-Grounded Representation for Scene Generation at Scale
figure: First 3D scene generation directly within an implicit 3D latent space via diffusion.
abstract: This paper introduces a novel approach for 3D scene generation that operates directly in an implicit 3D latent space, overcoming limitations of traditional 2D-based methods. By utilizing a 3D Representation Autoencoder and a 3D Diffusion Transformer, the method achieves efficient and spatially consistent generation of 3D scenes from various views, enhancing representation and semantic richness.

title: Strips as Tokens: Artist Mesh Generation with Native UV Segmentation
figure: First mesh generation method using a token ordering strategy inspired by triangle strips via autoregressive transformers
abstract: The paper introduces Strips as Tokens (SATO), a framework for generating artist-quality meshes with a novel token ordering strategy that preserves edge flow and semantic layout. It allows for decoding into both triangle and quadrilateral meshes, enhancing geometric regularity and structural coherence. Experiments show SATO outperforms previous methods in quality and segmentation.

title: SIC3D: Style Image Conditioned Text-to-3D Gaussian Splatting Generation
figure: First controllable image-conditioned text-to-3D generation via 3D Gaussian Splatting.
abstract: SIC3D is a novel pipeline for generating 3D objects from text while conditioning on image styles. It consists of two stages: generating 3D content from text and transferring style from a reference image using a new Variational Stylized Score Distillation loss. This approach improves geometric fidelity and style adherence compared to previous methods.

title: 3DrawAgent: Teaching LLM to Draw in 3D with Early Contrastive Experience
figure: First training-free framework for 3D sketch generation using LLMs and geometric feedback.
abstract: 3DrawAgent is a language-driven framework for generating 3D sketches using large language models. It employs a unique optimization strategy to refine 3D drawing skills through pairwise comparisons of generated sketches, enhancing spatial understanding without parameter updates. The method can produce complex 3D Bezier curves from textual prompts, showcasing emergent geometric reasoning and adaptab

title: SEM-ROVER: Semantic Voxel-Guided Diffusion for Large-Scale Driving Scene Generation
figure: First semantic-conditioned diffusion model for large-scale driving scene generation via voxel representation
abstract: This paper presents SEM-ROVER, a 3D generative framework that utilizes a voxel-based representation for generating large-scale outdoor driving scenes. It employs a semantic-conditioned diffusion model to ensure geometric coherence and multiview consistency, allowing for photorealistic rendering without per-scene optimization. The method scales effectively to large areas and demonstrates diverse sc

title: GaussianGrow: Geometry-aware Gaussian Growing from 3D Point Clouds with Text Guidance
figure: First method to generate 3D Gaussians from point clouds using text guidance via a multi-view diffusion model
abstract: GaussianGrow introduces a novel approach for generating 3D Gaussians from point clouds while ensuring geometric accuracy. It employs a text-guided scheme and a multi-view diffusion model to synthesize consistent appearances, addressing challenges in generating reliable geometries. The method iteratively detects camera poses to inpaint hard-to-observe regions, demonstrating effectiveness on both sy

title: Attention-Guided Flow-Matching for Sparse 3D Geological Generation
figure: First Attention-Guided Continuous Flow Matching framework for sparse multimodal geological modeling.
abstract: 3D-GeoFlow addresses the challenge of generating high-resolution 3D geological models from sparse data. It utilizes a continuous vector field regression approach optimized via Mean Squared Error, integrating 3D Attention Gates to enhance structural coherence. The model outperforms traditional methods and standard diffusion models in generating realistic geological structures.

title: Part-Level 3D Gaussian Vehicle Generation with Joint and Hinge Axis Estimation
figure: First framework to generate animatable 3D Gaussian vehicles from single images via part-edge refinement and kinematic reasoning.
abstract: This paper presents a generative framework for creating animatable 3D Gaussian vehicle models from single images or sparse multi-view inputs. It addresses challenges in part-level articulation and kinematic parameter prediction, enabling realistic simulations for autonomous driving applications.

title: Free-Range Gaussians: Non-Grid-Aligned Generative 3D Gaussian Reconstruction
figure: First method to predict non-grid-aligned 3D Gaussians via flow matching from multi-view images.
abstract: Free-Range Gaussians introduces a method for reconstructing non-grid-aligned 3D Gaussians from a few images, enhancing generative capabilities in unobserved regions. It employs a hierarchical patching scheme and a timestep-weighted rendering loss to improve fidelity, outperforming traditional pixel and voxel-aligned methods in quality and efficiency.

title: 3D-Fixer: Coarse-to-Fine In-place Completion for 3D Scenes from a Single Image
figure: First in-place completion method for 3D scenes from a single image via coarse-to-fine generation.
abstract: 3D-Fixer introduces a novel in-place completion paradigm for compositional 3D scene generation from a single view. It generates complete 3D assets based on partially visible point clouds, using a coarse-to-fine scheme to resolve occlusion ambiguities. The method leverages a dual-branch conditioning network and introduces the ARSG-110K dataset for improved training. Extensive experiments demonstrat

title: SparseSplat: Towards Applicable Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting with Pixel-Unaligned Prediction
figure: First feed-forward 3DGS model that adaptively adjusts Gaussian density according to scene structure.
abstract: SparseSplat introduces a novel feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting model that optimizes Gaussian density based on local scene information, resulting in compact 3DGS maps. It employs entropy-based probabilistic sampling to generate sparse Gaussians in textureless areas and dense Gaussians in rich information regions, achieving high rendering quality with significantly fewer Gaussians.

title: Adaptive Local Frequency Filtering for Fourier-Encoded Implicit Neural Representations
figure: First adaptive local frequency filtering method for Fourier-encoded INRs to enhance 3D shape representation.
abstract: This paper presents an adaptive local frequency filtering method for Fourier-encoded implicit neural representations, improving the modeling of continuous signals with spatially varying local spectra. The method enhances reconstruction quality and optimization speed in 2D image fitting and 3D shape representation tasks, providing insights into spatially varying frequency preferences.

title: Omni123: Exploring 3D Native Foundation Models with Limited 3D Data by Unifying Text to 2D and 3D Generation
figure: First 3D-native foundation model that unifies text-to-2D and text-to-3D generation via an autoregressive framework
abstract: Omni123 is a 3D-native foundation model that integrates text-to-2D and text-to-3D generation in a single autoregressive framework. It utilizes cross-modal consistency between images and 3D as a structural constraint, improving 3D representations by leveraging abundant 2D data. The model enhances text-guided 3D generation and editing, paving the way for multimodal 3D world models.

title: SDesc3D: Towards Layout-Aware 3D Indoor Scene Generation from Short Descriptions
figure: First framework for layout-aware 3D indoor scene generation from short descriptions via multi-view structural priors.
abstract: SDesc3D is a framework for generating 3D indoor scenes based on short textual descriptions, addressing challenges in physical plausibility and detail richness. It utilizes multi-view structural priors and regional functionality implications to enhance 3D reasoning and scene organization. The method includes an iterative scheme for refining structural plausibility and shows improved performance ove

title: ARGS: Auto-Regressive Gaussian Splatting via Parallel Progressive Next-Scale Prediction
figure: First auto-regressive framework for 3D object generation via Gaussian splatting.
abstract: This paper presents ARGS, an auto-regressive framework for 3D object generation that utilizes Gaussian splatting for next-scale predictions. It introduces a Gaussian simplification strategy and a tree-based transformer to enhance structural consistency, allowing for efficient multi-scale generation with controllable detail levels and visual fidelity.

title: Extend3D: Town-Scale 3D Generation
figure: First training-free pipeline for town-scale 3D scene generation from a single image via object-centric model.
abstract: Extend3D is a training-free pipeline for generating 3D scenes from a single image, utilizing an object-centric generative model. It extends the latent space for better representation of wide scenes and employs patch-wise generation with image conditioning. The method includes a novel approach to 3D completion and optimization for improved geometric structure and texture fidelity, outperforming pre

title: WorldFlow3D: Flowing Through 3D Distributions for Unbounded World Generation
figure: First method for unbounded 3D world generation via flow matching.
abstract: WorldFlow3D introduces a novel approach for generating unbounded 3D worlds by modeling the task as flowing through 3D data distributions. This method allows for controllability over geometric structure and visual texture, demonstrating high-quality generation across various scene types. The approach shows rapid convergence and generalizability, outperforming existing methods in scene fidelity.

title: Seen2Scene: Completing Realistic 3D Scenes with Visibility-Guided Flow
figure: First flow matching-based approach for scene completion and generation using real-world 3D scans.
abstract: Seen2Scene introduces a flow matching-based method for completing and generating realistic 3D scenes from incomplete real-world scans. It utilizes visibility-guided flow matching to learn from partial observations, representing scenes with truncated signed distance fields. The model adapts to various input types, including text and partial scans, achieving high accuracy in scene completion and gen

title: MeshTailor: Cutting Seams via Generative Mesh Traversal
figure: First mesh-native generative framework for synthesizing edge-aligned seams on 3D surfaces via autoregressive traversal.
abstract: MeshTailor introduces a novel framework for generating edge-aligned seams on 3D meshes, operating directly on the mesh graph to avoid projection artifacts. It employs a hierarchical seam graph and a dual-stream encoder to enhance seam layout coherence and structure, outperforming existing methods.

title: GaussianGPT: Towards Autoregressive 3D Gaussian Scene Generation
figure: First autoregressive model for 3D Gaussian scene generation via next-token prediction
abstract: GaussianGPT is a transformer-based model that generates 3D Gaussians for scene creation using an autoregressive approach. It compresses Gaussian primitives into a discrete latent grid and employs a causal transformer for sequential generation, allowing for flexible and context-aware 3D scene generation, including completion and outpainting.

title: TopoMesh: High-Fidelity Mesh Autoencoding via Topological Unification
figure: First high-fidelity mesh autoencoder that unifies topological structures via a sparse voxel-based VAE.
abstract: TopoMesh introduces a novel sparse voxel-based VAE that addresses the representation mismatch between ground-truth meshes and network predictions by unifying them under a shared topological framework. This approach enhances the preservation of sharp features and geometric details in 3D mesh generation, significantly outperforming existing VAEs in reconstruction fidelity.

title: SIMART: Decomposing Monolithic Meshes into Sim-ready Articulated Assets via MLLM
figure: First unified MLLM framework for part-level decomposition and kinematic prediction via Sparse 3D VQ-VAE
abstract: SIMART introduces a unified MLLM framework that enhances the generation of articulated 3D assets by performing part-level decomposition and kinematic prediction. It utilizes a Sparse 3D VQ-VAE to significantly reduce token counts, improving scalability and enabling high-fidelity multi-part assemblies for simulation-ready objects.

title: Know3D: Prompting 3D Generation with Knowledge from Vision-Language Models
figure: First framework that integrates multimodal language models for controllable 3D generation via diffusion.
abstract: Know3D introduces a framework that enhances 3D generation by incorporating knowledge from vision-language models, allowing for language-controllable generation of unseen regions in 3D assets. It utilizes a VLM-diffusion model to connect semantic understanding with geometric reconstruction, improving the fidelity and control of generated 3D shapes.

title: From Part to Whole: 3D Generative World Model with an Adaptive Structural Hierarchy
figure: First adaptive part-to-whole 3D generative model that learns a flexible structural hierarchy via latent space.
abstract: This paper presents a novel part-to-whole 3D generative world model that autonomously discovers latent structural slots from single images. It introduces an adaptive slot-gating mechanism for dynamic activation probabilities, ensuring compact and expressive structures across categories. The model enhances cross-category shape sharing and denoising through a learnable prototype bank, achieving impr

title: Text-Image Conditioned 3D Generation
figure: First 3D generation model that combines text and image conditioning for improved asset creation.
abstract: TIGON introduces a novel approach to 3D generation by combining text and image conditioning, addressing limitations of single-modality models. The method shows that joint reasoning over visual and textual inputs enhances the quality and flexibility of generated 3D assets, outperforming existing models. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of this cross-modal approach.

title: 2Xplat: Two Experts Are Better Than One Generalist
figure: First pose-free feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting framework using a two-expert design
abstract: 2Xplat introduces a novel pose-free feed-forward framework for 3D Gaussian Splatting that separates geometry estimation from Gaussian generation using two experts. This approach enhances the quality of 3D modeling from uncalibrated multi-view images, outperforming previous methods and suggesting benefits of modular design in 3D geometric tasks.

title: DreamPartGen: Semantically Grounded Part-Level 3D Generation via Collaborative Latent Denoising
figure: First semantically grounded part-level 3D generation via collaborative latent denoising.
abstract: DreamPartGen is a framework for part-aware text-to-3D generation that models geometry and appearance of parts while capturing inter-part dependencies. It uses Duplex Part Latents and Relational Semantic Latents to ensure geometric and semantic consistency, achieving state-of-the-art performance in geometric fidelity and text-shape alignment across benchmarks.

title: Points-to-3D: Structure-Aware 3D Generation with Point Cloud Priors
figure: First 3D generation framework leveraging point cloud priors via diffusion.
abstract: Points-to-3D introduces a diffusion-based framework that utilizes point cloud priors for geometry-controllable 3D asset and scene generation. It enhances existing methods by embedding explicit geometric constraints from point clouds, leading to improved rendering quality and geometric fidelity. The method can work with both accurate and estimated point clouds from images.

title: LoST: Level of Semantics Tokenization for 3D Shapes
figure: First tokenization method for 3D shapes that prioritizes semantic salience via autoregressive modeling.
abstract: LoST introduces a novel tokenization approach for 3D shapes that enhances autoregressive generation by ordering tokens based on semantic importance. This method improves the efficiency and quality of 3D shape generation, achieving state-of-the-art results in both geometric and semantic reconstruction metrics while enabling downstream tasks like semantic retrieval.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Sketch-Guided 3D Building Generation
figure: First multi-stage framework that generates architectural models from rough design sketches via generative and retrieval-based methods
abstract: This paper presents a multi-stage generative design framework for creating architectural models from rough sketches, addressing design drift and enabling component-level editing. It combines generative and retrieval methods to allow for personalized customization and modular design, particularly for Japanese detached houses. The framework supports targeted component replacement and is evaluated th

title: Interact3D: Compositional 3D Generation of Interactive Objects
figure: First framework for generating interactive 3D compositional objects from single images via SDF optimization
abstract: Interact3D introduces a novel framework for generating physically plausible interactive 3D compositional objects from single images. It utilizes advanced generative priors and a two-stage composition pipeline to ensure geometric fidelity and spatial relationships, addressing challenges like occlusions and collisions. The method incorporates a Vision-Language Model for iterative self-correction, re

title: IRIS: Intersection-aware Ray-based Implicit Editable Scenes
figure: First method for efficient interactive scene editing using ray-based implicit representations.
abstract: IRIS introduces a novel framework for efficient and interactive scene editing by employing an analytical sampling strategy that identifies interaction points between rays and scene primitives. This approach enhances rendering performance and enables high-fidelity, real-time rendering while allowing flexible shape editing.

title: SK-Adapter: Skeleton-Based Structural Control for Native 3D Generation
figure: First framework for precise skeletal manipulation in native 3D generation via structural control.
abstract: SK-Adapter introduces a framework for precise skeletal manipulation in native 3D generation, addressing the limitations of existing models. By treating 3D skeletons as control signals, it enhances structural control while maintaining generative quality. The method also supports local 3D editing, allowing for region-specific modifications. The paper includes the Objaverse-TMS dataset of text-mesh-s

title: EI-Part: Explode for Completion and Implode for Refinement
figure: First framework that generates high-quality 3D shapes with coherent components via Explode and Implode states
abstract: EI-Part is a novel framework for part-level 3D generation, addressing challenges in producing geometrically plausible components. It utilizes distinct representations for part completion and geometry refinement, incorporating a self-attention mechanism to maintain structural coherence. Extensive experiments show it achieves state-of-the-art performance in generating semantically meaningful parts w

title: Scene Generation at Absolute Scale: Utilizing Semantic and Geometric Guidance From Text for Accurate and Interpretable 3D Indoor Scene Generation
figure: First text-to-3D generation framework that maintains absolute scale and geometric consistency.
abstract: GuidedSceneGen is a text-to-3D framework that generates accurate and interpretable indoor scenes by maintaining a global coordinate frame. It uses a depth-conditioned diffusion model to synthesize 360° imagery aligned with a predicted global layout, improving spatial coherence. The method allows for progressive scene expansion and demonstrates superior 3D consistency compared to existing baselines

title: SceneAssistant: A Visual Feedback Agent for Open-Vocabulary 3D Scene Generation
figure: First open-vocabulary 3D scene generation method using visual feedback and VLMs.
abstract: SceneAssistant introduces a visual-feedback-driven agent for open-vocabulary 3D scene generation from natural language. It combines a 3D object generation model with Vision-Language Models to enable flexible scene composition and editing. The method allows for diverse and high-quality scene generation, outperforming existing approaches.

title: PolyCrysDiff: Controllable Generation of Three-Dimensional Computable Polycrystalline Material Structures
figure: First framework for controllable generation of 3D polycrystalline microstructures via conditional latent diffusion
abstract: PolyCrysDiff is a framework that enables the generation of computable 3D polycrystalline microstructures, crucial for understanding structure-property relationships. It uses conditional latent diffusion to reproduce target grain morphologies and spatial correlations, outperforming traditional methods. The generated structures are validated through simulations, aiding in the optimization and design

title: MV-SAM3D: Adaptive Multi-View Fusion for Layout-Aware 3D Generation
figure: First layout-aware 3D generation that incorporates multi-view consistency via a Multi-Diffusion process
abstract: MV-SAM3D enhances layout-aware 3D generation by integrating multi-view observations to improve spatial arrangement and physical plausibility of multiple objects. It employs adaptive weighting strategies for confidence-aware fusion and physics-aware optimization to ensure realistic object placements. The framework shows significant improvements in reconstruction fidelity without additional training

title: TopGen: Learning Structural Layouts and Cross-Fields for Quadrilateral Mesh Generation
figure: First framework that predicts structural layouts and cross-fields for quadrilateral mesh generation via learning-based methods
abstract: TopGen is a learning-based framework for quadrilateral mesh generation that predicts structural layouts and cross-fields simultaneously. It processes triangular meshes through point cloud sampling and a shape encoder, ensuring robustness to non-manifold geometries. The dual-query decoder classifies structural lines and regresses cross-fields in parallel, preserving geometric integrity and enabling

title: Learning Convex Decomposition via Feature Fields
figure: First feed-forward model for open-world convex decomposition via feature fields
abstract: This paper introduces a novel approach to convex decomposition using learned feature fields, resulting in a feed-forward model that produces high-quality decompositions of 3D shapes into convex bodies. The method enhances collision detection and generalizes across various representations, including meshes and CAD models. It also enables scalable self-supervised learning on large datasets.

title: ForgeDreamer: Industrial Text-to-3D Generation with Multi-Expert LoRA and Cross-View Hypergraph
figure: First text-to-3D generation framework for industrial applications via Multi-Expert LoRA and Cross-View Hypergraph.
abstract: ForgeDreamer addresses limitations in text-to-3D generation for industrial applications by introducing a Multi-Expert LoRA Ensemble for better cross-category generalization and a Cross-View Hypergraph Geometric Enhancement for improved geometric reasoning. This framework enhances semantic understanding and ensures manufacturing-level consistency, outperforming existing methods on a custom dataset.

title: PARSE: Part-Aware Relational Spatial Modeling
figure: First framework that models part-level interactions for spatially grounded scene generation via geometric constraints
abstract: PARSE introduces a framework for modeling inter-object part relations to create physically valid 3D scenes. It utilizes a Part-centric Assembly Graph to encode geometric relations and a solver for collision-free configurations. The framework is validated with a dataset of 10,000 3D indoor scenes, enhancing object-level layout reasoning and part-level understanding, leading to improved physical rea

title: CanoVerse: 3D Object Scalable Canonicalization and Dataset for Generation and Pose
figure: First large-scale canonical 3D dataset that improves generation stability via a new canonicalization framework
abstract: CanoVerse addresses the challenge of arbitrary global rotation in 3D objects by providing a massive dataset of 320K objects across 1,156 categories. This dataset enhances 3D generation stability and enables precise cross-modal shape retrieval and orientation estimation. The new canonicalization framework significantly reduces alignment time, transforming the process into a high-throughput data gen

title: LATO: 3D Mesh Flow Matching with Structured TOpology Preserving LAtents
figure: First flow matching-based synthesis of explicit 3D meshes with topology-preserving latents.
abstract: LATO introduces a novel latent representation for scalable synthesis of explicit 3D meshes using flow matching. It employs a Vertex Displacement Field and a sparse voxel VAE to create a structured, topology-aware latent space. The method generates complex geometry and well-formed topology efficiently, surpassing traditional diffusion models and autoregressive approaches.

title: FTSplat: Feed-forward Triangle Splatting Network
figure: First feed-forward framework for triangle primitive generation from multi-view images.
abstract: FTSplat introduces a feed-forward framework for generating triangle surfaces directly from calibrated multi-view images, enabling efficient 3D reconstruction without per-scene optimization. The method enhances geometric learning stability through a pixel-aligned triangle generation module and relative 3D point cloud supervision, producing simulation-ready models in a single pass.

title: Pano3DComposer: Feed-Forward Compositional 3D Scene Generation from Single Panoramic Image
figure: First efficient feed-forward framework for compositional 3D scene generation from single panoramic images.
abstract: Pano3DComposer is a novel framework that generates 3D scenes from panoramic images efficiently, overcoming limitations of existing methods. It decouples object generation from layout estimation and employs a transformation predictor for accurate object placement. The method achieves high geometric accuracy and rapid scene generation, making it suitable for various datasets.

title: Cog2Gen3D: Sculpturing 3D Semantic-Geometric Cognition for 3D Generation
figure: First 3D cognition-guided diffusion framework for controllable 3D generation.
abstract: Cog2Gen3D introduces a 3D cognition-guided diffusion framework that enhances 3D generation by integrating semantic and geometric representations. It utilizes a 3D cognition graph to ensure physical plausibility and structural rationality, significantly improving semantic fidelity and geometric accuracy compared to existing methods.

title: RelaxFlow: Text-Driven Amodal 3D Generation
figure: First text-driven amodal 3D generation method that preserves input observation via RelaxFlow.
abstract: RelaxFlow introduces a novel approach to text-driven amodal 3D generation, addressing the challenges of semantic ambiguity under occlusion. The method employs a dual-branch framework to manage control granularity, allowing for the generation of unseen regions while maintaining visual fidelity. The paper also presents new benchmarks for evaluation.

title: Preference Score Distillation: Leveraging 2D Rewards to Align Text-to-3D Generation with Human Preference
figure: First method to align text-to-3D generation with human preference via score distillation.
abstract: This paper introduces Preference Score Distillation (PSD), a framework that utilizes pretrained 2D reward models to enhance text-to-3D generation without requiring 3D training data. It addresses challenges in human preference alignment for diffusion models and proposes a novel classifier-free guidance mechanism to improve performance. Experiments show PSD's effectiveness in aesthetic metrics and i

title: TIMI: Training-Free Image-to-3D Multi-Instance Generation with Spatial Fidelity
figure: First training-free framework for Image-to-3D multi-instance generation with high spatial fidelity via ISG and SGU modules
abstract: TIMI introduces a training-free approach for Image-to-3D multi-instance generation, focusing on spatial fidelity. It utilizes an Instance-aware Separation Guidance module for instance disentanglement and a Spatial-stabilized Geometry-adaptive Update module to preserve geometric characteristics. The method outperforms existing techniques in layout and instance distinctiveness without additional tra

title: ArtLLM: Generating Articulated Assets via 3D LLM
figure: First framework that generates articulated assets directly from complete 3D meshes via a 3D multimodal large language model.
abstract: ArtLLM is a novel framework for generating articulated 3D objects from complete meshes, addressing limitations of existing methods. It uses a 3D multimodal large language model to autoregressively predict parts and joints, inferring kinematic structures from point clouds. The framework shows significant improvements in part layout accuracy and joint prediction, with applications in digital twin co

title: Mesh-Pro: Asynchronous Advantage-guided Ranking Preference Optimization for Artist-style Quadrilateral Mesh Generation
figure: First asynchronous online RL framework for 3D mesh generation via Advantage-guided Ranking Preference Optimization.
abstract: This paper presents Mesh-Pro, an innovative framework for 3D mesh generation that enhances training efficiency and generation quality using reinforcement learning. It introduces a novel algorithm, ARPO, and a unique tokenization method for mesh representation, achieving state-of-the-art results in artistic and dense mesh generation.

title: BuildAnyPoint: 3D Building Structured Abstraction from Diverse Point Clouds
figure: First framework for structured 3D building reconstruction from diverse point clouds via autoregressive mesh generation.
abstract: BuildAnyPoint is a generative framework that reconstructs structured 3D buildings from diverse point clouds using autoregressive mesh generation. It employs a Loosely Cascaded Diffusion Transformer to recover distributions from noisy points and generates compact meshes, showing significant improvements over previous methods in building abstraction.

title: HorizonForge: Driving Scene Editing with Any Trajectories and Any Vehicles
figure: First framework for controllable driving scene generation via Gaussian Splats and Meshes
abstract: HorizonForge is a framework for generating and editing driving scenes with high fidelity and control. It uses Gaussian Splats and Meshes for 3D manipulation and employs a noise-aware video diffusion process for coherent scene variations. The method shows significant improvements in user preference and fidelity compared to existing approaches. A benchmark, HorizonSuite, is also introduced for evalu

title: BoxSplitGen: A Generative Model for 3D Part Bounding Boxes in Varying Granularity
figure: First model that generates 3D part bounding boxes with varying granularity via iterative splitting.
abstract: BoxSplitGen introduces a framework for intuitive 3D shape generation by refining bounding boxes. It includes two generative models: one for generating bounding boxes through iterative splitting and another for converting these boxes into shapes using 3D shape priors from a diffusion model. Experiments show improved performance over existing methods.

title: L3DR: 3D-aware LiDAR Diffusion and Rectification
