figure: First 3D-aware LiDAR diffusion framework that rectifies RV artifacts via a 3D residual regression network
abstract: L3DR is a framework designed to improve 3D geometry realism in LiDAR diffusion by addressing RV artifacts. It employs a 3D residual regression network to predict point-level offsets, enhancing boundary sharpness and local geometry accuracy. The method shows superior performance across various benchmarks, demonstrating its effectiveness in generating realistic 3D models.

title: SceneTok: A Compressed, Diffusable Token Space for 3D Scenes
figure: First method to encode 3D scene information into compressed, diffusable tokens via a multi-view tokenizer
abstract: SceneTok introduces a novel tokenizer that encodes 3D scene information into a compressed set of permutation-invariant tokens, enabling efficient scene generation and novel view rendering. This approach outperforms existing methods in compression and reconstruction quality, achieving significant improvements in quality-speed trade-off.

title: Spatial-Temporal State Propagation Autoregressive Model for 4D Object Generation
figure: First model that generates spatial-temporal consistent 4D objects via autoregressive state propagation.
abstract: The paper presents 4DSTAR, a model that generates high-quality 4D objects while maintaining spatial-temporal consistency. It introduces a dynamic spatial-temporal state propagation mechanism to guide generation across timesteps and employs a 4D VQ-VAE to encode and decode the 4D structure into dynamic 3D Gaussians. Experimental results show its competitive performance against existing diffusion mo

title: PartRAG: Retrieval-Augmented Part-Level 3D Generation and Editing
figure: First retrieval-augmented framework for part-level 3D generation and editing via diffusion.
abstract: PartRAG addresses challenges in single-image 3D generation with part-level structure by integrating a part database with a diffusion transformer. It enables precise edits and maintains multi-view consistency through a Hierarchical Contrastive Retrieval module and a masked part-level editor. The method shows competitive results on various benchmarks and allows for interactive edits.

title: Cryo-SWAN: the Multi-Scale Wavelet-decomposition-inspired Autoencoder Network for molecular density representation of molecular volumes
figure: First voxel-based VAE for molecular density representation via multi-scale wavelet decomposition
abstract: Cryo-SWAN is a voxel-based variational autoencoder designed for robust representation of 3D shapes from molecular density data. It utilizes multi-scale wavelet decomposition for conditional encoding and improves reconstruction quality on various datasets. The model captures both global geometry and fine details, and integrates with diffusion models for denoising and shape generation.

title: DAV-GSWT: Diffusion-Active-View Sampling for Data-Efficient Gaussian Splatting Wang Tiles
figure: First data-efficient framework for synthesizing Gaussian Splatting Wang Tiles via diffusion and active view sampling.
abstract: DAV-GSWT introduces a framework that utilizes diffusion priors and active view sampling to create high-fidelity Gaussian Splatting Wang Tiles from minimal input. It incorporates a hierarchical uncertainty quantification mechanism to optimize viewpoint selection and enhance structural detail, significantly reducing data requirements while preserving visual quality for large-scale environments.

title: VAR-3D: View-aware Auto-Regressive Model for Text-to-3D Generation via a 3D Tokenizer
figure: First text-to-3D generation model that integrates view-aware VQ-VAE for improved geometric coherence via auto-regressive modeling.
abstract: VAR-3D addresses challenges in text-to-3D generation by using a view-aware VQ-VAE to convert complex 3D structures into discrete tokens. It introduces a rendering-supervised training strategy to enhance visual fidelity and structural consistency, significantly improving generation quality and text alignment compared to existing methods.

title: Meltdown: Circuits and Bifurcations in Point-Cloud-Conditioned 3D Diffusion Transformers
figure: First analysis of failure modes in point-cloud-conditioned 3D diffusion transformers via mechanistic study
abstract: This paper investigates the failure modes of point-cloud-conditioned 3D diffusion transformers, particularly a phenomenon termed 'Meltdown' where small perturbations in input can lead to significant reconstruction failures. The authors propose a control method, PowerRemap, to mitigate these issues, achieving high rescue rates in reconstruction quality.

title: Bioinspired123D: Generative 3D Modeling System for Bioinspired Structures
figure: First text-to-3D generation system using code-as-geometry for bioinspired structures
abstract: Bioinspired123D is a generative 3D modeling system that translates natural language design cues into Blender Python scripts for creating bioinspired geometries. It utilizes a lightweight pipeline and a domain-specific dataset to improve controllability and efficiency in 3D structure generation, achieving significant performance gains over existing models.

title: SceneSmith: Agentic Generation of Simulation-Ready Indoor Scenes
figure: First framework that generates simulation-ready indoor environments from natural language prompts via hierarchical agentic generation.
abstract: SceneSmith is a framework designed to generate diverse and complex indoor environments for robotic training. It utilizes a hierarchical approach to create scenes from architectural layouts to object placements, integrating text-to-3D synthesis for static objects. The method significantly increases object density and stability in simulations, achieving high realism and prompt fidelity in user studi

title: ShapeUP: Scalable Image-Conditioned 3D Editing
figure: First scalable image-conditioned 3D editing framework via supervised latent-to-latent translation
abstract: ShapeUP is a scalable framework for image-conditioned 3D editing that formulates the process as supervised latent-to-latent translation. It builds on a pretrained 3D foundation model and utilizes a 3D Diffusion Transformer to achieve fine-grained visual control and structural consistency in edits. Evaluations show it outperforms existing baselines in identity preservation and edit fidelity.

title: HY3D-Bench: Generation of 3D Assets
figure: First unified ecosystem for 3D asset generation with structured part-level decomposition.
abstract: HY3D-Bench is an open-source ecosystem aimed at enhancing 3D content creation by providing a library of 250k high-fidelity 3D objects and a scalable synthesis pipeline. It focuses on structured part-level decomposition for fine-grained perception and controllable editing, addressing data processing bottlenecks in the field.

title: PnP-U3D: Plug-and-Play 3D Framework Bridging Autoregression and Diffusion for Unified Understanding and Generation
figure: First unified framework for 3D understanding and generation combining autoregression with diffusion.
abstract: This paper presents a novel framework that integrates autoregressive and diffusion models for 3D understanding and generation. It enables effective information exchange between these two paradigms while minimizing training costs. The framework achieves state-of-the-art performance in various 3D tasks, including generation and editing, showcasing its potential for general-purpose 3D intelligence.

title: SceneLinker: Compositional 3D Scene Generation via Semantic Scene Graph from RGB Sequences
figure: First framework that generates compositional 3D scenes via semantic scene graph from RGB sequences.
abstract: SceneLinker is a framework that generates 3D scenes using semantic scene graphs derived from RGB sequences. It addresses challenges in capturing contextual relationships between objects and synthesizing diverse shapes by employing a graph-variational autoencoder. The method shows improved performance in generating consistent 3D spaces aligned with real-world layouts, facilitating Mixed Reality con

title: Learning to Build Shapes by Extrusion
figure: First method to generate 3D meshes via text-based extrusion sequences using a large language model.
abstract: This paper presents Text Encoded Extrusions (TEE), a novel method for generating 3D meshes by expressing mesh construction as sequences of face extrusions. The approach allows for arbitrary output face counts and supports editing existing meshes. The model is trained on a library of quadrilateral meshes, enabling reconstruction and novel shape synthesis.

title: CG-MLLM: Captioning and Generating 3D content via Multi-modal Large Language Models
figure: First MLLM that generates high-resolution 3D content via a decoupled architecture
abstract: CG-MLLM is a novel Multi-modal Large Language Model that enables 3D captioning and high-resolution 3D generation. It utilizes a Mixture-of-Transformer architecture to handle token-level and block-level content separately, integrating a vision-language backbone with a 3D VAE latent space. The model significantly improves the generation of high-fidelity 3D objects, enhancing both creation and percep

title: HiFi-Mesh: High-Fidelity Efficient 3D Mesh Generation via Compact Autoregressive Dependence
figure: First high-fidelity 3D mesh generator using compact autoregressive dependencies via LANE
abstract: HiFi-Mesh introduces the Latent Autoregressive Network (LANE) for high-fidelity 3D mesh generation, improving sequence length and inference speed through compact autoregressive dependencies and Adaptive Computation Graph Reconfiguration. It outperforms existing methods in generation speed, structural detail, and geometric consistency.

title: Quartet of Diffusions: Structure-Aware Point Cloud Generation through Part and Symmetry Guidance
figure: First 3D point cloud generation framework that integrates and enforces symmetry and part priors via coordinated diffusion models
abstract: The Quartet of Diffusions is a framework for generating structure-aware point clouds by modeling part composition and symmetry. It utilizes four coordinated diffusion models to learn distributions of global shape latents, symmetries, and semantic parts, ensuring high-quality outputs with coherent part placement. This method allows for fine-grained control over shape attributes while maintaining gl

title: GeoDiff3D: Self-Supervised 3D Scene Generation with Geometry-Constrained 2D Diffusion Guidance
figure: First self-supervised 3D scene generation using geometry-constrained 2D diffusion guidance.
abstract: GeoDiff3D is a self-supervised framework for 3D scene generation that leverages coarse geometry and a geometry-constrained 2D diffusion model. It addresses limitations in existing methods by reducing reliance on labeled data and improving structural coherence and detail in generated scenes. The approach allows for efficient and high-quality 3D scene construction.

title: RoamScene3D: Immersive Text-to-3D Scene Generation via Adaptive Object-aware Roaming
figure: First immersive text-to-3D scene generation that adapts object-aware roaming via semantic reasoning.
abstract: RoamScene3D generates immersive 3D scenes from text by addressing limitations of existing methods that struggle with spatial relationships and occluded content. It employs a vision-language model to create a scene graph for semantic guidance and introduces a Motion-Injected Inpainting model for adaptive camera motion. The method significantly enhances the consistency and photorealism of generated

title: ActionMesh: Animated 3D Mesh Generation with Temporal 3D Diffusion
figure: First animated 3D mesh generator using temporal 3D diffusion.
abstract: ActionMesh is a generative model that produces animated 3D meshes quickly and with high quality. It modifies existing 3D diffusion models to incorporate a temporal axis, allowing for the generation of synchronized sequences of 3D shapes. The model can take various inputs, including videos and text descriptions, and achieves state-of-the-art performance in geometric accuracy and temporal consistenc

title: FlowSSC: Universal Generative Monocular Semantic Scene Completion via One-Step Latent Diffusion
figure: First generative framework for monocular semantic scene completion via latent diffusion.
abstract: FlowSSC addresses the challenge of Semantic Scene Completion from monocular RGB images by treating it as a conditional generation problem. It integrates with existing methods to enhance performance and introduces a Shortcut Flow-matching mechanism for real-time inference in a compact triplane latent space, achieving high-fidelity generation in a single step.

title: ScenDi: 3D-to-2D Scene Diffusion Cascades for Urban Generation
figure: First method that integrates 3D and 2D diffusion models for urban scene generation.
abstract: ScenDi proposes a novel approach for generating realistic 3D urban scenes by combining 3D latent diffusion models with 2D video diffusion models. This method allows for controllable synthesis using various input conditions, enhancing appearance details while maintaining camera controllability. Experiments show its effectiveness on real-world datasets.

title: Motion 3-to-4: 3D Motion Reconstruction for 4D Synthesis
figure: First framework that synthesizes 4D dynamic objects from monocular video via static 3D shape generation.
abstract: Motion 3-to-4 is a feed-forward framework that synthesizes high-quality 4D dynamic objects from a single monocular video and an optional 3D reference mesh. It decomposes 4D synthesis into static 3D shape generation and motion reconstruction, achieving superior fidelity and spatial consistency compared to prior work. A project page is available.

title: Class-Partitioned VQ-VAE and Latent Flow Matching for Point Cloud Scene Generation
figure: First class-partitioned VQ-VAE that generates point cloud scenes via latent flow matching.
abstract: The paper presents a Class-Partitioned VQ-VAE that effectively decodes object latent features for point cloud scene generation. It introduces a class-partitioned codebook to address codebook collapse and employs a Latent-space Flow Matching Model for generating object features and class labels. The method achieves significant reductions in reconstruction errors for complex scenes without relying o

title: Proc3D: Procedural 3D Generation and Parametric Editing of 3D Shapes with Large Language Models
figure: First system for editable 3D model generation and parametric editing via LLMs
abstract: Proc3D introduces a system for generating editable 3D models using procedural compact graphs, allowing real-time modifications through natural language prompts. It combines generative approaches with LLMs to enhance editing efficiency and alignment with text prompts, outperforming traditional methods significantly.

title: ShapeR: Robust Conditional 3D Shape Generation from Casual Captures
figure: First robust conditional 3D shape generation from casually captured sequences via a rectified flow transformer
abstract: ShapeR introduces a novel method for generating 3D object shapes from casually captured image sequences, addressing the limitations of existing methods that require clean inputs. By utilizing visual-inertial SLAM and vision-language models, it generates high-fidelity metric 3D shapes while ensuring robustness against real-world challenges. The approach significantly outperforms existing methods in

title: ATATA: One Algorithm to Align Them All
figure: First multi-modal algorithm for joint inference of aligned 3D shape generation via Rectified Flow models
abstract: The paper presents a new algorithm for joint inference of paired structurally aligned samples using Rectified Flow models. It addresses limitations of existing methods by improving computation speed and visual quality in 3D shape generation. The approach shows high structural alignment and is applicable to various domains, outperforming state-of-the-art methods in image, video, and 3D generation.

title: Gen3R: 3D Scene Generation Meets Feed-Forward Reconstruction
figure: First method that combines video diffusion models with reconstruction for 3D scene generation via aligned latents
abstract: Gen3R integrates foundational reconstruction models with video diffusion models for scene-level 3D generation. It generates RGB videos and 3D geometry, including camera poses and depth maps, by aligning geometric and appearance latents. The method shows state-of-the-art performance in conditioned 3D scene generation and enhances reconstruction robustness through generative priors.

title: Muses: Designing, Composing, Generating Nonexistent Fantasy 3D Creatures without Training
figure: First training-free method for fantastic 3D creature generation via feed-forward.
abstract: Muses introduces a novel approach for generating 3D creatures without training, utilizing a 3D skeleton for coherent design and composition. It combines graph-constrained reasoning with voxel-based assembly and image-guided appearance modeling to create visually appealing and contextually relevant 3D assets. The method shows state-of-the-art performance in visual fidelity and offers potential for

title: Memorization in 3D Shape Generation: An Empirical Study
figure: First empirical study quantifying memorization in 3D generative models via a new evaluation framework.
abstract: This paper investigates the memorization aspect of 3D generative models, proposing a framework to quantify it. The study reveals how data modality and diversity influence memorization, and suggests strategies to mitigate it while maintaining generation quality. The findings are based on experiments with a Vecset diffusion model, providing insights into improving generative model performance.

title: A Three-Level Alignment Framework for Large-Scale 3D Retrieval and Controlled 4D Generation
figure: First framework for large-scale 3D retrieval and controlled 4D generation via structured alignment.
abstract: Uni4D is a unified framework that enhances large-scale open vocabulary 3D retrieval and controlled 4D generation through a three-level alignment of text, 3D models, and images. It utilizes a multi-head attention model for improved semantic alignment and demonstrates high-quality results in both 3D retrieval and 4D generation.

title: UltraShape 1.0: High-Fidelity 3D Shape Generation via Scalable Geometric Refinement
figure: First scalable 3D diffusion framework for high-fidelity geometry generation via two-stage refinement
abstract: UltraShape 1.0 introduces a scalable framework for high-fidelity 3D geometry generation using a two-stage diffusion process. It synthesizes a coarse structure and refines it for detailed geometry, supported by a comprehensive data processing pipeline that enhances the quality of 3D datasets. The model focuses on local geometric details and is trained on publicly available datasets, showing competi

title: LiDARDraft: Generating LiDAR Point Cloud from Versatile Inputs
figure: First method to generate LiDAR point clouds from versatile inputs via 3D layout representation.
abstract: LiDARDraft generates realistic and diverse LiDAR point clouds for autonomous driving simulation by bridging user inputs like text and images with 3D layouts. It employs a rangemap-based ControlNet for pixel-level alignment, enabling controllable generation of LiDAR point clouds and allowing environments to be created from various descriptions and sketches.

title: Symmetry Matters: Auditing and Symmetrizing 3D Generative Models
figure: First audit and improve symmetry in 3D point cloud generation via a data-centric intervention
abstract: This paper investigates symmetry preservation in 3D generative models, revealing a gap in symmetry encoding. It proposes a symmetry-aware training intervention using half-objects to enhance geometric consistency and visual plausibility in generated shapes. The findings advocate for incorporating symmetry in the evaluation and training of 3D generative models.

title: Hierarchical Neural Surfaces for 3D Mesh Compression
figure: First method to build compact INRs for generating 3D meshes via hierarchical structure
abstract: This paper introduces a method for creating compact implicit neural representations (INRs) of zero-genus 3D manifolds, enabling the generation of triangle meshes. The approach involves mapping a mesh's surface to a unit sphere and encoding a displacement vector field, allowing for real-time decoding of 3D meshes with a balance between quality and compression size.

title: Native and Compact Structured Latents for 3D Generation
figure: First structured latent representation for 3D generation via O-Voxel and Sparse Compression VAE
abstract: This paper introduces a new approach for 3D generative modeling using a structured latent representation called O-Voxel, which effectively captures complex topologies and detailed appearances. The Sparse Compression VAE is designed for efficient 3D generation, achieving high-quality geometry and material attributes while maintaining efficient inference.

title: Repurposing 2D Diffusion Models for 3D Shape Completion
figure: First to adapt 2D diffusion models for 3D shape completion via Shape Atlas.
abstract: This paper introduces a framework that repurposes 2D diffusion models for completing 3D shapes from incomplete point clouds. By utilizing a compact 2D representation of 3D geometry, the method effectively bridges the gap between 2D and 3D modalities, enabling high-quality shape completions even with limited 3D data. The approach is validated on established datasets and demonstrates practical appli

title: Feedforward 3D Editing via Text-Steerable Image-to-3D
figure: First method to add text steerability to image-to-3D generation via feedforward approach
abstract: Steer3D introduces a feedforward method that integrates text steerability into image-to-3D models, allowing for easy editing of generated 3D assets. Inspired by ControlNet, it adapts image-to-3D generation for direct text steering, achieving faster and more consistent results compared to existing methods. The approach leverages a scalable data engine and a two-stage training recipe.

title: CADKnitter: Compositional CAD Generation from Text and Geometry Guidance
figure: First compositional CAD generation framework that uses geometry-guided diffusion sampling.
abstract: CADKnitter is a framework for generating compositional CAD models from text and geometry guidance. It addresses the limitations of single-part CAD generation by producing complementary parts that adhere to geometric and semantic constraints. The method is validated with a dataset of over 310,000 CAD models and shows significant improvements over existing approaches.

title: SceneMaker: Open-set 3D Scene Generation with Decoupled De-occlusion and Pose Estimation Model
figure: First decoupled 3D scene generation framework that enhances de-occlusion and pose estimation.
abstract: SceneMaker is a decoupled framework for 3D scene generation that addresses challenges in producing high-quality geometry and accurate poses under occlusion. It separates the de-occlusion model from object generation and improves it using diverse datasets. The framework also includes a unified pose estimation model. Experiments show its effectiveness on indoor and open-set scenes. Code and datasets

title: Are We Ready for RL in Text-to-3D Generation? A Progressive Investigation
figure: First RL-enhanced text-to-3D model for autoregressive generation
abstract: This paper explores the application of reinforcement learning (RL) to text-to-3D generation, addressing challenges in reward design and algorithm effectiveness. It introduces AR3D-R1, the first RL-enhanced model that progresses from coarse shape generation to texture refinement, and proposes new benchmarks for evaluating 3D generation models.

title: SWiT-4D: Sliding-Window Transformer for Lossless and Parameter-Free Temporal 4D Generation
figure: First parameter-free temporal 4D mesh generation from monocular videos via a Sliding-Window Transformer.
abstract: SWiT-4D introduces a Sliding-Window Transformer for generating high-quality animated 3D assets from monocular videos, enhancing image-to-3D models with temporal modeling. It achieves strong data efficiency and high-fidelity geometry with minimal supervision, outperforming existing methods in temporal smoothness across various benchmarks.

title: UniPart: Part-Level 3D Generation with Unified 3D Geom-Seg Latents
figure: First part-level 3D generation method using a unified geometry-segmentation latent representation via latent diffusion.
abstract: UniPart introduces a two-stage latent diffusion framework for part-level 3D generation, enhancing geometric fidelity and segmentation controllability. It utilizes a unified geometry-segmentation latent representation to jointly encode object geometry and part structure, outperforming existing methods in quality and control.

title: WonderZoom: Multi-Scale 3D World Generation
figure: First method to generate multi-scale 3D scenes from a single image via scale-adaptive Gaussian surfels.
abstract: WonderZoom introduces a novel method for generating 3D scenes with varying spatial scales from a single image. It overcomes limitations of existing models by using scale-adaptive Gaussian surfels and a progressive detail synthesizer, allowing for coherent scene content across different granularities. The approach significantly improves quality and alignment in multi-scale 3D world creation.

title: Photo3D: Advancing Photorealistic 3D Generation through Structure-Aligned Detail Enhancement
figure: First framework that enhances photorealistic 3D generation via structure-aligned detail enhancement.
abstract: Photo3D is a framework aimed at improving photorealistic 3D generation by addressing the challenges of realistic appearances in generated geometry. It utilizes a structure-aligned multi-view synthesis pipeline and a detail-enhanced dataset to ensure consistency between 3D structures and their textures. The method shows strong generalization across various 3D-native generation paradigms, achieving

title: OCCDiff: Occupancy Diffusion Model for High-Fidelity 3D Building Reconstruction from Noisy Point Clouds
figure: First occupancy diffusion model for high-fidelity 3D building reconstruction from noisy point clouds
abstract: OCCDiff addresses the challenge of reconstructing buildings from LiDAR point clouds by using a latent diffusion process in the occupancy function space. It combines a function autoencoder with a point encoder to generate continuous occupancy functions, enhancing model performance through multi-task training. The method produces high-fidelity samples that are robust to noise.

title: MoCA: Mixture-of-Components Attention for Scalable Compositional 3D Generation
figure: First compositional 3D generative model with scalable attention via component routing and compression
abstract: MoCA addresses the scalability issues in part-aware 3D generation by introducing a compositional model that utilizes importance-based component routing and compression of unimportant components. This allows for efficient creation of 3D assets with a large number of components, outperforming existing methods in both object and scene generation tasks.

title: MeshRipple: Structured Autoregressive Generation of Artist-Meshes
figure: First autoregressive mesh generator that maintains long-range geometric dependencies via frontier-aware BFS tokenization.
abstract: MeshRipple introduces a novel approach to autoregressive mesh generation by expanding meshes outward from an active generation frontier. It employs a frontier-aware BFS tokenization, an expansive prediction strategy, and a sparse-attention global memory to ensure high surface fidelity and topological completeness, outperforming existing methods.

title: SpaceControl: Introducing Test-Time Spatial Control to 3D Generative Modeling
figure: First method for test-time spatial control of 3D asset generation via geometric inputs.
abstract: SpaceControl introduces a training-free method for explicit spatial control in 3D asset generation, allowing users to manipulate geometry with various inputs. It integrates with existing generative models and balances geometric fidelity with visual quality. The approach is validated through quantitative evaluations and user studies, and includes an interactive interface for real-time editing and g

title: Order Matters: 3D Shape Generation from Sequential VR Sketches
figure: First framework for generating 3D shapes from sequential VR sketches via a diffusion-based generator
abstract: VRSketch2Shape introduces a novel framework for generating 3D shapes from sequential VR sketches, addressing the temporal ordering of strokes to enhance structure and design intent. It includes an automated pipeline for sketch generation, a dataset of sketch-shape pairs, and an order-aware sketch encoder. The method shows improved geometric fidelity and generalization from synthetic to real sketch

title: SPLICE: Part-Level 3D Shape Editing from Local Semantic Extraction to Global Neural Mixing
figure: First part-level neural implicit representation for intuitive 3D shape editing via Gaussian ellipsoids
abstract: SPLICE introduces a part-level neural implicit representation for 3D shape editing, allowing intuitive and high-fidelity modifications. It encodes shape parts independently and uses a global attention-based decoder to integrate them, enabling various editing operations while maintaining semantic consistency. Experiments show SPLICE outperforms existing methods in shape-editing tasks.

title: GaussianBlender: Instant Stylization of 3D Gaussians with Disentangled Latent Spaces
figure: First text-driven 3D stylization method using structured, disentangled latent spaces via a feed-forward framework
abstract: GaussianBlender introduces a novel feed-forward framework for text-driven 3D stylization, enabling instant edits with high fidelity and multi-view consistency. It learns structured latent spaces from 3D Gaussians, allowing for efficient geometry and appearance manipulation, thus facilitating scalable 3D asset production without the need for time-intensive optimizations.

title: GAOT: Generating Articulated Objects Through Text-Guided Diffusion Models
figure: First articulated object generator that uses text-guided diffusion models.
abstract: GAOT introduces a three-phase framework for generating articulated objects from text prompts using diffusion models and hypergraph learning. It fine-tunes a point cloud generation model for coarse object representation, refines it with hypergraph learning, and generates joints using a diffusion model. The method shows superior performance on the PartNet-Mobility dataset.

title: KeyPointDiffuser: Unsupervised 3D Keypoint Learning via Latent Diffusion Models
figure: First unsupervised framework for learning spatially structured 3D keypoints via latent diffusion models.
abstract: This paper presents an unsupervised framework for learning spatially structured 3D keypoints from point cloud data, which enhances 3D generative pipelines. The keypoints serve as a compact representation that conditions a diffusion model to reconstruct full shapes, achieving improved consistency across object instances.

title: TabletopGen: Instance-Level Interactive 3D Tabletop Scene Generation from Text or Single Image
figure: First framework for instance-level interactive 3D tabletop scene generation from text or single image.
abstract: TabletopGen is a training-free framework that generates diverse, instance-level interactive 3D tabletop scenes from a reference image. It reconstructs each instance into a 3D model and aligns them for assembly into a collision-free scene. The method excels in visual fidelity and spatial accuracy, significantly improving upon existing techniques.

title: CC-FMO: Camera-Conditioned Zero-Shot Single Image to 3D Scene Generation with Foundation Model Orchestration
figure: First zero-shot, camera-conditioned pipeline for single-image to 3D scene generation.
abstract: CC-FMO presents a novel approach for generating high-quality 3D scenes from a single image, addressing challenges in coherence and pose estimation. By combining semantics-aware and structured latent representations, it achieves high-fidelity object geometries and scene-level coherence, outperforming existing methods.

title: PhysGen: Physically Grounded 3D Shape Generation for Industrial Design
figure: First physics-based 3D shape generation pipeline for industrial design via flow matching.
abstract: PhysGen introduces a unified physics-based 3D shape generation pipeline aimed at enhancing realism in industrial design. It employs a flow matching model with physical guidance, iteratively refining shapes to align with desired physical properties. The method integrates a shape-and-physics variational autoencoder to encode both aspects into a unified latent space, improving shape realism beyond vi

title: SplatFont3D: Structure-Aware Text-to-3D Artistic Font Generation with Part-Level Style Control
figure: First structure-aware text-to-3D artistic font generation framework with part-level style control via Gaussian splatting
abstract: SplatFont3D is a novel framework for generating 3D artistic fonts from text prompts, addressing the challenges of 3D artistic font generation. It utilizes a Glyph2Cloud module for enhancing 2D glyphs into 3D point clouds and employs a pretrained 2D diffusion model for optimization. The method allows for precise part-level style control and demonstrates improved style-text consistency and rendering

title: GeoWorld: Unlocking the Potential of Geometry Models to Facilitate High-Fidelity 3D Scene Generation
figure: First method that generates high-fidelity 3D scenes from a single image using geometry models.
abstract: GeoWorld enhances image-to-3D scene generation by generating consecutive video frames and utilizing geometry models for richer geometric features. It introduces a geometry alignment loss and adaptation module to improve geometric consistency, resulting in high-fidelity 3D scenes from single images and camera trajectories, outperforming previous methods.

title: Fast3Dcache: Training-free 3D Geometry Synthesis Acceleration
figure: First training-free caching framework that accelerates 3D diffusion inference while preserving geometric fidelity.
abstract: Fast3Dcache introduces a geometry-aware caching framework to enhance the efficiency of 3D diffusion models. It addresses the computational challenges of 3D shape generation by implementing a Predictive Caching Scheduler Constraint and a Spatiotemporal Stability Criterion, achieving significant speed-ups and reduced computational costs while maintaining geometric quality.

title: ITS3D: Inference-Time Scaling for Text-Guided 3D Diffusion Models
figure: First framework for inference-time scaling in text-guided 3D diffusion models via optimization.
abstract: ITS3D enhances generative quality in text-to-3D diffusion models by optimizing Gaussian noise input through a verifier-guided search algorithm. It introduces techniques for stability, efficiency, and exploration, including Gaussian normalization, dimensionality reduction, and a singular space reset mechanism. Experiments show improved generation quality, highlighting the potential of efficient sea

title: AnchorFlow: Training-Free 3D Editing via Latent Anchor-Aligned Flows
figure: First training-free 3D editing method that ensures latent anchor consistency via flows
abstract: AnchorFlow introduces a training-free approach to 3D shape editing that maintains geometric stability and semantic fidelity. By establishing a global latent anchor and enforcing coherence through a relaxed anchor-alignment loss, it allows for significant modifications without mask supervision. Experiments demonstrate its effectiveness in producing robust edits across various types.

title: Controllable 3D Object Generation with Single Image Prompt
figure: First controllable 3D object generation from a single image prompt via diffusion.
abstract: This paper presents two methods for generating 3D objects from a single image prompt without requiring textual inversion, enhancing control over conditions like depth and pose. The proposed techniques improve 3D consistency and outperform existing methods in user studies. The code is available on GitHub.

title: PAT3D: Physics-Augmented Text-to-3D Scene Generation
figure: First physics-augmented text-to-3D scene generation framework that integrates vision-language models with physics-based simulation.
abstract: PAT3D is a novel framework that generates physically plausible 3D scenes from text prompts by combining vision-language models with physics-based simulation. It organizes generated objects into a hierarchical scene tree and uses a differentiable simulator to ensure realistic interactions. The method enhances scene quality through simulation-in-the-loop optimization, achieving high physical plausib

title: GENA3D: Generative Amodal 3D Modeling by Bridging 2D Priors and 3D Coherence
figure: First framework that integrates 2D generative priors with 3D geometric reasoning for amodal 3D modeling.
abstract: GENA3D addresses the challenge of generating complete 3D objects under partial occlusions by combining learned 2D generative priors with 3D geometric reasoning. This approach ensures both generative plausibility and geometric coherence, allowing for the generation of coherent 3D objects from limited observations while maintaining geometric fidelity.

title: ShapeGen: Towards High-Quality 3D Shape Synthesis
figure: First high-quality image-to-3D shape generation via 3D representation improvements and linear transformers
abstract: ShapeGen addresses challenges in 3D shape generation from images, such as detail and surface quality. It introduces enhancements in representation and resolution, leveraging linear transformers to produce high-fidelity 3D assets suitable for various applications. Extensive experiments demonstrate its state-of-the-art performance.

title: MFM-point: Multi-scale Flow Matching for Point Cloud Generation
figure: First multi-scale flow matching framework for point cloud generation.
abstract: MFM-Point introduces a multi-scale Flow Matching framework for generating point clouds, enhancing scalability and performance while maintaining simplicity. It employs a coarse-to-fine generation approach and a structured downsampling and upsampling strategy to preserve geometric structure. Experimental results show it outperforms existing point-based methods and competes with representation-based

title: 4DWorldBench: A Comprehensive Evaluation Framework for 3D/4D World Generation Models
figure: First comprehensive evaluation framework for 3D/4D world generation models.
abstract: 4DWorldBench introduces a unified evaluation framework for World Generation Models, assessing their ability to create realistic 3D/4D worlds from various modalities. It measures perceptual quality, physical realism, and consistency, aiming to enhance the transition from visual to world generation. The benchmark supports tasks like Image-to-3D/4D and Text-to-3D/4D, integrating adaptive conditioning

title: PartDiffuser: Part-wise 3D Mesh Generation via Discrete Diffusion
figure: First part-wise 3D mesh generation method via semi-autoregressive diffusion.
abstract: PartDiffuser introduces a semi-autoregressive diffusion framework for generating 3D meshes from point clouds. It balances global structural consistency with local detail by employing autoregression between parts and a parallel discrete diffusion process within each part. This method significantly improves the generation of detailed 3D meshes, demonstrating superior performance over existing models

title: Yo'City: Personalized and Boundless 3D Realistic City Scene Generation via Self-Critic Expansion
figure: First personalized and boundless 3D city scene generation via self-critic expansion.
abstract: Yo'City introduces a novel framework for generating realistic 3D city scenes that are user-customized and infinitely expandable. It employs a hierarchical structure for city planning and utilizes a combination of image synthesis and image-to-3D generation techniques. The method includes a mechanism for continuous city evolution, ensuring coherent growth based on user interactions. Extensive evalua

title: LATTICE: Democratize High-Fidelity 3D Generation at Scale
figure: First framework that bridges quality and scalability in high-fidelity 3D asset generation via a semi-structured representation.
abstract: LATTICE introduces a framework for high-fidelity 3D asset generation, addressing challenges in predicting spatial structure and geometric surfaces. It utilizes VoxSet, a semi-structured representation for efficient generation, and employs a two-stage pipeline for detailed geometry production. The method achieves state-of-the-art performance while enabling scalable 3D asset creation.

title: Single Image to High-Quality 3D Object via Latent Features
figure: First framework that generates high-quality 3D objects from single images via latent features.
abstract: LatentDreamer is a novel framework that generates 3D objects from single images using a pre-trained variational autoencoder to map 3D geometries to latent features. This approach allows for the sequential generation of coarse and refined geometries along with realistic textures, achieving high fidelity and speed in the generation process.

title: RAISECity: A Multimodal Agent Framework for Reality-Aligned 3D World Generation at City-Scale
figure: First city-scale 3D world generation framework leveraging multimodal tools.
abstract: RAISECity is a framework designed for generating detailed city-scale 3D worlds, addressing challenges in quality and scalability. It utilizes an agentic approach to integrate real-world knowledge and maintain robust representations, resulting in high perceptual quality and fidelity in 3D scene generation.

title: ArticFlow: Generative Simulation of Articulated Mechanisms
figure: First generative model for articulated mechanisms using a two-stage flow matching framework.
abstract: ArticFlow introduces a two-stage flow matching framework for generating articulated 3D shapes, addressing challenges in action-dependent deformations. It learns a controllable velocity field to synthesize diverse morphologies and predict kinematics, achieving high accuracy and quality compared to existing methods.

title: Range-Edit: Semantic Mask Guided Outdoor LiDAR Scene Editing
figure: First method to edit LiDAR scans using semantic masks for point cloud generation via diffusion.
abstract: This research presents a novel approach for generating synthetic LiDAR point clouds by editing real-world scans with semantic mask guidance. The method utilizes range image projection and diffusion-based generation to ensure geometric consistency and realism, addressing challenges in acquiring diverse point cloud datasets for autonomous driving. The approach is validated on the KITTI-360 dataset,

title: WorldGen: From Text to Traversable and Interactive 3D Worlds
figure: First system to create interactive 3D worlds from text prompts via procedural and diffusion-based generation.
abstract: WorldGen is a system that automatically generates large-scale, interactive 3D worlds from text descriptions. It combines LLM-driven scene layout reasoning, procedural generation, and diffusion-based 3D generation to create coherent and navigable environments. The modular system allows for fine control over layout and style, making it accessible for creators without specialized 3D skills.

title: Mesh RAG: Retrieval Augmentation for Autoregressive Mesh Generation
figure: First retrieval-augmented framework for autoregressive mesh generation.
abstract: Mesh RAG introduces a novel framework for autoregressive mesh generation that enhances quality and speed by decoupling generation from sequential dependencies. It utilizes point cloud segmentation and registration to retrieve and integrate mesh components, allowing for efficient and parallelizable inference. The method shows significant improvements in mesh quality and enables incremental editing

title: TRIM: Scalable 3D Gaussian Diffusion Inference with Temporal and Spatial Trimming
figure: First scalable 3D Gaussian diffusion inference method with temporal and spatial trimming.
abstract: TRIM is a method designed to enhance the efficiency of 3D Gaussian diffusion models by implementing temporal and spatial trimming strategies. This approach accelerates inference without sacrificing output quality, allowing for scalable generation of 3D shapes. The method includes a lightweight selector model for evaluating latent Gaussian primitives and instance mask denoising to reduce computatio

title: FlashMesh: Faster and Better Autoregressive Mesh Synthesis via Structured Speculation
figure: First fast autoregressive mesh generation framework via structured speculation
abstract: FlashMesh introduces a fast and high-fidelity framework for generating 3D meshes using autoregressive models. It employs a predict-correct-verify paradigm to enhance inference speed and quality by leveraging structural correlations in mesh data. The method achieves significant speedup and improved fidelity in mesh generation, demonstrating the effectiveness of its speculative decoding scheme.

title: GeoSceneGraph: Geometric Scene Graph Diffusion Model for Text-guided 3D Indoor Scene Synthesis
figure: First method that synthesizes 3D indoor scenes from text using geometric scene graphs via diffusion.
abstract: GeoSceneGraph synthesizes indoor 3D scenes from text prompts by leveraging the graph structure and geometric symmetries of scenes, without relying on predefined relationship classes. It achieves performance comparable to existing methods while being suitable for resource-constrained devices.

title: B-Rep Distance Functions (BR-DF): How to Represent a B-Rep Model by Volumetric Distance Functions?
figure: First method to represent CAD B-Rep models using volumetric distance functions via a multi-branch latent diffusion model.
abstract: This paper introduces B-Rep Distance Functions (BR-DF), a new geometric representation for CAD models using signed distance functions. It encodes B-Rep geometry and topology, enabling a reliable conversion to watertight CAD models. The proposed method employs a multi-branch latent diffusion model to generate SDF and per-face UDFs, achieving high performance and a 100% success rate in generating fa

title: Learning Compact Latent Space for Representing Neural Signed Distance Functions with High-fidelity Geometry Details
figure: First method to represent multiple SDFs in a common space for high-fidelity geometry details via compact latent representations
abstract: This paper presents a method for representing multiple neural signed distance functions (SDFs) in a common latent space, enhancing the recovery of high-fidelity geometry details. The approach combines generalization and overfitting learning strategies, along with a novel sampling method to improve training efficiency and reduce artifacts. Evaluations demonstrate its advantages over existing method

title: GEN3D: Generating Domain-Free 3D Scenes from a Single Image
figure: First method that generates high-quality 3D scenes from a single image via Gaussian splatting.
abstract: GEN3D introduces a novel approach for generating diverse and high-quality 3D scenes from a single image, overcoming limitations of traditional multi-view methods. It creates an initial point cloud from RGBD data and optimizes a Gaussian splatting representation to finalize the scene. The method shows strong generalization and performance across various datasets.

title: Let Language Constrain Geometry: Vision-Language Models as Semantic and Spatial Critics for 3D Generation
figure: First framework that uses vision-language models as critics for 3D generation via dual-query signals.
abstract: VLM3D enhances text-to-3D generation by using vision-language models as semantic and spatial critics. It addresses limitations in semantic alignment and spatial understanding by introducing a dual-query critic signal. This framework improves both optimization-based and feed-forward 3D generation methods, ensuring better semantic fidelity and geometric coherence.

title: PhysX-Anything: Simulation-Ready Physical 3D Assets from Single Image
figure: First simulation-ready physical 3D generative framework from a single image via VLM.
abstract: PhysX-Anything introduces a novel framework for generating simulation-ready physical 3D assets from a single image, focusing on geometry, articulation, and physical properties. It utilizes a new tokenization method to enhance generative quality and constructs a dataset to improve diversity in physical 3D objects. The framework shows strong performance in generating robust 3D assets suitable for em

title: DriveLiDAR4D: Sequential and Controllable LiDAR Scene Generation for Autonomous Driving
figure: First sequential LiDAR scene generation with controllable foreground objects via a novel pipeline.
abstract: DriveLiDAR4D introduces a novel pipeline for generating realistic LiDAR point clouds, addressing limitations in sequential generation and scene manipulation. The method produces temporally consistent scenes with controllable foregrounds and realistic backgrounds, achieving state-of-the-art performance on nuScenes and KITTI datasets.

title: LSS3D: Learnable Spatial Shifting for Consistent and High-Quality 3D Generation from Single-Image
figure: First image-to-3D method that uses learnable spatial shifting for consistent generation across multi-view inputs.
abstract: LSS3D addresses challenges in multi-view diffusion-based 3D generation by introducing learnable spatial shifting to improve shape and texture alignment. This method enhances robustness to non-frontal perspectives and provides high-quality 3D outputs with complete geometric details. A quantitative evaluation pipeline is also included for performance comparisons.

title: Sat2RealCity: Geometry-Aware and Appearance-Controllable 3D Urban Generation from Satellite Imagery
figure: First geometry-aware and appearance-controllable framework for 3D urban generation from satellite imagery.
abstract: Sat2RealCity addresses challenges in 3D urban generation by using satellite imagery to create individual building entities, reducing reliance on large-scale 3D assets. It introduces spatial priors for geometric generation, an appearance-guided modeling mechanism, and a semantic-guided generation pipeline, significantly improving structural consistency and realism in generated urban environments.

title: LoG3D: Ultra-High-Resolution 3D Shape Modeling via Local-to-Global Partitioning
figure: First ultra-high-resolution 3D shape modeling via local-to-global partitioning with UDFs.
abstract: LoG3D introduces a novel VAE framework using unsigned distance fields for high-fidelity 3D shape generation. It employs a local-to-global architecture that processes subvolumes to capture details and ensure global coherence, achieving ultra-high resolutions and superior generative quality.

title: Target-Balanced Score Distillation
figure: First method to enhance texture realism in 3D asset generation via multi-objective optimization.
abstract: This paper presents Target-Balanced Score Distillation (TBSD), which addresses the trade-off between texture optimization and shape distortion in 3D asset generation. By utilizing Target Negative Prompts, TBSD formulates the generation process as a multi-objective optimization problem, leading to high-fidelity textures and accurate shapes, outperforming existing methods.

title: DensiCrafter: Physically-Constrained Generation and Fabrication of Self-Supporting Hollow Structures
figure: First framework for generating lightweight, self-supporting 3D hollow structures via density field optimization
abstract: DensiCrafter addresses the challenge of generating lightweight, self-supporting 3D hollow structures by optimizing density fields. It integrates with existing models and introduces physically constrained loss terms to ensure manufacturability. The method shows significant material reduction and maintains geometric fidelity, validated through real-world 3D printing.

title: AnchorDS: Anchoring Dynamic Sources for Semantically Consistent Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First text-to-3D generation method that anchors dynamic sources for improved semantic consistency via score distillation.
abstract: AnchorDS reformulates text-to-3D optimization by mapping a dynamically evolving source distribution to a fixed target distribution, using both text prompts and rendered images for guidance. This approach stabilizes generation and enhances detail, color, and semantic consistency, outperforming previous methods in quality and efficiency.

title: Fuse3D: Generating 3D Assets Controlled by Multi-Image Fusion
figure: First method capable of controllable 3D asset generation from multiple condition images
abstract: Fuse3D introduces a novel approach for generating 3D assets using multiple images to control different regions, enhancing flexibility and detail. It features a Multi-Condition Fusion Module for integrating visual features and a Local Attention Enhancement Strategy for resolving control conflicts. The method demonstrates high-quality 3D asset generation through effective fusion of 2D image regions.

title: A Finite Difference Approximation of Second Order Regularization of Neural-SDFs
figure: First finite-difference framework for curvature regularization in SDF learning.
abstract: This paper presents a finite-difference approach for curvature regularization in neural signed distance field (SDF) learning, offering a computationally efficient alternative to existing methods that rely on higher-order differentiation. The proposed method achieves comparable reconstruction fidelity while significantly reducing GPU memory usage and training time.

title: ProcGen3D: Learning Neural Procedural Graph Representations for Image-to-3D Reconstruction
figure: First neural procedural graph representation for image-to-3D reconstruction via transformer.
abstract: ProcGen3D introduces a novel approach for generating procedural graph abstractions of 3D objects from images. By employing edge-based tokenization and a transformer model, it predicts procedural graphs that can be decoded into complex 3D assets. The method incorporates Monte Carlo Tree Search for better alignment with input images, demonstrating superior performance on various object types compare

title: RaLD: Generating High-Resolution 3D Radar Point Clouds with Latent Diffusion
figure: First framework to generate high-resolution 3D radar point clouds via latent diffusion.
abstract: RaLD introduces a novel framework for generating dense and accurate 3D point clouds from raw radar spectrums, addressing the limitations of existing methods that rely on inefficient voxel representations. By leveraging latent diffusion models and integrating scene-level autoencoding, RaLD enhances the generation process for robust perception in challenging environments.

title: Rethinking Metrics and Diffusion Architecture for 3D Point Cloud Generation
figure: First architecture for generating high-fidelity 3D point clouds via diffusion.
abstract: This paper addresses the need for robust evaluation metrics in 3D point cloud generation and introduces a new architecture, the Diffusion Point Transformer, which leverages transformer-based models. It proposes improved metrics for evaluating generated point clouds and demonstrates state-of-the-art performance on the ShapeNet dataset.

title: Walking the Schrödinger Bridge: A Direct Trajectory for Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First text-to-3D generation framework that utilizes the Schrödinger Bridge for robust 3D optimization via Trajectory-Centric Distillation.
abstract: This paper presents Trajectory-Centric Distillation (TraCe), a novel framework for text-to-3D generation that addresses artifacts in generated 3D assets by formulating the process as a direct transport trajectory. It connects the current rendering to a text-conditioned target, improving quality and fidelity in 3D optimization.

title: Wonder3D++: Cross-domain Diffusion for High-fidelity 3D Generation from a Single Image
figure: First cross-domain diffusion model for high-fidelity 3D generation from a single image
abstract: Wonder3D++ introduces a novel method for generating high-fidelity textured meshes from single-view images using a cross-domain diffusion model. It improves quality and efficiency in single-view reconstruction by employing a multi-view attention mechanism and a cascaded mesh extraction algorithm, achieving robust results compared to prior works.

title: Oitijjo-3D: Generative AI Framework for Rapid 3D Heritage Reconstruction from Street View Imagery
figure: First generative AI framework for 3D heritage reconstruction from street view imagery
abstract: Oitijjo-3D is a generative AI framework that enables rapid 3D reconstruction of cultural heritage sites using Google Street View imagery. It employs a two-stage pipeline for structure-texture synthesis and neural image-to-3D generation, producing photorealistic models quickly and without the need for specialized equipment. This approach aims to democratize cultural preservation in resource-limited

title: Hierarchical Transformers for Unsupervised 3D Shape Abstraction
figure: First hierarchical transformer that learns general 3D shape hierarchies in an unsupervised manner.
abstract: HiT introduces a hierarchical neural field representation for 3D shapes, learning coarse-to-fine hierarchies across shape categories. It employs a hierarchical transformer to identify common substructures without fixed constraints, allowing for flexible inference of complex hierarchies. The model is effective in unsupervised shape segmentation across multiple categories.

title: FullPart: Generating each 3D Part at Full Resolution
figure: First part-based 3D generation framework that combines implicit and explicit paradigms for full-resolution parts.
abstract: FullPart is a novel framework for part-based 3D generation that combines implicit and explicit methods to generate detailed parts at full resolution. It addresses issues of geometric detail and alignment by using a bounding box layout and a center-point encoding strategy. The paper also introduces PartVerse-XL, a large dataset for 3D part generation, and demonstrates state-of-the-art results in th

title: FreeArt3D: Training-Free Articulated Object Generation using 3D Diffusion
figure: First training-free framework for articulated 3D object generation using 3D diffusion.
abstract: FreeArt3D introduces a training-free method for generating articulated 3D objects by repurposing a pre-trained static 3D diffusion model. It optimizes geometry, texture, and articulation parameters from a few images, achieving high fidelity and versatility without the need for extensive training data.

title: 4-Doodle: Text to 3D Sketches that Move!
figure: First framework for generating dynamic 3D sketches from text via dual-space distillation.
abstract: 4-Doodle introduces a novel task of text-to-3D sketch animation, focusing on generating stylized, view-consistent 3D vector sketches. It employs a training-free framework that utilizes pretrained image and video diffusion models to ensure multi-view consistency and structural alignment, enabling expressive motion in sketches. The method shows significant improvements in fidelity and controllabilit

title: TRELLISWorld: Training-Free World Generation from Object Generators
figure: First training-free approach for 3D scene synthesis using object diffusion models.
abstract: This paper introduces TRELLISWorld, a training-free method for 3D scene generation that repurposes text-to-3D object diffusion models as modular tile generators. It reformulates scene generation as a multi-tile denoising problem, allowing for scalable synthesis of coherent scenes while maintaining local semantic control, without the need for scene-level datasets.

title: LAMP: Data-Efficient Linear Affine Weight-Space Models for Parameter-Controlled 3D Shape Generation and Extrapolation
figure: First data-efficient framework for controllable 3D shape generation via affine mixing in weight space
abstract: LAMP is a framework for generating high-fidelity 3D geometries under parameter constraints, improving data efficiency and control. It aligns SDF decoders and generates new designs through a parameter-constrained affine mixing problem. The method shows enhanced performance in extrapolation and optimization, demonstrating its potential for design exploration and dataset generation.

title: ArtiLatent: Realistic Articulated 3D Object Generation via Structured Latents
figure: First framework that synthesizes articulated 3D objects via structured latents and latent diffusion.
abstract: ArtiLatent is a generative framework for synthesizing articulated 3D objects with fine geometry and realistic appearance. It models part geometry and articulation dynamics using a variational autoencoder and a latent diffusion model, enabling diverse sampling. The method improves visual realism by accounting for articulation-dependent visibility changes, outperforming existing approaches in geomet

title: Topology Sculptor, Shape Refiner: Discrete Diffusion Model for High-Fidelity 3D Meshes Generation
figure: First method for generating high-fidelity 3D meshes via Discrete Diffusion Models.
abstract: The paper presents Topology Sculptor, Shape Refiner (TSSR), a method for generating high-quality 3D meshes using Discrete Diffusion Models. TSSR enhances efficiency and control through parallel generation, decoupled training, and a novel connection loss, achieving high fidelity in complex datasets. The code will be available on GitHub.

title: Towards General Modality Translation with Contrastive and Predictive Latent Diffusion Bridge
figure: First general-purpose framework for modality translation that includes 3D shape generation via latent diffusion.
abstract: This paper presents the Latent Denoising Diffusion Bridge Model (LDDBM), a framework for translating information across different modalities, including multi-view to 3D shape generation. It introduces a contrastive alignment loss and a domain-agnostic architecture for noise prediction in latent space, achieving strong performance on various modality translation tasks.

title: Comparative Analysis of Procedural Planet Generators
figure: First real-time procedural planet generators using FBM and layered noise techniques
abstract: This paper introduces two real-time procedural planet generators developed in the Godot engine, utilizing Fractal Brownian Motion and Minecraft-inspired noise techniques. It includes a quadtree-based Level of Detail system and planetary mesh generation, supported by a user study comparing the generated instances.

title: Seed3D 1.0: From Images to High-Fidelity Simulation-Ready 3D Assets
figure: First model to generate simulation-ready 3D assets from single images with accurate geometry and textures.
abstract: Seed3D 1.0 is a foundation model that generates high-fidelity, simulation-ready 3D assets from single images, addressing scalability and physics accuracy challenges. It produces assets with realistic geometry and materials, suitable for integration into physics engines, and can generate complete scenes by assembling objects into coherent environments.

title: ShapeCraft: LLM Agents for Structured, Textured and Interactive 3D Modeling
figure: First framework for structured text-to-3D generation via LLM agents and GPS representation
abstract: ShapeCraft introduces a multi-agent framework for generating structured, textured, and interactive 3D models from natural language. By using a Graph-based Procedural Shape representation, it enhances the accuracy and interactivity of 3D asset creation, outperforming existing methods in generating geometrically and semantically rich models.

title: FlashWorld: High-quality 3D Scene Generation within Seconds
figure: First 3D scene generator that produces high-quality outputs from single images or text prompts in seconds via a dual-mode diffusion model.
abstract: FlashWorld introduces a generative model for rapid 3D scene generation from single images or text prompts, achieving 10-100 times faster results than prior methods while enhancing visual quality. It employs a dual-mode pre-training and cross-mode post-training strategy to integrate multi-view and 3D-oriented generation, ensuring 3D consistency and improved rendering quality.

title: Text-to-3D by Stitching a Multi-view Reconstruction Network to a Video Generator
figure: First framework that stitches a text-to-video generator with a 3D reconstruction network for text-to-3D generation.
abstract: VIST3A combines a latent text-to-video model with a 3D reconstruction system to enhance text-to-3D generation. It addresses challenges in model integration and alignment, leading to improved generation of consistent 3D scene geometry. The approach outperforms previous models, enabling high-quality text-to-pointmap generation.

title: CymbaDiff: Structured Spatial Diffusion for Sketch-based 3D Semantic Urban Scene Generation
figure: First structured spatial diffusion model for sketch-based 3D semantic urban scene generation.
abstract: CymbaDiff introduces a novel approach for generating 3D outdoor semantic scenes from sketches, addressing the lack of annotated datasets. It enhances spatial coherence and captures cylindrical continuity, achieving superior semantic consistency and realism. The method is evaluated on the new SketchSem3D benchmark, which includes diverse datasets for rigorous testing.

title: A 3D Generation Framework from Cross Modality to Parameterized Primitive
figure: First framework that generates 3D models from cross-modality inputs using parameterized primitives.
abstract: This paper presents a multi-stage framework for generating 3D models using parameterized primitives, guided by text and image inputs. It addresses challenges in surface smoothness and storage efficiency, demonstrating effectiveness on various datasets with promising results.

title: Generating Surface for Text-to-3D using 2D Gaussian Splatting
figure: First method to generate 3D object surfaces using 2D Gaussian splatting via text conditioning.
abstract: This paper presents DirectGaussian, a novel approach for generating 3D object surfaces using 2D Gaussian splatting informed by text. It addresses the challenges of complex geometric shapes by incorporating multi-view normal and texture priors, along with curvature constraints for geometric consistency. The method demonstrates the ability to create diverse and high-fidelity 3D content.

title: OBJVanish: Physically Realizable Text-to-3D Adv. Generation of LiDAR-Invisible Objects
figure: First text-to-3D adversarial generation method for creating LiDAR-invisible objects.
abstract: The paper presents Phy3DAdvGen, a novel text-to-3D adversarial generation method that creates 3D models of objects invisible to LiDAR detectors. It systematically optimizes text prompts to produce physically realizable 3D pedestrians that evade detection, demonstrating effectiveness in both simulation and real-world environments.

title: PointNSP: Autoregressive 3D Point Cloud Generation with Next-Scale Level-of-Detail Prediction
figure: First autoregressive model for 3D point cloud generation with next-scale level-of-detail prediction.
abstract: PointNSP introduces a coarse-to-fine generative framework for autoregressive point cloud generation, addressing the limitations of traditional models by preserving global shape structure and refining geometry at higher scales. It achieves state-of-the-art generation quality and efficiency, outperforming diffusion-based methods, particularly in dense generation scenarios.

title: SemMorph3D: Unsupervised Semantic-Aware 3D Morphing via Mesh-Guided Gaussians
figure: First semantic-aware 3D morphing method using mesh-guided Gaussians.
abstract: SemMorph3D presents a framework for semantic-aware 3D shape and texture morphing from multi-view images. It combines mesh-guided strategies with 3D Gaussian Splatting to ensure stable transformations while maintaining topological integrity and color consistency. The method outperforms existing techniques on the TexMorph benchmark, achieving significant improvements in morphing quality without requ

title: UniLat3D: Geometry-Appearance Unified Latents for Single-Stage 3D Generation
figure: First unified framework for geometry and appearance in single-stage 3D generation via VAE.
abstract: UniLat3D introduces a unified framework for high-fidelity 3D asset generation, integrating geometry and appearance in a single latent space. It utilizes a geometry-appearance Unified VAE to efficiently produce diverse 3D formats from a single image, achieving high quality and fidelity in a streamlined process.

title: Light-SQ: Structure-aware Shape Abstraction with Superquadrics for Generated Meshes
figure: First structure-aware shape abstraction method using superquadrics for generated meshes via SDF carving.
abstract: Light-SQ is a superquadric-based optimization framework designed for user-generated content applications, focusing on structure-aware shape abstraction. It minimizes overlap between primitives and enhances editability while preserving geometric details. The method introduces SDF carving, a block-regrow-fill strategy, and adaptive residual pruning to achieve efficient and high-fidelity shape abstra

title: M3DLayout: A Multi-Source Dataset of 3D Indoor Layouts and Structured Descriptions for 3D Generation
figure: First large-scale dataset for 3D indoor layout generation with structured descriptions.
abstract: M3DLayout is a comprehensive dataset designed for text-driven 3D indoor layout generation, featuring 21,367 layouts and over 433k object instances from diverse sources. It aims to enhance the learning capabilities of 3D layout generation models by providing a rich resource for training, enabling complex spatial and semantic pattern learning. The dataset supports both text-conditioned diffusion and

title: StrucADT: Generating Structure-controlled 3D Point Clouds with Adjacency Diffusion Transformer
figure: First structure-controllable point cloud generation model via adjacency relationships.
abstract: StrucADT introduces a novel approach to generating controllable 3D point clouds by utilizing a StructureGraph representation that captures part existences and adjacency relationships. This model employs a Diffusion Transformer to produce diverse and high-quality point clouds based on user-defined structures, achieving state-of-the-art results in controllable point cloud generation.

title: FM-SIREN & FM-FINER: Implicit Neural Representation Using Nyquist-based Orthogonality
figure: First implicit neural representation that uses Nyquist-informed frequency multipliers for improved 3D shape fitting.
abstract: FM-SIREN and FM-FINER enhance implicit neural representations by using Nyquist-informed frequency multipliers, reducing feature redundancy and improving signal reconstruction across various tasks, including 3D shape fitting. This approach maintains efficiency while outperforming existing methods.

title: Large Material Gaussian Model for Relightable 3D Generation
figure: First model that generates high-quality 3D content with PBR materials via multiview material diffusion.
abstract: This paper presents the Large Material Gaussian Model (MGM), a framework for generating high-quality 3D content with Physically Based Rendering (PBR) materials. It utilizes a multiview material diffusion model and Gaussian material representation to produce realistic 3D assets suitable for dynamic relighting. The method enhances material modeling and visual appeal compared to existing techniques.

title: Drag4D: Align Your Motion with Text-Driven 3D Scene Generation
figure: First framework that integrates object motion control with text-driven 3D scene generation via a unified architecture.
abstract: Drag4D is an interactive framework that allows users to control the motion of 3D objects generated from text and a single image. It enhances text-to-3D background generation and employs a 3D copy-and-paste method for object integration, ensuring precise spatial alignment and temporal animation along user-defined trajectories using a motion-conditioned video diffusion model.

title: Hunyuan3D-Omni: A Unified Framework for Controllable Generation of 3D Assets
figure: First unified framework for controllable 3D asset generation via multi-modal conditioning
abstract: Hunyuan3D-Omni is a framework for generating 3D assets that allows fine-grained control using various conditioning signals like images, point clouds, and skeletal poses. It integrates these modalities into a single architecture and employs a progressive sampling strategy to enhance generation accuracy and robustness, making it suitable for production workflows.

title: QuadGPT: Native Quadrilateral Mesh Generation with Autoregressive Models
figure: First autoregressive framework for generating quadrilateral meshes in an end-to-end manner
abstract: QuadGPT introduces a novel autoregressive framework for generating quadrilateral meshes directly, overcoming limitations of previous methods that convert triangle meshes. It employs a unified tokenization method and a specialized RL fine-tuning approach to enhance generation quality, setting a new benchmark for quad-mesh generation.

title: ARMesh: Autoregressive Mesh Generation via Next-Level-of-Detail Prediction
figure: First autoregressive model for mesh generation via next-level-of-detail prediction
abstract: ARMesh introduces a novel autoregressive approach to generate 3D meshes in a progressive coarse-to-fine manner, inspired by 2D image refinement techniques. By generalizing meshes to simplicial complexes, it allows for intuitive control over generation quality and time, enabling applications like mesh refinement and editing.

title: MeshMosaic: Scaling Artist Mesh Generation via Local-to-Global Assembly
figure: First local-to-global framework for artist mesh generation that scales to over 100K triangles via autoregressive modeling
abstract: MeshMosaic addresses the challenges of scaling artist-designed meshes to high triangle counts by introducing a local-to-global framework. It segments shapes into patches, generating each patch autoregressively while ensuring coherence and connectivity. This approach allows for the generation of high-resolution meshes with improved geometric fidelity and user preference, outperforming existing meth

title: MMPart: Harnessing Multi-Modal Large Language Models for Part-Aware 3D Generation
figure: First part-aware 3D generation framework from a single image via VLM and generative modeling.
abstract: MMPart introduces a framework for generating part-aware 3D models from a single image, addressing limitations in existing methods that lack structural information. It utilizes a vision-language model to create prompts and a generative model to produce isolated images of objects, which are then used to generate consistent multi-view images before reconstructing them into 3D models.

title: Vision-Language Models as Differentiable Semantic and Spatial Rewards for Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First text-to-3D generation framework integrating vision-language models as differentiable priors via Score Distillation Sampling.
abstract: VLM3D enhances text-to-3D generation by integrating vision-language models into the Score Distillation Sampling framework, addressing limitations in semantic alignment and spatial constraints. It improves 3D consistency and relational reasoning in multi-object scenes, outperforming previous methods in fidelity and coherence.

title: Causal Reasoning Elicits Controllable 3D Scene Generation
figure: First 3D scene generation method that incorporates causal reasoning via large language models.
abstract: CausalStruct introduces a framework for 3D scene generation that integrates causal reasoning to enhance the modeling of object dependencies and physical constraints. By using causal graphs and large language models, it refines scene layouts based on causal order and physics-driven adjustments, improving coherence and adaptability in generated scenes.

title: GenCAD-3D: CAD Program Generation using Multimodal Latent Space Alignment and Synthetic Dataset Balancing
figure: First multimodal framework for CAD program generation via latent diffusion models
abstract: GenCAD-3D introduces a multimodal generative framework that aligns latent embeddings between CAD and geometric encoders, utilizing latent diffusion models for generating CAD sequences. It also features SynthBal, a synthetic data augmentation strategy that enhances dataset representation for complex CAD geometries, improving reconstruction accuracy and performance on high-complexity models.

title: CraftMesh: High-Fidelity Generative Mesh Manipulation via Poisson Seamless Fusion
figure: First high-fidelity generative mesh manipulation framework via Poisson Seamless Fusion
abstract: CraftMesh addresses the challenge of controllable, high-fidelity mesh editing by decomposing the process into a pipeline that combines 2D and 3D generative models. It generates region-specific 3D meshes from edited 2D images and seamlessly fuses them into original models, achieving superior consistency and detail in complex editing tasks.

title: StyleSculptor: Zero-Shot Style-Controllable 3D Asset Generation with Texture-Geometry Dual Guidance
figure: First zero-shot style-controllable 3D asset generation via a novel SD-Attn module
abstract: StyleSculptor introduces a training-free method for generating style-guided 3D assets from content and style images. It employs a Style Disentangled Attention module for dynamic feature fusion, enabling fine-grained control over texture and geometry styles. The approach allows for exclusive stylization and adjustable style intensity, outperforming existing methods in producing high-fidelity 3D ass

title: Improving Accuracy and Efficiency of Implicit Neural Representations: Making SIREN a WINNER
figure: First method to improve SIREN for 3D shape fitting via adaptive weight initialization.
abstract: The paper introduces WINNER, a method that enhances sinusoidal representation networks (SIRENs) by addressing their limitations in fitting signals outside their frequency support. By adding Gaussian noise to the initialization of weights, WINNER improves performance in audio, image, and 3D shape fitting tasks, suggesting new strategies for optimizing neural network training.

title: Hunyuan3D Studio: End-to-End AI Pipeline for Game-Ready 3D Asset Generation
figure: First end-to-end AI pipeline for game-ready 3D asset generation via neural modules.
abstract: Hunyuan3D Studio is an AI-powered platform that automates the generation of high-quality 3D assets for game development. It integrates various neural modules to transform concept images or textual descriptions into production-ready 3D models, optimizing geometry and textures while meeting game engine requirements.

title: SPGen: Spherical Projection as Consistent and Flexible Representation for Single Image 3D Shape Generation
figure: First method to use Spherical Projection for single image 3D shape generation via diffusion.
