abstract: SPGen introduces a novel Spherical Projection representation for single image 3D shape generation, addressing inter-view inconsistencies and enabling the representation of complex internal structures. It operates in the image domain, providing consistency, flexibility, and efficiency, and shows significant improvements in geometric quality over existing methods.

title: X-Part: high fidelity and structure coherent shape decomposition
figure: First controllable generative model for semantically meaningful part decomposition of 3D shapes.
abstract: X-Part is a generative model that decomposes holistic 3D objects into semantically meaningful and structurally coherent parts. It enhances controllability and geometric fidelity by using bounding boxes as prompts and injecting point-wise semantic features. The model supports interactive part generation and achieves state-of-the-art performance in part-level shape generation, paving the way for edi

title: DreamLifting: A Plug-in Module Lifting MV Diffusion Models for 3D Asset Generation
figure: First end-to-end PBR-ready 3D asset generation via multi-view diffusion models
abstract: DreamLifting introduces the Lightweight Gaussian Asset Adapter (LGAA) for autonomous 3D asset creation, integrating geometry modeling and PBR materials using multi-view diffusion priors. The framework features a modular design with components that adapt existing diffusion models for improved data efficiency and quality in generating relightable mesh assets from 2D Gaussian Splatting.

title: SynthDrive: Scalable Real2Sim2Real Sensor Simulation Pipeline for High-Fidelity Asset Generation and Driving Data Synthesis
figure: First scalable real2sim2real system for high-fidelity asset generation via 3D generation.
abstract: SynthDrive introduces a scalable system for generating diverse scenarios in autonomous driving through 3D asset generation. It overcomes limitations of existing CG-based and learning-based methods by automating asset mining and rare-case data synthesis, enhancing the robustness of perception training.

title: Unifi3D: A Study on 3D Representations for Generation and Reconstruction in a Common Framework
figure: First unified framework for evaluating 3D representations in generation and reconstruction.
abstract: This paper presents Unifi3D, a framework for evaluating various 3D representations like voxel grids and point clouds in terms of quality and efficiency for generation and reconstruction. It emphasizes the importance of joint evaluation of generation and reconstruction processes and provides insights for selecting suitable 3D models for different applications. The code is available at GitHub.

title: Category-Aware 3D Object Composition with Disentangled Texture and Shape Multi-view Diffusion
figure: First method for category-aware 3D object composition via multi-view diffusion
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach for 3D object synthesis that integrates multiple content sources to create structurally coherent 3D models. The method utilizes multi-view images and normal maps, along with adaptive text-image harmony, to generate new 3D models while ensuring texture consistency and shape accuracy through specialized diffusion techniques. A project page is available.

title: A Continuous-Time Consistency Model for 3D Point Cloud Generation
figure: First continuous-time consistency model for 3D point cloud generation via a time-conditioned neural network.
abstract: ConTiCoM-3D is a novel model for synthesizing 3D shapes directly in point space, utilizing a continuous-time approach that avoids discretized diffusion steps. It integrates a continuous noise schedule with a geometric loss for stable training on high-dimensional point sets, achieving fast and high-fidelity shape generation. Experiments demonstrate its competitive performance against existing model

title: 3D-LATTE: Latent Space 3D Editing from Textual Instructions
figure: First training-free method for 3D editing from textual instructions via latent space manipulation
abstract: 3D-LATTE introduces a novel method for editing 3D assets based on textual instructions, overcoming limitations of previous 2D-based approaches. By operating within the latent space of a 3D diffusion model, it allows for direct manipulation of 3D geometry, achieving high-fidelity edits across various shapes and semantic changes.

title: Complete Gaussian Splats from a Single Image with Denoising Diffusion Models
figure: First method to complete 3D scenes with Gaussian splats from a single image via latent diffusion.
abstract: This paper introduces a latent diffusion model that reconstructs complete 3D scenes using Gaussian splats from a single image, addressing challenges in occluded areas. It learns a distribution of 3D representations in a self-supervised manner, enabling high-quality renderings and diverse samples.

title: Droplet3D: Commonsense Priors from Videos Facilitate 3D Generation
figure: First large-scale video dataset with multi-view annotations for 3D generation via a generative model.
abstract: Droplet3D addresses the scarcity of 3D data by leveraging videos with commonsense priors for 3D asset generation. It introduces a large-scale dataset and a generative model that supports image and text inputs, producing spatially consistent and semantically plausible 3D content. The approach shows potential for scene-level applications and is open-sourced.

title: LSD-3D: Large-Scale 3D Driving Scene Generation with Geometry Grounding
figure: First method to generate large-scale 3D driving scenes with accurate geometry via score distillation.
abstract: LSD-3D presents a novel approach to generate large-scale 3D driving scenes with accurate geometry, addressing limitations of existing neural reconstruction methods. By combining proxy geometry generation with score distillation from 2D image priors, it enables controllable and realistic scene generation, allowing for causal novel view synthesis and object permanence.

title: FastMesh: Efficient Artistic Mesh Generation via Component Decoupling
figure: First efficient artistic mesh generation via component decoupling using autoregressive models.
abstract: FastMesh introduces a novel framework for generating artistic meshes by decoupling vertex and face generation, significantly reducing redundancy in token sequences. It employs an autoregressive model for vertex generation and a bidirectional transformer for mesh completion, achieving over 8x faster generation speed and improved mesh quality compared to existing methods.

title: Scaffold Diffusion: Sparse Multi-Category Voxel Structure Generation with Discrete Diffusion
figure: First model to generate sparse multi-category 3D voxel structures via discrete diffusion.
abstract: Scaffold Diffusion introduces a generative model for creating realistic sparse multi-category 3D voxel structures, addressing challenges of memory scaling and class imbalance. By treating voxels as tokens, it extends discrete diffusion language models to generate coherent 3D structures, outperforming prior methods even with high sparsity. An interactive viewer is provided for visualizing the gener

title: SAT-SKYLINES: 3D Building Generation from Satellite Imagery and Coarse Geometric Priors
figure: First 3D building generation method from satellite imagery using coarse geometric priors.
abstract: SatSkylines introduces a novel approach for generating 3D building models from satellite images and coarse geometric priors. It addresses challenges in existing methods by modeling the transformation from noisy priors to detailed geometries, allowing for flexible geometric control. The paper also presents Skylines-50K, a dataset of over 50,000 unique 3D building assets to support detailed model ge

title: Structural Energy-Guided Sampling for View-Consistent Text-to-3D
figure: First method to enforce multi-view consistency in text-to-3D generation via structural energy-guided sampling.
abstract: This paper introduces Structural Energy-Guided Sampling (SEGS) to improve text-to-3D generation by addressing the Janus problem, which causes objects to appear distorted from certain angles. SEGS operates at sampling time to ensure multi-view consistency without retraining, enhancing geometric alignment and viewpoint fidelity.

title: MV-RAG: Retrieval Augmented Multiview Diffusion
figure: First text-to-3D pipeline that retrieves 2D images to enhance multiview diffusion synthesis.
abstract: MV-RAG is a novel text-to-3D generation pipeline that retrieves relevant 2D images from a large database to condition a multiview diffusion model, improving 3D consistency and accuracy for out-of-domain concepts. It combines multiview data training with augmented conditioning views and introduces a new evaluation set for OOD prompts, demonstrating significant improvements over existing methods.

title: Collaborative Multi-Modal Coding for High-Quality 3D Generation
figure: First feed-forward 3D-native generative model that learns from multi-modalities via collaborative coding
abstract: TriMM is a novel 3D-native generative model that integrates multi-modal data (RGB, RGBD, point clouds) to enhance 3D asset generation. It employs collaborative multi-modal coding and a triplane latent diffusion model to produce high-quality 3D assets, achieving competitive performance with limited training data.

title: Tinker: Diffusion's Gift to 3D--Multi-View Consistent Editing From Sparse Inputs without Per-Scene Optimization
figure: First framework for multi-view consistent 3D editing from sparse inputs via diffusion models
abstract: Tinker is a framework for high-fidelity 3D editing that enables multi-view consistent edits from minimal images without per-scene optimization. It utilizes pretrained diffusion models to achieve this, introducing components for reference-driven edits and novel-view generation. Tinker aims to simplify 3D content creation and has shown state-of-the-art performance in various tasks.

title: PhysGM: Large Physical Gaussian Model for Feed-Forward 4D Synthesis
figure: First feed-forward framework for 4D synthesis using Gaussian representation from a single image
abstract: PhysGM is a feed-forward framework that predicts 3D Gaussian representations and physical properties from a single image, enabling fast 4D rendering and simulation. It overcomes limitations of previous methods by integrating physics-aware reconstruction and optimizing simulations with reference videos. The approach is validated with a new dataset of 50K+ 3D assets annotated with physical propertie

title: UniUGG: Unified 3D Understanding and Generation via Geometric-Semantic Encoding
figure: First unified framework for 3D understanding and generation via latent diffusion model
abstract: UniUGG introduces a unified framework for 3D understanding and generation, utilizing a latent diffusion model to create high-quality 3D representations from reference images. It enhances spatial understanding through a geometric-semantic learning strategy, supporting both 3D generation and visual question answering tasks.

title: HierOctFusion: Multi-scale Octree-based 3D Shape Generation via Part-Whole-Hierarchy Message Passing
figure: First part-aware multi-scale octree diffusion model for 3D shape generation.
abstract: HierOctFusion is a part-aware multi-scale octree diffusion model that improves hierarchical feature interaction for generating fine-grained and sparse 3D object structures. It incorporates a cross-attention conditioning mechanism to effectively propagate semantic features from parts to the whole, addressing the limitations of existing holistic modeling approaches. Experiments show enhanced shape q

title: Quantum Visual Fields with Neural Amplitude Encoding
figure: First quantum implicit neural representation for 3D geometric field learning via neural amplitude encoding
abstract: This paper introduces Quantum Visual Fields (QVF), a novel architecture for learning 2D images and 3D geometric fields using quantum implicit neural representations. QVF encodes classical data into quantum statevectors, enabling efficient training and direct measurement extraction. Experiments show its effectiveness in 3D shape interpolation and field completion, outperforming existing quantum met

title: VertexRegen: Mesh Generation with Continuous Level of Detail
figure: First mesh generation framework enabling continuous level of detail via autoregressive modeling.
abstract: VertexRegen is a novel framework for mesh generation that allows for continuous levels of detail. Unlike existing methods that generate meshes in a partial manner, it reformulates the process as the reversal of edge collapse, enabling anytime generation of valid meshes with varying detail levels. Experimental results show it produces high-quality meshes comparable to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Empowering Children to Create AI-Enabled Augmented Reality Experiences
figure: First AR-based environment that empowers children to create 3D characters using text-to-3D generative AI models.
abstract: Capybara is an AR-based visual programming environment that allows children to create and animate 3D characters using AI. It enables customization and interaction with physical objects, fostering creativity and engagement in learning experiences. User studies indicate its effectiveness in bridging virtual and physical worlds.

title: LL3M: Large Language 3D Modelers
figure: First system that generates 3D assets via code-writing using LLMs
abstract: LL3M is a multi-agent system that utilizes large language models to generate 3D assets by writing Python code for Blender. It reformulates shape generation as a code-writing task, allowing for modularity and integration with artist workflows. The system coordinates LLM agents to create and edit geometry based on text prompts, enabling diverse and unconstrained shapes and materials. The project sho

title: FantasyStyle: Controllable Stylized Distillation for 3D Gaussian Splatting
figure: First 3DGS-based style transfer framework relying entirely on diffusion model distillation.
abstract: FantasyStyle addresses challenges in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) style transfer, such as multi-view inconsistency and content leakage. It introduces a framework that enhances cross-view consistency and employs negative guidance to optimize 3D Gaussians, achieving superior stylization quality and realism. The method outperforms existing approaches in various scenes and styles, with code available

title: Make Your MoVe: Make Your 3D Contents by Adapting Multi-View Diffusion Models to External Editing
figure: First method to enhance 3D content editing while preserving geometry via multi-view diffusion models
abstract: This paper presents a method for generating personalized 3D content by adapting multi-view diffusion models to external editing. It introduces a geometry preservation module that aligns edited assets with their original geometry, improving multi-view consistency and mesh quality without compromising the underlying shape.

title: MeshLLM: Empowering Large Language Models to Progressively Understand and Generate 3D Mesh
figure: First framework that leverages LLMs to generate text-serialized 3D meshes via Primitive-Mesh decomposition.
abstract: MeshLLM is a novel framework that utilizes large language models to understand and generate 3D meshes serialized in text. It introduces a Primitive-Mesh decomposition strategy to enhance dataset scale and improve mesh topology understanding, outperforming existing methods in quality and comprehension.

title: Guiding Diffusion-Based Articulated Object Generation by Partial Point Cloud Alignment and Physical Plausibility Constraints
figure: First diffusion model for generating articulated objects with physical plausibility via point cloud alignment.
abstract: PhysNAP introduces a diffusion model for generating articulated objects that aligns with partial point clouds and enhances physical plausibility. It uses signed distance functions (SDFs) to represent part shapes and incorporates constraints to ensure non-penetration and mobility. The model is evaluated on the PartNet-Mobility dataset, showing improved constraint consistency and generative ability

title: Sel3DCraft: Interactive Visual Prompts for User-Friendly Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First interactive visual prompt system for user-friendly text-to-3D generation.
abstract: Sel3DCraft enhances text-to-3D generation by introducing a visual prompt engineering system that guides users through the creation process. It features a dual-branch structure for candidate exploration, a multi-view hybrid scoring method for model assessment, and a visual analytics suite for defect identification. User studies show it outperforms existing T23D systems in fostering creativity.

title: XSpecMesh: Quality-Preserving Auto-Regressive Mesh Generation Acceleration via Multi-Head Speculative Decoding
figure: First quality-preserving acceleration method for auto-regressive mesh generation via multi-head speculative decoding
abstract: XSpecMesh accelerates auto-regressive mesh generation by using a multi-head speculative decoding approach, allowing multiple tokens to be predicted in parallel. It includes a verification and resampling strategy to ensure quality, achieving a 1.7x speedup in inference without compromising generation quality. Code will be released.

title: BANG: Dividing 3D Assets via Generative Exploded Dynamics
figure: First generative model for intuitive part-level decomposition of 3D objects via latent diffusion.
abstract: BANG introduces a generative approach for part-level decomposition of 3D objects, utilizing a latent diffusion model to create smooth sequences of exploded states while maintaining geometric coherence. It allows users to control the decomposition process with spatial prompts and extends to multimodal interactions for enhanced 3D creation workflows, including applications in 3D printing.

title: EarthCrafter: Scalable 3D Earth Generation via Dual-Sparse Latent Diffusion
figure: First scalable 3D Earth generation framework via dual-sparse latent diffusion.
abstract: EarthCrafter introduces a novel framework for large-scale 3D Earth generation, leveraging a dual innovation in data infrastructure and model architecture. It utilizes the Aerial-Earth3D dataset and employs dual sparse 3D-VAEs for efficient geometric and textural generation, enabling versatile applications while ensuring geographic plausibility.

title: Blended Point Cloud Diffusion for Localized Text-guided Shape Editing
figure: First method for localized shape editing via point cloud diffusion with structural guidance.
abstract: This paper presents a novel inpainting-based framework for localized editing of 3D shapes represented as point clouds, utilizing diffusion models to ensure global coherence while allowing fine-grained modifications. The proposed coordinate blending algorithm enhances identity preservation during edits, outperforming existing techniques in fidelity and adherence to textual descriptions.

title: Towards Geometric and Textural Consistency 3D Scene Generation via Single Image-guided Model Generation and Layout Optimization
figure: First framework for 3D scene generation with geometric and textural consistency via single image guidance
abstract: This paper presents a three-stage framework for generating 3D scenes from a single RGB image, focusing on geometric accuracy and texture fidelity. The method includes instance segmentation, spatial geometry capture, and layout optimization to ensure coherent multi-object scenes. Experiments show significant improvements over existing methods in both object quality and scene layout synthesis.

title: DreamScene: 3D Gaussian-based End-to-end Text-to-3D Scene Generation
figure: First end-to-end framework for high-quality editable 3D scene generation from text via Gaussian-based sampling.
abstract: DreamScene is an innovative framework that generates 3D scenes from natural language, addressing challenges in automation and consistency. It utilizes a scene planning module and a graph-based placement algorithm to create structured layouts, followed by a multi-timestep sampling method for object geometry. The system also allows for fine-grained scene editing and demonstrates superior quality and

title: AutoPartGen: Autogressive 3D Part Generation and Discovery
figure: First autoregressive model for 3D part generation from images and existing 3D objects
abstract: AutoPartGen is a model that generates 3D objects composed of parts in an autoregressive manner, using inputs like images, 2D masks, or existing 3D objects. It leverages a latent 3D representation with strong compositional properties, allowing for coherent assembly of parts into complete objects or scenes. The model demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in 3D part generation.

title: PhysX-3D: Physical-Grounded 3D Asset Generation
figure: First end-to-end paradigm for physical-grounded 3D asset generation via a feed-forward framework.
abstract: PhysX-3D introduces a novel approach to 3D asset generation that incorporates physical properties, addressing the gap in existing models that focus solely on geometry and texture. The framework includes PhysXNet, a physics-grounded dataset, and PhysXGen, which generates 3D assets from images while ensuring physical accuracy. The method shows promising results in producing realistic 3D assets suita

title: Robust 3D-Masked Part-level Editing in 3D Gaussian Splatting with Regularized Score Distillation Sampling
figure: First robust 3D Gaussian editing framework enabling precise part-level modifications via regularized score distillation.
abstract: RoMaP introduces a local 3D Gaussian editing framework that enhances part-level modifications through robust 3D mask generation and a regularized score distillation loss. It addresses challenges in achieving precise local edits by improving part segmentations and contextual coherence, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance in local 3D editing of Gaussian scenes and objects.

title: Advancing Text-to-3D Generation with Linearized Lookahead Variational Score Distillation
figure: First text-to-3D generation method using linearized lookahead for variational score distillation.
abstract: This paper introduces Linearized Lookahead Variational Score Distillation ($L^2$-VSD) to enhance text-to-3D generation by addressing convergence issues in variational score distillation. By optimizing the order of score model and 3D model interactions, the method improves generation quality and stability, outperforming previous techniques in extensive experiments.

title: From One to More: Contextual Part Latents for 3D Generation
figure: First part-aware diffusion framework for coherent multi-part 3D generation.
abstract: The paper introduces CoPart, a part-aware diffusion framework that enhances 3D generation by decomposing objects into contextual part latents. This approach addresses limitations in existing models by enabling part independence, relationship modeling, and fine-grained conditioning. The authors also present Partverse, a new dataset for training, and demonstrate CoPart's effectiveness in part-level

title: OmniPart: Part-Aware 3D Generation with Semantic Decoupling and Structural Cohesion
figure: First part-aware 3D object generation framework that decouples structure planning and synthesis via autoregressive and flow models.
abstract: OmniPart introduces a framework for part-aware 3D object generation, enhancing control over part structures through a two-stage process involving autoregressive planning and spatially-conditioned synthesis. This method allows for intuitive part decomposition and supports user-defined granularity, achieving state-of-the-art performance in 3D content generation.

title: Empowering Bridge Digital Twins by Bridging the Data Gap with a Unified Synthesis Framework
figure: First framework that generates complete 3D bridge point clouds with annotations via a unified synthesis approach
abstract: This paper presents a framework for generating 3D bridge data to address challenges in traditional inspection methods. It creates complete point clouds with annotations and simulates diverse incomplete point clouds for training segmentation and completion networks. The framework enhances the automated management of bridge infrastructure.

title: DreamArt: Generating Interactable Articulated Objects from a Single Image
figure: First framework that generates interactable articulated objects from single-view images via a three-stage pipeline.
abstract: DreamArt introduces a novel framework for generating high-fidelity articulated objects from single-view images, addressing challenges in part decomposition and articulation modeling. It employs a three-stage pipeline for mesh reconstruction, part-level articulation modeling, and texture refinement, demonstrating effective generation of articulated assets.

title: SegmentDreamer: Towards High-fidelity Text-to-3D Synthesis with Segmented Consistency Trajectory Distillation
figure: First framework that enhances text-to-3D generation via Segmented Consistency Trajectory Distillation.
abstract: SegmentDreamer introduces a novel framework for high-fidelity text-to-3D generation by addressing the imbalance in consistency models. It reformulates Score Distillation Sampling through Segmented Consistency Trajectory Distillation, improving visual quality and enabling efficient 3D asset creation. Experiments show it outperforms existing methods.

title: Sat2City: 3D City Generation from A Single Satellite Image with Cascaded Latent Diffusion
figure: First 3D city generation from a single satellite image via cascaded latent diffusion.
abstract: Sat2City introduces a framework for generating detailed 3D city structures from satellite images using a cascaded latent diffusion model. It addresses the limitations of existing methods by utilizing sparse voxel grids and a novel dataset of synthesized 3D cities. The approach enhances fidelity in urban scene generation, making it applicable for gaming and digital twins.

title: LangScene-X: Reconstruct Generalizable 3D Language-Embedded Scenes with TriMap Video Diffusion
figure: First framework to generate generalizable 3D language-embedded scenes from sparse views via TriMap video diffusion.
abstract: LangScene-X introduces a generative framework for creating 3D language-embedded scenes from sparse views, addressing challenges in traditional reconstruction methods. It utilizes a TriMap video diffusion model to generate consistent RGBs, normals, and segmentation maps, and employs a Language Quantized Compressor for efficient language embedding. The method shows improved quality and generalizabil

title: Topology-Preserved Auto-regressive Mesh Generation in the Manner of Weaving Silk
figure: First auto-regressive mesh generation method that preserves topology via novel tokenization.
abstract: This paper presents a new approach to auto-regressive mesh generation that enhances topology preservation through a novel tokenization algorithm. By ensuring critical geometric properties, the method achieves state-of-the-art compression efficiency and demonstrates improved geometric integrity in generated meshes.

title: DiGA3D: Coarse-to-Fine Diffusional Propagation of Geometry and Appearance for Versatile 3D Inpainting
figure: First unified 3D inpainting pipeline that propagates geometry and appearance via diffusion.
abstract: DiGA3D is a novel 3D inpainting pipeline that addresses challenges in maintaining appearance and geometry consistency across multiple views. It utilizes diffusion models for robust feature propagation and introduces a new loss function to enhance geometric consistency. The method shows effectiveness across various 3D inpainting tasks.

title: TextMesh4D: Zero-shot Text-to-4D Mesh Generation
figure: First zero-shot framework for text-to-4D mesh generation via Jacobian Deformation Field.
abstract: TextMesh4D introduces a novel approach for generating dynamic 3D meshes from text prompts, addressing challenges in topology and deformation. By shifting deformation modeling to faces and employing a Local-Global Semantic Regularizer, it achieves high fidelity and temporal consistency in generated 4D assets, demonstrating efficiency on standard hardware.

title: SG-LDM: Semantic-Guided LiDAR Generation via Latent-Aligned Diffusion
figure: First semantic-guided lidar point cloud generation via latent-aligned diffusion.
abstract: SG-LDM introduces a Semantic-Guided Lidar Diffusion Model that enhances lidar point cloud synthesis by leveraging semantic conditioning. This approach operates directly in the lidar space, achieving high-fidelity point clouds and enabling cross-domain translation for improved data augmentation in downstream tasks. Experiments show SG-LDM outperforms existing models.

title: BridgeShape: Latent Diffusion Schrödinger Bridge for 3D Shape Completion
figure: First 3D shape completion method using latent diffusion Schrödinger bridge.
abstract: BridgeShape introduces a novel framework for 3D shape completion that formulates the task as an optimal transport problem, enhancing the transition from incomplete to complete shapes. It utilizes a Depth-Enhanced VQ-VAE to encode shapes into a compact latent space, improving geometric detail and resolution. The method achieves state-of-the-art performance on 3D shape completion benchmarks.

title: AlignCVC: Aligning Cross-View Consistency for Single-Image-to-3D Generation
figure: First method to improve single-image-to-3D generation via distribution alignment.
abstract: AlignCVC introduces a novel framework for single-image-to-3D generation that enhances cross-view consistency by aligning generated and reconstructed multi-view distributions with the ground-truth. This approach improves generation quality and accelerates inference, demonstrating effectiveness in 3D generation tasks.

title: Geometry and Perception Guided Gaussians for Multiview-consistent 3D Generation from a Single Image
figure: First method that integrates geometry and perception for multiview-consistent 3D generation from a single image via Gaussian optimization.
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach for generating realistic 3D objects from single-view images by integrating geometry and perception information. The method uses geometry priors for rough shape capture and perception priors from a 2D diffusion model to enhance multiview consistency. It introduces Score Distillation Sampling for effective knowledge transfer and employs a reprojection-based strat

title: DreamAnywhere: Object-Centric Panoramic 3D Scene Generation
figure: First modular system for fast 3D scene generation from text via hybrid inpainting
abstract: DreamAnywhere is a modular system for generating 3D scenes from text, addressing limitations of existing methods by synthesizing 360° panoramic images, decomposing them into objects, and creating detailed 3D representations. It supports immersive navigation and object-level editing, making it suitable for low-budget movie production and rapid prototyping, with significant improvements in coherence

title: PrITTI: Primitive-based Generation of Controllable and Editable 3D Semantic Urban Scenes
figure: First primitive-based model for controllable and editable 3D semantic urban scene generation via latent diffusion.
abstract: PrITTI introduces a primitive-based approach for generating 3D semantic urban scenes using a latent diffusion model. This method allows for diverse, controllable, and editable scene generation with improved efficiency and lower memory usage compared to traditional voxel-based methods. It also supports various applications like scene editing and photo-realistic synthesis.

title: PhysID: Physics-based Interactive Dynamics from a Single-view Image
figure: First method to generate physics-based interactive dynamics from a single-view image via large generative models.
abstract: PhysID enables the creation of physics-based interactive dynamics from a single-view image, utilizing large generative models for 3D mesh generation and physical property prediction. This approach simplifies 3D modeling and property calibration, allowing for real-time, interactive experiences on mobile devices with minimal manual input.

title: Programmable-Room: Interactive Textured 3D Room Meshes Generation Empowered by Large Language Models
figure: First interactive framework for generating and editing 3D room meshes via natural language instructions.
abstract: Programmable-Room is a framework that generates and edits 3D room meshes based on natural language instructions. It breaks down the task into simpler steps, utilizing a large language model for visual programming and a diffusion model for texture generation. The framework demonstrates flexibility and outperforms existing models in both quantitative and qualitative assessments.

title: Assembler: Scalable 3D Part Assembly via Anchor Point Diffusion
figure: First framework for scalable 3D part assembly via diffusion models.
abstract: Assembler is a framework for 3D part assembly that reconstructs complete objects from part meshes and reference images. It employs diffusion models to sample plausible configurations, addressing challenges in scaling to diverse objects. The method introduces a shape-centric representation based on sparse anchor point clouds and constructs a large dataset of part-object assemblies. It achieves stat

title: Hunyuan3D 2.5: Towards High-Fidelity 3D Assets Generation with Ultimate Details
figure: First high-fidelity 3D asset generator with detailed shape and texture via diffusion models
abstract: Hunyuan3D 2.5 is a suite of 3D diffusion models designed for generating high-fidelity textured 3D assets. It introduces a new shape foundation model, LATTICE, which enhances shape generation with a focus on detail and quality. The model achieves significant improvements in both shape and texture generation, outperforming previous methods.

title: Nabla-R2D3: Effective and Efficient 3D Diffusion Alignment with 2D Rewards
figure: First effective 3D diffusion model alignment using 2D rewards via reinforcement learning.
abstract: Nabla-R2D3 addresses the challenge of generating high-quality 3D assets by introducing a reinforcement learning framework that aligns 3D diffusion models with 2D rewards. This method enhances the ability of generative models to produce realistic textures and geometries, achieving better performance than traditional finetuning approaches.

title: Dive3D: Diverse Distillation-based Text-to-3D Generation via Score Implicit Matching
figure: First text-to-3D generation framework using Score Implicit Matching for diverse outputs
abstract: Dive3D introduces a novel framework for text-to-3D generation that enhances diversity and text alignment by replacing KL-based objectives with Score Implicit Matching loss. This approach improves visual fidelity and outperforms existing methods in qualitative and quantitative assessments across various prompts.

title: X-Scene: Large-Scale Driving Scene Generation with High Fidelity and Flexible Controllability
figure: First framework for large-scale driving scene generation with high fidelity and flexible controllability via diffusion models
abstract: X-Scene introduces a novel framework for generating large-scale driving scenes with geometric intricacy and appearance fidelity. It allows for multi-granular control through user input and semantic guidance, enhancing scene composition. The method generates 3D semantic occupancy and corresponding multi-view images, ensuring alignment and temporal consistency. The approach supports diverse applicat

title: GeoSDF: Plane Geometry Diagram Synthesis via Signed Distance Field
figure: First framework to synthesize plane geometry diagrams via Signed Distance Field.
abstract: GeoSDF introduces a novel framework for automatically generating plane geometry diagrams using Signed Distance Field (SDF). It represents geometric elements in SDF and constructs constraint functions to optimize the relationships between them. The method ensures mathematical accuracy and visual plausibility, achieving high synthesis accuracy and solving geometry problems effectively.

title: NeuVAS: Neural Implicit Surfaces for Variational Shape Modeling
figure: First variational shape modeling method using neural implicit surfaces with sparse input shape control.
abstract: NeuVAS introduces a variational approach for modeling shapes using neural implicit surfaces, addressing challenges in generating high-quality surfaces from sparse geometric controls like 3D curve sketches. The method incorporates a smoothness term to minimize shape variation and effectively models sharp feature curves, showing significant advantages over existing techniques.

title: EmbodiedGen: Towards a Generative 3D World Engine for Embodied Intelligence
figure: First platform for scalable interactive 3D world generation via generative AI
abstract: EmbodiedGen is a toolkit for generating high-quality, controllable, and photorealistic 3D assets for embodied intelligence tasks. It supports various generation methods including Image-to-3D and Text-to-3D, addressing the limitations of traditional 3D asset creation. The generated assets can be used in physics simulations, enhancing the training and evaluation of AI systems.

title: Efficient Part-level 3D Object Generation via Dual Volume Packing
figure: First part-level 3D object generation method that uses dual volume packing via a single input image.
abstract: This paper presents a novel framework for part-level 3D object generation, addressing the limitations of existing methods that produce a single fused mesh. The proposed method generates high-quality 3D objects with semantically meaningful parts from a single image, utilizing a dual volume packing strategy to enhance quality, diversity, and generalization.

title: DreamCS: Geometry-Aware Text-to-3D Generation with Unpaired 3D Reward Supervision
figure: First text-to-3D generation framework that integrates unpaired 3D reward supervision for human-aligned geometry.
abstract: DreamCS addresses the challenges in text-to-3D generation by introducing a novel framework that utilizes a large-scale unpaired 3D preference dataset and a reward model to enhance the alignment of generated 3D assets with human preferences. The method shows significant improvements in producing geometrically faithful and preferred 3D models compared to existing techniques.

title: LLM-to-Phy3D: Physically Conform Online 3D Object Generation with LLMs
figure: First physically conforming 3D object generation using LLMs via iterative refinement.
abstract: LLM-to-Phy3D enhances existing LLM-to-3D models by enabling the generation of physically viable 3D objects through a novel online refinement loop. This approach integrates visual and physics-based evaluations to improve geometric novelty and physical performance, demonstrating significant advancements in generative design for engineering applications.

title: Orientation Matters: Making 3D Generative Models Orientation-Aligned
figure: First orientation-aligned 3D object generation from single images via multi-view diffusion and VAE.
abstract: This paper introduces orientation-aligned 3D object generation, addressing misalignment issues in existing models. By constructing the Objaverse-OA dataset, the authors fine-tune 3D generative models to produce consistently oriented objects from single images. The method shows improved performance over traditional alignment techniques and enables applications like zero-shot orientation estimation

title: R3D2: Realistic 3D Asset Insertion via Diffusion for Autonomous Driving Simulation
figure: First diffusion model for realistic 3D asset insertion in autonomous driving simulation.
abstract: R3D2 is a lightweight diffusion model that enables realistic insertion of complete 3D assets into existing driving scenes, addressing limitations of traditional simulation methods. It generates plausible rendering effects in real time and enhances the realism of inserted assets, facilitating scalable validation of autonomous driving systems. The model is trained on a novel dataset and supports tex

title: NOVA3D: Normal Aligned Video Diffusion Model for Single Image to 3D Generation
figure: First single-image-to-3D generation framework leveraging video diffusion for improved multi-view consistency
abstract: NOVA3D is a novel framework for generating 3D content from a single image, utilizing strong 3D priors from a pretrained video diffusion model. It incorporates a Geometry-Temporal Alignment attention mechanism to enhance multi-view consistency and texture fidelity, addressing inaccuracies in pose alignment. The method shows significant improvements over existing approaches.

title: CrossGen: Learning and Generating Cross Fields for Quad Meshing
figure: First framework that generates cross fields for quad meshing via feed-forward prediction and latent generative modeling.
abstract: CrossGen introduces a novel framework for generating cross fields essential for quad mesh generation, balancing efficiency and quality. It utilizes a point-sampled surface as input, employing an auto-encoder to encode surfaces into a sparse voxel grid. The method supports rapid computation of high-quality cross fields and incorporates a diffusion model for generating new shapes from partial inputs

title: PartCrafter: Structured 3D Mesh Generation via Compositional Latent Diffusion Transformers
figure: First structured 3D generative model that synthesizes multiple 3D meshes from a single RGB image via compositional latent diffusion.
abstract: PartCrafter is a novel 3D generative model that creates multiple semantically meaningful and geometrically distinct 3D meshes from a single RGB image. It employs a unified compositional generation architecture, allowing for end-to-end part-aware generation of both individual objects and complex scenes. The model introduces a compositional latent space and a hierarchical attention mechanism to ensu

title: Voyager: Long-Range and World-Consistent Video Diffusion for Explorable 3D Scene Generation
figure: First method for generating explorable 3D scenes from a single image via video diffusion.
abstract: Voyager introduces a video diffusion framework for generating world-consistent 3D point-cloud sequences from a single image, allowing for user-defined camera paths. It integrates components for aligned RGB and depth video generation, long-range exploration, and scalable data processing, improving visual quality and geometric accuracy for 3D scene generation.

title: MIND: Material Interface Generation from UDFs for Non-Manifold Surface Reconstruction
figure: First method to generate material interfaces directly from UDFs for non-manifold mesh extraction.
abstract: MIND introduces a novel algorithm for generating material interfaces from unsigned distance fields (UDFs), enabling effective non-manifold mesh extraction. By deriving a multi-labeled global field, the method addresses challenges in representing complex geometries and significantly improves upon existing techniques. Extensive experiments validate its robustness across various data sources.

title: ReSpace: Text-Driven Autoregressive 3D Indoor Scene Synthesis and Editing
figure: First autoregressive framework for text-driven 3D indoor scene synthesis and editing.
abstract: ReSpace introduces a generative framework for autoregressive text-driven 3D indoor scene synthesis and editing, addressing limitations of current methods. It features a structured scene representation with explicit room boundaries and allows for object manipulation through natural language. The approach surpasses state-of-the-art in object addition and achieves high-quality scene synthesis.

title: ShapeLLM-Omni: A Native Multimodal LLM for 3D Generation and Understanding
figure: First native multimodal LLM that generates and understands 3D assets via VQVAE.
abstract: ShapeLLM-Omni is a native 3D large language model that can understand and generate 3D assets and text. It utilizes a 3D vector-quantized variational autoencoder for efficient shape representation and reconstruction. The model is trained on a large-scale dataset, 3D-Alpaca, which includes generation, comprehension, and editing tasks, aiming to enhance multimodal models with 3D capabilities.

title: WorldExplorer: Towards Generating Fully Navigable 3D Scenes
figure: First method to generate fully navigable 3D scenes from text via autoregressive video trajectory generation
abstract: WorldExplorer introduces a novel approach to generating fully navigable 3D scenes from text, overcoming limitations of existing methods. It utilizes autoregressive video trajectory generation to create consistent visual quality across various viewpoints, enabling realistic exploration of virtual environments.

title: PromptVFX: Text-Driven Fields for Open-World 3D Gaussian Animation
figure: First text-driven framework for 3D Gaussian animation via field prediction.
abstract: PromptVFX introduces a text-driven framework for 3D animation that reformulates the task as a field prediction problem. It utilizes large language and vision-language models to generate time-varying 4D flow fields for 3D Gaussians, allowing users to create visual effects with simple text prompts. This approach simplifies the animation process, making it accessible for both novices and experts.

title: ArtiScene: Language-Driven Artistic 3D Scene Generation Through Image Intermediary
figure: First automated pipeline for artistic 3D scene generation using 2D images as intermediaries.
abstract: ArtiScene simplifies 3D scene design by leveraging generated 2D images to guide 3D synthesis, allowing users to create scenes from text descriptions without extensive training. The method outperforms existing benchmarks in layout and aesthetic quality, demonstrating high generalizability across various scenes and styles.

title: LTM3D: Bridging Token Spaces for Conditional 3D Generation with Auto-Regressive Diffusion Framework
figure: First framework that combines diffusion and auto-regressive models for conditional 3D shape generation.
abstract: LTM3D is a framework that integrates diffusion and auto-regressive models for conditional 3D shape generation. It enhances token dependency learning and supports multiple 3D representations. The method shows improved fidelity and accuracy in generating shapes from text and images, outperforming existing techniques.

title: Uni-Instruct: One-step Diffusion Model through Unified Diffusion Divergence Instruction
figure: First unified framework for one-step diffusion models that improves text-to-3D generation via diffusion expansion theory.
abstract: Uni-Instruct unifies over 10 one-step diffusion distillation methods into a new framework, enhancing the training of diffusion models. It achieves state-of-the-art performance on CIFAR10 and ImageNet benchmarks and shows improved results in text-to-3D generation, outperforming previous methods in quality and diversity.

title: ART-DECO: Arbitrary Text Guidance for 3D Detailizer Construction
figure: First 3D detailizer that generates high-quality assets from coarse proxies using text guidance via Score Distillation Sampling.
abstract: The paper presents a 3D detailizer that transforms coarse 3D shapes into detailed assets guided by text prompts. It utilizes a pretrained multi-view image diffusion model and Score Distillation Sampling to generate shapes with varying structures and consistent styles. The method allows for interactive editing and produces superior quality compared to existing models, demonstrating strong generaliz

title: Harnessing the Power of Training-Free Techniques in Text-to-2D Generation for Text-to-3D Generation via Score Distillation Sampling
figure: First exploration of training-free techniques in text-to-3D generation via Score Distillation Sampling.
abstract: This paper investigates the impact of training-free techniques on Score Distillation Sampling for text-to-3D generation. It reveals trade-offs in object size, surface smoothness, texture details, and geometric errors, providing insights for optimizing these techniques dynamically during the generation process.

title: CAD-Coder: Text-to-CAD Generation with Chain-of-Thought and Geometric Reward
figure: First text-to-CAD generation framework using CadQuery scripts via LLMs.
abstract: CAD-Coder is a framework that reformulates text-to-CAD generation as creating CadQuery scripts, enhancing geometric validation and modeling vocabulary. It employs a two-stage learning pipeline with supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning, along with a chain-of-thought planning process. The framework allows LLMs to generate diverse and valid CAD models from natural language, pushing the b

title: ViewCraft3D: High-Fidelity and View-Consistent 3D Vector Graphics Synthesis
figure: First method for efficient 3D vector graphics synthesis with view consistency via 3D priors.
abstract: ViewCraft3D (VC3D) is an efficient method for generating 3D vector graphics that maintains view consistency. It utilizes 3D object analysis and a geometric extraction algorithm to fit graphics to the underlying structure, enhancing visual quality while reducing computational overhead. Experiments show VC3D outperforms previous methods in both qualitative and quantitative evaluations.

title: Equivariant Flow Matching for Point Cloud Assembly
figure: First equivariant flow matching model for point cloud assembly.
abstract: This paper introduces equivariant diffusion assembly (Eda), a model for reconstructing complete 3D shapes from multiple point cloud pieces. It leverages flow matching to learn related vector fields, ensuring efficient training and competitive performance on practical datasets, even with non-overlapping input pieces.

title: Generative Data Augmentation for Object Point Cloud Segmentation
figure: First part-aware generative model for point cloud segmentation via diffusion.
abstract: This paper presents a generative data augmentation (GDA) pipeline that utilizes a part-aware generative model based on diffusion to enhance point cloud segmentation training. By generating high-quality point clouds conditioned on segmentation masks, the GDA approach enriches training data with variants and pseudo-labeled samples, outperforming traditional data augmentation methods.

title: SeaLion: Semantic Part-Aware Latent Point Diffusion Models for 3D Generation
figure: First semantic part-aware latent point diffusion model for 3D point cloud generation
abstract: SeaLion introduces a novel diffusion model for generating diverse point clouds with fine-grained segmentation labels. It predicts noise for perturbed latent points and their associated part labels during denoising, enhancing generation quality. The model is evaluated using a new part-aware Chamfer distance metric and shows significant performance improvements over existing methods.

title: Direct3D-S2: Gigascale 3D Generation Made Easy with Spatial Sparse Attention
figure: First scalable 3D generation framework using Spatial Sparse Attention for efficient volumetric representation.
abstract: Direct3D-S2 is a scalable framework for generating high-resolution 3D shapes using Signed Distance Functions (SDFs). It introduces a Spatial Sparse Attention mechanism to enhance efficiency in Diffusion Transformer computations, achieving significant speedups and improved training stability. The model is capable of training at high resolutions with reduced GPU requirements, making gigascale 3D gen

title: Mesh-RFT: Enhancing Mesh Generation via Fine-grained Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
figure: First method to optimize mesh quality at the granularity of individual faces via fine-grained reinforcement fine-tuning.
abstract: Mesh-RFT addresses challenges in 3D mesh generation by introducing a fine-grained reinforcement fine-tuning framework that enhances mesh quality through localized refinement. It employs a topology-aware scoring system to evaluate geometric integrity and topological regularity, achieving state-of-the-art performance in mesh generation.

title: Constructing a 3D Scene from a Single Image
figure: First framework that generates coherent 3D scenes from a single top-down image via region-based generation and spatial-aware inpainting.
abstract: SceneFuse-3D is a training-free framework that synthesizes coherent 3D scenes from a single top-down image. It employs region-based generation and spatial-aware inpainting to enhance image-to-3D alignment and ensure high-quality geometry. The method outperforms existing baselines in geometry quality and spatial coherence, demonstrating effective scene-level asset generation without 3D supervision.

title: PlantDreamer: Achieving Realistic 3D Plant Models with Diffusion-Guided Gaussian Splatting
figure: First method for realistic 3D plant generation via diffusion-guided Gaussian splatting
abstract: PlantDreamer is a novel approach for generating realistic 3D plant models, addressing challenges in complex plant geometry and textures. It combines a depth ControlNet, Low-Rank Adaptation, and Gaussian culling to enhance realism. The method supports both synthetic generation and enhancement of real-world plant point clouds, outperforming existing text-to-3D models in fidelity, making it useful fo

title: Sparc3D: Sparse Representation and Construction for High-Resolution 3D Shapes Modeling
figure: First unified framework for high-resolution 3D shape modeling via sparse representation and latent diffusion.
abstract: Sparc3D introduces a novel framework that combines a sparse deformable representation with a modality-consistent VAE for high-resolution 3D object synthesis. It addresses challenges in mesh data and volumetric grids, achieving state-of-the-art fidelity while preserving fine details and reducing costs, making it suitable for scalable 3D generation.

title: FreeMesh: Boosting Mesh Generation with Coordinates Merging
figure: First method to enhance mesh generation via a new tokenization technique using coordinate merging.
abstract: This paper presents a new metric, Per-Token-Mesh-Entropy (PTME), to evaluate mesh tokenizers and introduces a coordinate merging technique to improve compression ratios. The method aims to enhance existing mesh generation methods by optimizing tokenization, validated through experiments on various tokenization techniques.

title: Touch2Shape: Touch-Conditioned 3D Diffusion for Shape Exploration and Reconstruction
figure: First touch-conditioned diffusion model for shape exploration and reconstruction.
abstract: Touch2Shape introduces a touch-conditioned diffusion model to enhance 3D shape exploration and reconstruction. By utilizing tactile images, it captures local 3D information, addressing limitations of existing models. The approach includes a touch embedding module for shape representation and a reinforcement learning policy for exploration, improving reconstruction quality significantly.

title: TopoDiT-3D: Topology-Aware Diffusion Transformer with Bottleneck Structure for 3D Point Cloud Generation
figure: First topology-aware diffusion model for 3D point cloud generation.
abstract: TopoDiT-3D introduces a Topology-Aware Diffusion Transformer that enhances 3D point cloud generation by integrating global topological information. It employs a bottleneck structure to improve feature learning and training efficiency, outperforming existing models in visual quality and diversity. The work highlights the significance of topological data in maintaining shape consistency.

title: Step1X-3D: Towards High-Fidelity and Controllable Generation of Textured 3D Assets
figure: First framework that combines hybrid VAE-DiT geometry generation with diffusion-based texture synthesis for 3D asset generation.
abstract: Step1X-3D is an open framework for high-fidelity and controllable generation of textured 3D assets, addressing challenges in data scarcity and algorithmic limitations. It features a hybrid VAE-DiT architecture for geometry generation and a diffusion-based module for texture synthesis, achieving state-of-the-art performance and bridging 2D and 3D generation techniques.

title: Bridging Geometry-Coherent Text-to-3D Generation with Multi-View Diffusion Priors and Gaussian Splatting
figure: First framework that couples multi-view priors for geometrically consistent text-to-3D generation via Score Distillation Sampling.
abstract: This paper introduces Coupled Score Distillation (CSD), a method that enhances text-to-3D generation by addressing geometric inconsistencies through multi-view joint distribution priors. The approach optimizes 3D Gaussian Splatting and employs a deformable tetrahedral grid for high-quality mesh refinement, demonstrating improved efficiency and quality in generated 3D content.

title: Apply Hierarchical-Chain-of-Generation to Complex Attributes Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First automated method for text-to-3D generation of complex attribute objects via hierarchical chain decomposition.
abstract: The paper presents Hierarchical-Chain-of-Generation (HCoG), an automated method for text-to-3D generation that addresses challenges in creating objects with complex attributes. It utilizes a large language model to decompose descriptions into parts, ensuring coherent and attribute-faithful 3D object generation. The method improves upon existing approaches by optimizing the generation order and par

title: BuildingBlock: A Hybrid Approach for Structured Building Generation
figure: First hybrid approach for structured building generation via point-cloud and LLM integration
abstract: BuildingBlock is a hybrid method for generating diverse and structured 3D buildings, combining generative models, procedural content generation, and large language models. It features a two-phase pipeline for layout generation and building construction, achieving state-of-the-art results in architectural workflows.

title: TerraFusion: Joint Generation of Terrain Geometry and Texture Using Latent Diffusion Models
figure: First method to jointly generate terrain geometry and texture via latent diffusion models
abstract: TerraFusion introduces a novel approach for generating 3D terrain models by jointly creating heightmaps and textures using a latent diffusion model. The method captures the correlation between geometry and color, allowing for intuitive terrain generation while maintaining realism. It employs unsupervised training for paired generation and supervised learning for user control through sketches.

title: MeshGen: Generating PBR Textured Mesh with Render-Enhanced Auto-Encoder and Generative Data Augmentation
figure: First image-to-3D pipeline that generates high-quality PBR textured meshes via a render-enhanced auto-encoder.
abstract: MeshGen introduces an advanced image-to-3D pipeline that generates high-quality 3D meshes with detailed geometry and PBR textures. It addresses challenges in existing models through innovations like a render-enhanced point-to-shape auto-encoder and generative rendering augmentation techniques. Extensive experiments show that MeshGen outperforms previous methods in shape and texture generation.

title: Approximating Signed Distance Fields With Sparse Ellipsoidal Radial Basis Function Networks: A Dynamic Multi-Objective Optimization Strategy
figure: First method to approximate SDF fields using sparse ellipsoidal radial basis functions via dynamic multi-objective optimization.
abstract: This paper presents a method for approximating signed distance fields (SDFs) of implicit surfaces using a small number of ellipsoidal radial basis functions (ERBFs). The approach balances sparsity and precision through a dynamic optimization strategy, enhancing computational efficiency and accuracy. Experiments show significant improvements in representation with fewer parameters compared to exist

title: Rethinking Score Distilling Sampling for 3D Editing and Generation
figure: First method that unifies 3D generation and editing via score distillation.
abstract: This paper introduces Unified Distillation Sampling (UDS), which integrates generation and editing of 3D assets using score distillation sampling. UDS refines gradient terms to enhance both tasks, outperforming existing methods in generating detailed 3D assets and facilitating effective editing. The code is available on GitHub.

title: Autoencoder Models for Point Cloud Environmental Synthesis from WiFi Channel State Information: A Preliminary Study
figure: First method to generate point clouds from WiFi Channel State Information via a two-stage autoencoder.
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning framework that generates point clouds from WiFi Channel State Information using a two-stage autoencoder approach. It combines a PointNet autoencoder for point cloud generation with a CNN autoencoder to map CSI data to a latent space, enabling accurate environmental point cloud reconstruction. Experimental results demonstrate its effectiveness for wireless sensin

title: CoherenDream: Boosting Holistic Text Coherence in 3D Generation via Multimodal Large Language Models Feedback
figure: First study to incorporate MLLMs into SDS optimization for text-to-3D generation.
abstract: CoherenDream enhances text-to-3D generation by introducing a novel SDS objective that utilizes feedback from multimodal large language models to improve text-3D alignment. The framework includes a specialized MLLM for evaluating multiview text alignment and a layout initialization method for faster optimization. The approach shows consistent improvements across various metrics.

title: Point2Quad: Generating Quad Meshes from Point Clouds via Face Prediction
figure: First learning-based method for quad-only mesh generation from point clouds
abstract: Point2Quad introduces a novel approach for generating quad meshes from point clouds, addressing challenges like coplanarity and convexity. The method utilizes a k-NN-based candidate generation and employs encoders to extract geometric and topological features, ultimately refining results through a quad-specific post-processing. Experiments show its effectiveness over existing methods.

title: CLR-Wire: Towards Continuous Latent Representations for 3D Curve Wireframe Generation
figure: First framework for 3D curve wireframe generation using continuous latent representations via VAE.
abstract: CLR-Wire is a novel framework for generating 3D curve-based wireframes by integrating geometry and topology into a continuous latent representation. It employs a flow matching model to map Gaussian noise to latent spaces, enabling both unconditional and conditioned generation. The method shows significant improvements in accuracy and diversity for CAD design and 3D content creation.

title: Cyc3D: Fine-grained Controllable 3D Generation via Cycle Consistency Regularization
figure: First controllable 3D generation framework via cyclic consistency regularization
abstract: Cyc3D enhances controllable 3D generation by ensuring consistency between generated content and input conditions through a cyclic process. It employs a feed-forward backbone to generate 3D objects from input conditions and text prompts, improving alignment and detail preservation. Experiments show significant performance improvements over existing methods.

title: HiScene: Creating Hierarchical 3D Scenes with Isometric View Generation
figure: First hierarchical framework for 3D scene generation via video-diffusion-based amodal completion
abstract: HiScene introduces a hierarchical framework for generating 3D scenes that bridges 2D image generation and 3D object generation. It treats scenes as hierarchical objects, allowing for manipulatable items and ensuring spatial coherence. The method employs a video-diffusion-based technique for amodal completion, effectively managing occlusions and shadows, resulting in natural object arrangements and

title: SOPHY: Learning to Generate Simulation-Ready Objects with Physical Materials
figure: First model that generates simulation-ready 3D objects with physical materials via joint shape and material synthesis.
abstract: SOPHY is a generative model that synthesizes 3D shapes along with their texture and physical material properties, enabling the creation of objects ready for simulations. It introduces a dataset with detailed physical attributes and demonstrates improved realism in generated shapes through joint modeling of geometry and material properties.

title: ESCT3D: Efficient and Selectively Controllable Text-Driven 3D Content Generation with Gaussian Splatting
figure: First efficient and selectively controllable text-driven 3D content generation via Gaussian splatting.
abstract: ESCT3D addresses challenges in text-driven 3D content generation by enhancing efficiency and controllability. It allows users to provide simple text inputs and additional conditions for precise control, integrating multi-view information to improve results. The method demonstrates robust generalization and facilitates high-quality 3D content generation in a single attempt.

title: GaussVideoDreamer: 3D Scene Generation with Video Diffusion and Inconsistency-Aware Gaussian Splatting
figure: First 3D scene generation method that integrates video diffusion with Gaussian splatting for improved multiview consistency.
abstract: GaussVideoDreamer addresses challenges in single-image 3D scene reconstruction by combining image, video, and 3D generation. It introduces a progressive video inpainting strategy and a 3D Gaussian Splatting consistency mask to enhance multiview consistency and performance. Experimental results show significant improvements in quality and speed over existing methods.

title: OctGPT: Octree-based Multiscale Autoregressive Models for 3D Shape Generation
figure: First multiscale autoregressive model for 3D shape generation via octree representation
abstract: OctGPT is a novel multiscale autoregressive model that enhances 3D shape generation efficiency and performance, rivaling state-of-the-art diffusion models. It utilizes an octree representation for capturing hierarchical structures and employs a vector quantized variational autoencoder for fine details. The model supports various conditioning methods and demonstrates significant improvements in tra

title: Text To 3D Object Generation For Scalable Room Assembly
figure: First system that generates high-fidelity 3D object assets from text prompts via diffusion models.
abstract: This paper presents an end-to-end system for generating synthetic data for scalable 3D indoor scenes. By combining text-to-image and multi-view diffusion models with Neural Radiance Field meshing, it creates customizable 3D object assets from text prompts and integrates them into floor plans, addressing data scarcity in machine learning.

title: MASH: Masked Anchored SpHerical Distances for 3D Shape Representation and Generation
figure: First method to represent and generate 3D shapes using Masked Anchored Spherical Distances.
abstract: MASH introduces a novel representation of 3D shapes using Masked Anchored Spherical Distances, allowing for accurate shape generation and completion. It combines local surface patches with spherical distance functions and employs a differentiable optimization algorithm to convert point clouds into MASH representations, demonstrating versatility across various applications.

title: GeoTexBuild: 3D Building Model Generation from Map Footprints
figure: First modular framework that generates 3D building models from map footprints via a three-stage process.
abstract: GeoTexBuild is a generative framework that creates 3D building models from map footprints, aimed at architects and city planners. It employs a three-stage process for height map generation, geometry reconstruction, and appearance stylization, integrating ControlNet and Multi-view diffusion models to control geometric and visual attributes effectively.

title: ContrastiveGaussian: High-Fidelity 3D Generation with Contrastive Learning and Gaussian Splatting
figure: First high-fidelity 3D generation method using contrastive learning and Gaussian splatting via score distillation.
abstract: ContrastiveGaussian addresses the challenge of creating 3D content from single-view images by integrating contrastive learning into the generative process. It improves 3D generation quality by leveraging perceptual loss to differentiate between samples and incorporates a super-resolution model along with a Quantity-Aware Triplet Loss for better sample differentiation. The method achieves enhanced

title: QEMesh: Employing A Quadric Error Metrics-Based Representation for Mesh Generation
figure: First high-quality mesh generation method using a Quadric Error Metrics-based representation via a latent diffusion model.
abstract: QEMesh introduces a novel model for high-quality mesh generation by extending a Quadric Error Metrics-based representation. It utilizes a latent diffusion model with a multi-decoder VAE to generate parameters for mesh creation, addressing common issues in mesh generation such as surface realism and structural completeness. The method shows competitive performance against state-of-the-art technique

title: PRISM: Probabilistic Representation for Integrated Shape Modeling and Generation
figure: First compositional approach for 3D shape generation integrating categorical diffusion models with Statistical Shape Models and Gaussian Mixture Models.
abstract: PRISM addresses challenges in 3D shape generation by integrating categorical diffusion models with Statistical Shape Models and Gaussian Mixture Models. This novel approach captures part-level geometric variations and semantics, enabling high fidelity and diversity in generated shapes while maintaining structural coherence. Extensive experiments show significant improvements in quality and control

title: Interpretable Single-View 3D Gaussian Splatting using Unsupervised Hierarchical Disentangled Representation Learning
figure: First unsupervised interpretable 3D Gaussian Splatting via hierarchical disentangled representation learning
abstract: The paper presents 3DisGS, an interpretable single-view 3D Gaussian Splatting framework that enhances understanding of 3D semantics. It utilizes a dual-branch architecture for coarse- and fine-grained 3D semantic discovery, achieving high-quality and rapid reconstruction while maintaining interpretability.

title: ConsDreamer: Advancing Multi-View Consistency for Zero-Shot Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First method to address multi-view consistency in zero-shot text-to-3D generation via score distillation.
abstract: ConsDreamer enhances zero-shot text-to-3D generation by addressing view biases that cause inconsistencies in 3D object features across different views. It introduces a View Disentanglement Module to refine conditional prompts and a similarity-based loss for geometric consistency, demonstrating effective integration with various 3D representations.

title: VideoScene: Distilling Video Diffusion Model to Generate 3D Scenes in One Step
figure: First method to distill video diffusion model for direct 3D scene generation.
abstract: VideoScene introduces a novel approach to generate 3D scenes from video diffusion models in a single step. It addresses challenges in recovering 3D structures from sparse views by employing a 3D-aware leap flow distillation strategy, resulting in faster and more accurate scene generation compared to previous methods. The paper highlights its potential for future video-to-3D applications.

title: 3DBonsai: Structure-Aware Bonsai Modeling Using Conditioned 3D Gaussian Splatting
figure: First framework for generating complex bonsai structures via text-to-3D with 3D Gaussian priors
abstract: 3DBonsai introduces a novel text-to-3D framework for generating intricate bonsai structures, overcoming limitations of previous methods. It employs a trainable 3D space colonization algorithm and two generation pipelines to enhance structural detail. The approach significantly outperforms existing techniques and sets a new benchmark for structure-aware bonsai generation.

title: High-fidelity 3D Object Generation from Single Image with RGBN-Volume Gaussian Reconstruction Model
figure: First high-fidelity 3D object generation from single images via a hybrid Voxel-Gaussian model
abstract: This paper introduces GS-RGBN, a novel RGBN-volume Gaussian Reconstruction Model that generates high-fidelity 3D objects from single-view images. It addresses issues of geometric ambiguity and structure in 3D Gaussians by employing a hybrid Voxel-Gaussian representation, leading to improved reconstruction quality and efficiency.

title: Distilling Multi-view Diffusion Models into 3D Generators
figure: First to distill multi-view diffusion models into a 3D generator using Gaussian splatting.
abstract: DD3G distills a multi-view diffusion model into a 3D generator, enhancing generalization by simulating its ODE trajectory. It introduces a novel generator structure that efficiently fuses probabilistic flow and converts images into 3D Gaussians rapidly. The method addresses challenges in generation quality through a joint optimization objective and is validated on various datasets. The project is

title: MeshCraft: Exploring Efficient and Controllable Mesh Generation with Flow-based DiTs
figure: First efficient and controllable mesh generation framework via flow-based diffusion.
abstract: MeshCraft introduces a novel framework for efficient and controllable mesh generation, utilizing continuous spatial diffusion to create discrete triangle faces. It combines a transformer-based VAE for encoding and decoding meshes with a flow-based diffusion transformer that allows for predefined mesh face counts, achieving high-fidelity results significantly faster than previous methods.

title: DSO: Aligning 3D Generators with Simulation Feedback for Physical Soundness
figure: First framework that aligns 3D generators with simulation feedback for stable object generation via DPO/DRO
abstract: The DSO framework enhances 3D object generation by incorporating feedback from a physics simulator to ensure stability. It fine-tunes a feed-forward generator using stability scores, allowing for faster and more reliable production of self-supporting 3D objects without needing ground-truth data. This approach improves the likelihood of generating stable geometries compared to traditional optimizat

title: Hi3DGen: High-fidelity 3D Geometry Generation from Images via Normal Bridging
figure: First high-fidelity 3D geometry generation from images via normal bridging
abstract: Hi3DGen is a novel framework designed to generate high-fidelity 3D geometry from 2D images, addressing challenges in reproducing fine-grained details. It includes an image-to-normal estimator, a normal-to-geometry learning approach, and a 3D data synthesis pipeline. The framework shows superior performance in generating detailed 3D models compared to existing methods.

title: SparseFlex: High-Resolution and Arbitrary-Topology 3D Shape Modeling
figure: First high-resolution 3D shape generation with arbitrary topology via SparseFlex.
abstract: SparseFlex introduces a novel sparse-structured isosurface representation for high-fidelity 3D mesh modeling, enabling efficient reconstruction at resolutions up to 1024^3. It combines a variational autoencoder and a rectified flow transformer for generating detailed shapes with arbitrary topology, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy in reconstruction metrics.

title: Progressive Rendering Distillation: Adapting Stable Diffusion for Instant Text-to-Mesh Generation without 3D Data
figure: First text-to-mesh generator that adapts Stable Diffusion via Progressive Rendering Distillation.
abstract: This paper presents TriplaneTurbo, a novel text-to-mesh generator that utilizes Progressive Rendering Distillation to create high-quality 3D meshes from text prompts without requiring 3D training data. By distilling multi-view diffusion models, it enhances generation quality and speed, outperforming previous models in efficiency and quality.

title: Shape Generation via Weight Space Learning
figure: First exploration of weight space for 3D shape generation via submanifold modulation
abstract: This paper investigates the use of weight space in a large 3D shape-generative model to enhance shape generation and fine-tuning. It presents evidence that manipulating weight space can significantly affect topology and local geometry, even with limited data, suggesting new methods for 3D shape generation.

title: R2LDM: An Efficient 4D Radar Super-Resolution Framework Leveraging Diffusion Model
figure: First efficient framework for generating dense 4D radar point clouds via diffusion model.
abstract: R2LDM introduces a novel approach for generating dense 4D radar point clouds using a Latent Voxel Diffusion Model. By leveraging voxel features, it effectively captures 3D shape information and reconstructs point clouds from high-dimensional latent features. The method significantly enhances radar point clouds, improving downstream tasks like point cloud registration and object detection.

title: iFlame: Interleaving Full and Linear Attention for Efficient Mesh Generation
figure: First efficient mesh generation framework combining linear and full attention via interleaving.
abstract: iFlame introduces a transformer-based architecture for mesh generation that interleaves linear and full attention mechanisms to enhance efficiency while maintaining high-quality output. This approach reduces training time and improves inference speed, demonstrating effective performance on ShapeNet and Objaverse datasets.

title: SV4D 2.0: Enhancing Spatio-Temporal Consistency in Multi-View Video Diffusion for High-Quality 4D Generation
figure: Improved multi-view video diffusion for dynamic 3D asset generation.
abstract: SV4D 2.0 enhances the generation of dynamic 3D assets through a multi-view video diffusion model, improving robustness to occlusions and motion while producing higher-quality outputs. Key advancements include a new network architecture, enhanced training data, and a refined training strategy. The model shows significant performance improvements in detail and 4D consistency over its predecessor.

title: Unleashing Vecset Diffusion Model for Fast Shape Generation
figure: First framework to accelerate VAE and DiT in 3D shape generation via FlashVDM.
abstract: The Vecset Diffusion Model (VDM) has advanced 3D shape generation, but struggles with speed. FlashVDM addresses this by enhancing both VAE and DiT processes, allowing for rapid generation with fewer inference steps while maintaining quality. The model significantly outperforms existing methods, achieving faster inference times. Code and models are available at the provided GitHub link.

title: Repurposing 2D Diffusion Models with Gaussian Atlas for 3D Generation
figure: First to repurpose 2D diffusion models for 3D object generation using Gaussian Atlas.
abstract: This paper presents a method to adapt pre-trained 2D diffusion models for generating 3D objects by introducing Gaussian Atlas, which utilizes dense 2D grids. The approach includes a large dataset, GaussianVerse, with 205K 3D Gaussian fittings, demonstrating effective transfer learning and bridging the gap between 2D and 3D modeling.

title: VideoRFSplat: Direct Scene-Level Text-to-3D Gaussian Splatting Generation with Flexible Pose and Multi-View Joint Modeling
