It also demonstrates flexibility for tasks like appearance re-editing and part segmentation, outperforming existing methods in point

title: HD-Fusion: Detailed Text-to-3D Generation Leveraging Multiple Noise Estimation
figure: First detailed text-to-3D generation leveraging multiple noise estimation via 2D diffusion priors
abstract: This paper presents HD-Fusion, a method for text-to-3D generation that enhances model quality and detail by utilizing multiple noise estimation processes alongside a pretrained 2D diffusion prior. The approach aims to produce high-resolution 3D models, demonstrating improved detail compared to existing methods.

title: GET3D--: Learning GET3D from Unconstrained Image Collections
figure: First method that generates textured 3D shapes from 2D images with unknown pose and scale via a unified framework.
abstract: GET3D-- addresses the challenge of generating high-quality 3D models from 2D images with unknown camera poses and scales. It introduces a 3D shape generator and a learnable camera sampler to optimize the generation process. The method shows improved alignment and texture quality in generated shapes, validated through extensive experiments on various datasets.

title: 3D Semantic Subspace Traverser: Empowering 3D Generative Model with Shape Editing Capability
figure: First semantic generative model for category-specific 3D shape generation and editing via latent-space GAN.
abstract: This paper introduces the 3D Semantic Subspace Traverser, a generative model that incorporates semantic attributes for category-specific 3D shape generation and editing. By utilizing implicit functions and a latent-space GAN, the model allows for the manipulation of semantic attributes during shape generation, producing plausible shapes with complex structures. The code and models are available on

title: Points-to-3D: Bridging the Gap between Sparse Points and Shape-Controllable Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First framework that bridges sparse points and shape-controllable text-to-3D generation via score distillation.
abstract: Points-to-3D introduces a framework that enhances text-to-3D generation by using sparse 3D points as geometric priors. It addresses view inconsistency and arbitrary shapes in existing methods by employing a point cloud guidance loss and optimizing NeRF for better appearance consistency. The approach demonstrates improved shape controllability and view consistency in generated 3D models.

title: Patch-Wise Point Cloud Generation: A Divide-and-Conquer Approach
figure: First patch-wise point cloud generation framework using a divide-and-conquer approach
abstract: This paper presents a novel framework for generating high-fidelity 3D point clouds by dividing the generation process into patch-wise tasks. Utilizing learnable priors and transformers, the method captures local and global geometric structures, demonstrating superior performance on ShapeNet compared to existing methods.

title: Variational Autoencoding of Dental Point Clouds
figure: First probabilistic variational autoencoder for dental point clouds via VF-Net
abstract: This paper presents VF-Net, a variational autoencoder designed for dental point clouds, addressing challenges in digital dentistry. It introduces the FDI 16 dataset and improves computational efficiency by replacing Chamfer distance optimization with a suitable encoder. The method demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in dental sample generation and shape completion.

title: Sketch-A-Shape: Zero-Shot Sketch-to-3D Shape Generation
figure: First zero-shot sketch-to-3D shape generation using pre-trained vision model features
abstract: This paper explores the generation of 3D shapes from sketches using a 3D generative model conditioned on features from a pre-trained vision model. The approach allows for effective generation of multiple 3D shapes from a single sketch without requiring paired datasets, demonstrating resilience to domain shifts.

title: AutoDecoding Latent 3D Diffusion Models
figure: First 3D autodecoder framework for generating static and articulated 3D assets via diffusion from 2D images or monocular videos.
abstract: This paper introduces a 3D autodecoder framework that generates static and articulated 3D assets by embedding learned properties in latent space. It can decode into volumetric representations for consistent appearance and geometry, using either camera supervision or learning it during training. The method shows superior performance on various benchmark datasets.

title: Hybrid Neural Diffeomorphic Flow for Shape Representation and Generation via Triplane
figure: First method that generates 3D shapes using Hybrid Neural Diffeomorphic Flow with triplane features.
abstract: This paper introduces HNDF, a method for 3D shape representation and generation that utilizes hybrid supervision to learn dense correspondences and topology preservation. By employing diffeomorphic flows and triplane features, it generates diverse 3D shapes while ensuring consistency with template shapes. The approach is evaluated on medical image datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness in shape

title: DiT-3D: Exploring Plain Diffusion Transformers for 3D Shape Generation
figure: First Diffusion Transformer for 3D shape generation using voxelized point clouds
abstract: DiT-3D introduces a novel Diffusion Transformer for generating 3D shapes, operating directly on voxelized point clouds. It enhances scalability and quality compared to U-Net methods, incorporating 3D positional embeddings and window attention to manage computational costs. Experimental results show it achieves state-of-the-art performance in 3D point cloud generation on the ShapeNet dataset.

title: Magic123: One Image to High-Quality 3D Object Generation Using Both 2D and 3D Diffusion Priors
figure: First method to generate high-quality 3D meshes from a single image using 2D and 3D diffusion priors
abstract: Magic123 introduces a two-stage approach for generating high-quality textured 3D meshes from a single unposed image. It combines 2D and 3D diffusion priors to optimize a neural radiance field for coarse geometry and a differentiable mesh representation for high-resolution output. The method shows significant improvements over existing image-to-3D techniques.

title: Michelangelo: Conditional 3D Shape Generation based on Shape-Image-Text Aligned Latent Representation
figure: First conditional 3D shape generator using shape-image-text aligned latent representation via VAE and diffusion.
abstract: This paper introduces a novel approach for generating 3D shapes from 2D images or texts by aligning the three modalities in a shared latent space. It employs a VAE to encode 3D shapes and a diffusion model to map from image/text to shape space, resulting in higher-quality and diverse 3D shape generation that aligns with the given conditions.

title: DreamEditor: Text-Driven 3D Scene Editing with Neural Fields
figure: First framework for text-driven editing of neural fields via score distillation.
abstract: DreamEditor is a framework that enables controlled editing of neural fields using text prompts. It represents scenes as mesh-based neural fields, allowing localized editing. By leveraging a pretrained text-to-image diffusion model, it identifies editing regions based on text semantics and aligns geometry and texture through score distillation. The method shows significant improvements in editing r

title: DreamTime: An Improved Optimization Strategy for Diffusion-Guided 3D Generation
figure: First to improve 3D generation via optimized timestep sampling in diffusion models
abstract: This paper addresses limitations in 3D content creation from text-to-image diffusion models by proposing an improved optimization strategy. The authors identify issues with slow convergence and low diversity in generated 3D models and suggest a method to align the 3D optimization process with the sampling process of diffusion models, resulting in enhanced quality and diversity.

title: Pushing the Limits of 3D Shape Generation at Scale
figure: First large-scale 3D shape generation model with 3.6 billion parameters via Auto-Regressive approach
abstract: This paper introduces Argus-3D, the largest 3D shape generation model to date, utilizing an Auto-Regressive model with 3.6 billion parameters. It enhances the quality and diversity of generated shapes through tri-plane features and a discrete codebook for efficient quantization. The model is trained on a diverse dataset of approximately 900,000 objects, demonstrating significant improvements in vi

title: 3D VR Sketch Guided 3D Shape Prototyping and Exploration
figure: First 3D shape generation network that uses 3D VR sketches as a condition via normalizing flow.
abstract: This paper presents a 3D shape generation network that utilizes 3D VR sketches created by novices to generate geometrically realistic 3D shapes. The method addresses sketch ambiguity by producing multiple 3D shapes that correspond to the sketch's structure, employing normalizing flow for latent space distribution modeling and a dedicated loss for fidelity to the input sketch. The code is available

title: Point-Cloud Completion with Pretrained Text-to-image Diffusion Models
figure: First point-cloud completion method that uses pretrained text-to-image diffusion models.
abstract: SDS-Complete addresses the challenge of incomplete point-cloud data by leveraging text-guided image generation techniques. It utilizes a pre-trained diffusion model to complete point clouds based on the semantics of the input data, achieving significant improvements in reconstruction quality for out-of-distribution objects. The method shows a 50% reduction in Chamfer loss compared to existing appr

title: ZeroForge: Feedforward Text-to-Shape Without 3D Supervision
figure: First zero-shot text-to-shape generation method that avoids 3D supervision via feed-forward architecture.
abstract: ZeroForge introduces a novel approach for text-to-shape generation that operates without the need for supervised training or expensive inference-time optimization. By adapting existing feed-forward models and employing a combination of data-free CLIP-loss and contrastive losses, it enhances the generative capabilities of text-to-shape models, achieving open-vocabulary shape generation.

title: InstructP2P: Learning to Edit 3D Point Clouds with Text Instructions
figure: First framework for 3D shape editing on point clouds guided by text instructions via diffusion.
abstract: InstructP2P is an end-to-end framework that enables 3D shape editing on point clouds using high-level textual instructions. It combines a text-conditioned point cloud diffusion model with language models to allow for color and geometry editing. The method demonstrates generalization to new shape categories and instructions, showcasing its adaptability.

title: Aladdin: Zero-Shot Hallucination of Stylized 3D Assets from Abstract Scene Descriptions
figure: First system to generate stylized 3D assets from abstract scene descriptions via foundation models
abstract: The paper presents a system that generates stylized 3D assets based on abstract scene descriptions, leveraging foundation models for creative freedom. It allows 3D artists to create assets without specifying object details, demonstrating improved fidelity to scene semantics compared to traditional methods.

title: SENS: Part-Aware Sketch-based Implicit Neural Shape Modeling
figure: First part-aware sketch-based implicit neural shape modeling method for 3D generation and editing.
abstract: SENS is a method for generating and editing 3D models from hand-drawn sketches, including abstract ones. It maps sketches into a part-aware neural implicit shape architecture, allowing intuitive generation and editing of 3D shapes. The model captures user intent and supports part reconstruction for nuanced adjustments, demonstrating strong performance in user studies.

title: DiffInDScene: Diffusion-based High-Quality 3D Indoor Scene Generation
figure: First diffusion-based framework for high-quality indoor scene generation via cascaded 3D diffusion pipeline
abstract: DiffInDScene introduces a novel framework for high-quality 3D indoor scene generation using a cascaded diffusion pipeline. It efficiently generates room-level geometry in a coarse-to-fine manner, leveraging a diffusion-based SDF fusion approach. The results show superior mesh quality compared to ground truth data, and the method can also refine multi-view stereo reconstructions.

title: NAP: Neural 3D Articulation Prior
figure: First 3D deep generative model to synthesize articulated object models via diffusion.
abstract: The paper introduces Neural 3D Articulation Prior (NAP), a generative model designed to synthesize 3D articulated objects. It employs a novel articulation tree parameterization and a diffusion-denoising model to generate articulated objects from random graphs. The approach captures the interplay between geometry and motion structure, demonstrating high performance in generating articulated objects

title: Sin3DM: Learning a Diffusion Model from a Single 3D Textured Shape
figure: First diffusion model that generates variations from a single 3D textured shape via triplane feature maps
abstract: Sin3DM is a diffusion model that learns from a single 3D textured shape to generate high-quality variations with detailed geometry and texture. It compresses the input into a lower-dimensional latent space and uses triplane feature maps for representation. The model supports applications like retargeting and local editing, demonstrating superior generation quality compared to previous methods.

title: Few-shot 3D Shape Generation
figure: First attempt to realize few-shot 3D shape generation by adapting generative models.
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach for few-shot 3D shape generation, adapting pre-trained generative models to target domains with limited data. By maintaining probability distributions of pairwise relative distances, the method generates diverse shapes using only silhouettes of target samples. The effectiveness is demonstrated through qualitative and quantitative evaluations.

title: Towards Better Gradient Consistency for Neural Signed Distance Functions via Level Set Alignment
figure: First method to improve SDF inference accuracy via level set alignment loss
abstract: This paper introduces a level set alignment loss to enhance the gradient consistency of neural signed distance functions (SDFs) when inferring from point clouds or multi-view images. By aligning level sets to the zero level set, the method aims to improve the accuracy of SDF representations, addressing challenges posed by discrete point clouds and limited observations. The proposed loss can be app

title: Locally Attentional SDF Diffusion for Controllable 3D Shape Generation
figure: First controllable 3D shape generation via locally attentional SDF diffusion.
abstract: This paper presents a diffusion-based framework for 3D shape generation that enhances user control over local geometry. It employs a two-stage model to generate occupancy fields and signed distance fields, utilizing a local attention mechanism for improved feature learning from 2D sketches. The method demonstrates superior controllability and generalizability in generating diverse 3D shapes.

title: DiffFacto: Controllable Part-Based 3D Point Cloud Generation with Cross Diffusion
figure: First controllable part-based 3D point cloud generation via cross diffusion.
abstract: DiffFacto introduces a probabilistic generative model for controllable part-based 3D point cloud generation. It learns independent distributions for part style and configuration, enabling coherent shape generation with multiple control axes. The method achieves state-of-the-art quality and supports various editing applications.

title: Towards AI-Architecture Liberty: A Comprehensive Survey on Design and Generation of Virtual Architecture by Deep Learning
figure: Comprehensive survey on 3D shape generation techniques for virtual architecture using deep learning.
abstract: This survey reviews 149 articles on 3D shape generation and architectural design, identifying principles and challenges in virtual architecture. It discusses the latest approaches and highlights research agendas for enhancing designer-AI collaboration in generating virtual buildings.

title: GenCorres: Consistent Shape Matching via Coupled Implicit-Explicit Shape Generative Models
figure: First unsupervised joint shape matching approach using coupled implicit-explicit shape generative models.
abstract: GenCorres introduces a novel unsupervised joint shape matching method that learns a mesh generator to fit a large collection of unorganized deformable shapes. It preserves geometric structures through deformation constraints and outperforms existing techniques by leveraging a larger synthetic shape collection. The method also provides a concise encoding of consistent shape correspondences.

title: NeuralField-LDM: Scene Generation with Hierarchical Latent Diffusion Models
figure: First hierarchical latent diffusion model for complex 3D scene generation
abstract: NeuralField-LDM is a generative model that synthesizes complex 3D environments using Latent Diffusion Models. It employs a scene auto-encoder to represent image and pose pairs as neural fields, which are then compressed into latent representations. A hierarchical diffusion model is used for scene generation, achieving significant improvements over existing methods and enabling various 3D content c

title: Control3Diff: Learning Controllable 3D Diffusion Models from Single-view Images
figure: First controllable 3D diffusion model for single-view image synthesis
abstract: Control3Diff is a 3D diffusion model that integrates the strengths of diffusion models and 3D GANs, enabling controllable 3D-aware image synthesis from single-view datasets. It models the latent distribution for direct control during the diffusion process and is validated on various benchmarks using different conditioning inputs.

title: Lift3D: Synthesize 3D Training Data by Lifting 2D GAN to 3D Generative Radiance Field
figure: First 3D data generation framework that lifts 2D GAN to 3D generative radiance field.
abstract: Lift3D proposes a novel framework for synthesizing photorealistic 3D training data from 2D GANs, addressing limitations of existing NeRF-based 3D GANs. It allows for higher resolution outputs and provides explicit 3D information, enhancing performance in 3D vision tasks like object detection. The framework is evaluated on autonomous driving datasets, showing significant improvements.

title: DITTO-NeRF: Diffusion-based Iterative Text To Omni-directional 3D Model
figure: First method to generate high-quality 3D NeRF models from text prompts or single images via iterative reconstruction.
abstract: DITTO-NeRF addresses challenges in creating high-quality 3D models from text or images by proposing a novel pipeline that iteratively reconstructs 3D NeRF models. It improves fidelity and diversity while reducing training times compared to existing methods, utilizing progressive reconstruction schemes.

title: $\infty$-Diff: Infinite Resolution Diffusion with Subsampled Mollified States
figure: First infinite resolution diffusion model that operates in an infinite-dimensional Hilbert space.
abstract: $\infty$-Diff is a generative diffusion model that enables infinite resolution data generation by training on randomly sampled coordinates. It uses non-local integral operators for spatial context aggregation and a mollified diffusion process for smoothing. The model shows high-quality results even at significant subsampling rates, offering efficiency and scalability beyond training resolution.

title: HyperDiffusion: Generating Implicit Neural Fields with Weight-Space Diffusion
figure: First approach for unconditional generative modeling of implicit neural fields via weight-space diffusion
abstract: HyperDiffusion introduces a novel method for generating implicit neural fields by operating directly on MLP weights. It trains a diffusion process in the weight space to synthesize new neural implicit fields, enabling high-fidelity representation of complex 3D shapes and 4D mesh animations.

title: StarNet: Style-Aware 3D Point Cloud Generation
figure: First style-aware network for high-fidelity 3D point cloud generation via disentangled latent codes
abstract: StarNet addresses the challenges in 3D point cloud generation by introducing a style-aware network that combines point-wise distance loss and adversarial loss. This approach enables the generation of high-fidelity point clouds while effectively disentangling the Gaussian prior from high-level attributes, resulting in improved quality and efficiency compared to existing methods.

title: Learning Versatile 3D Shape Generation with Improved AR Models
figure: First improved auto-regressive model for 3D shape generation via discrete representation learning.
abstract: The Improved Auto-regressive Model (ImAM) addresses limitations in 3D shape generation by using discrete representation learning based on a latent vector, reducing computational costs while preserving geometric details. It supports both unconditional and conditional generation with various inputs, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance in synthesizing diverse shapes across multiple categories.

title: Unsupervised Inference of Signed Distance Functions from Single Sparse Point Clouds without Learning Priors
figure: First method to infer SDFs from single sparse point clouds without learned priors via a neural network.
abstract: This paper presents a method for inferring signed distance functions (SDFs) from single sparse point clouds without relying on learned priors or signed distance supervision. The approach utilizes parameterized surfaces for coarse surface sampling and employs a thin plate splines-based network to infer SDFs, enhancing generalization and accuracy for unseen point clouds. Experimental results demonst

title: Fantasia3D: Disentangling Geometry and Appearance for High-quality Text-to-3D Content Creation
figure: First method for high-quality text-to-3D content creation via disentangled geometry and appearance modeling.
abstract: Fantasia3D introduces a novel approach for text-to-3D content creation by disentangling geometry and appearance. It utilizes a hybrid scene representation and incorporates surface normals into an image diffusion model for improved geometry learning. The method also integrates a spatially varying BRDF for realistic rendering, enhancing compatibility with graphics engines. Experiments demonstrate it

title: CompoNeRF: Text-guided Multi-object Compositional NeRF with Editable 3D Scene Layout
figure: First text-guided framework for editable multi-object scene generation via NeRF.
abstract: CompoNeRF addresses the challenge of generating consistent multi-object environments from text prompts by integrating an editable 3D scene layout with object-specific guidance. It enhances scene fidelity and allows for flexible editing and recomposition of scenes, achieving significant improvements in semantic accuracy and multi-view consistency.

title: DreamStone: Image as Stepping Stone for Text-Guided 3D Shape Generation
figure: First text-guided 3D shape generation that uses images as a stepping stone via feature-space alignment.
abstract: DreamStone introduces a novel approach for text-guided 3D shape generation that utilizes images to connect text and shape modalities. It employs a two-stage feature-space alignment strategy with a pre-trained single-view reconstruction model and incorporates a text-guided 3D shape stylization module. The method enhances generative diversity and fidelity, outperforming existing techniques in qualit

title: TAPS3D: Text-Guided 3D Textured Shape Generation from Pseudo Supervision
figure: First text-guided 3D textured shape generator using pseudo supervision
abstract: TAPS3D introduces a framework for generating controllable 3D textured shapes from textual descriptions without requiring ground truth captions. It utilizes pseudo captions derived from 2D images and incorporates low-level image regularization to enhance texture and geometry diversity. The model can generate high-fidelity 3D shapes directly from text during inference.

title: VecFontSDF: Learning to Reconstruct and Synthesize High-quality Vector Fonts via Signed Distance Functions
figure: First method to synthesize vector fonts using signed distance functions.
abstract: VecFontSDF introduces an end-to-end trainable approach for reconstructing and synthesizing high-quality vector fonts through signed distance functions. It models each glyph as shape primitives and converts them to quadratic Bézier curves, enabling effective font design automation. Experiments show significant improvements over existing methods in vector font tasks.

title: SALAD: Part-Level Latent Diffusion for 3D Shape Generation and Manipulation
figure: First part-level latent diffusion model for 3D shape generation and manipulation.
abstract: SALAD introduces a cascaded diffusion model utilizing a part-level implicit 3D representation, achieving state-of-the-art generation quality. It allows for part-level shape editing and manipulation without additional training. The model enhances completion and editing tasks by learning diffusion in both low and high-dimensional subspaces, outperforming previous methods in experiments.

title: Compositional 3D Scene Generation using Locally Conditioned Diffusion
figure: First compositional 3D scene generation using locally conditioned diffusion with text prompts.
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to compositional 3D scene generation using locally conditioned diffusion, allowing for control over semantic parts with text prompts and bounding boxes. The method enhances fidelity in scene generation compared to existing object-level approaches.

title: CC3D: Layout-Conditioned Generation of Compositional 3D Scenes
figure: First conditional generative model for compositional 3D scene generation via 2D layouts
abstract: CC3D is a conditional generative model that synthesizes complex 3D scenes based on 2D semantic layouts, trained with single-view images. Unlike traditional 3D GANs focused on single objects, it generates scenes with multiple objects by modeling their compositional nature. The model features a new 3D field representation and demonstrates improved visual and geometric quality in scene generation.

title: 3D-CLFusion: Fast Text-to-3D Rendering with Contrastive Latent Diffusion
figure: First fast text-to-3D rendering method using contrastive latent diffusion with pre-trained NeRFs
abstract: 3D-CLFusion addresses the challenges of text-to-3D creation by leveraging pre-trained latent-based NeRFs, enabling fast 3D content generation in under a minute. The method introduces a latent diffusion prior network that learns from CLIP text/image embeddings, allowing for high-resolution multi-view synthesis without further optimization during inference. The approach is significantly faster than

title: FullFormer: Generating Shapes Inside Shapes
figure: First implicit generative model that generates complex 3D shapes with internal geometric details via a transformer-based autoregressive approach
abstract: FullFormer introduces an implicit generative model that generates complex 3D shapes with rich internal details using unsigned distance fields. It employs a transformer-based autoregressive model to decode context-rich tokens from vector quantized shape embeddings into 3D shapes. The model achieves state-of-the-art results in point cloud generation for various ShapeNet classes, particularly focusin

title: 3DQD: Generalized Deep 3D Shape Prior via Part-Discretized Diffusion Process
figure: First generalized 3D shape prior model for multiple tasks via part-discretized diffusion process
abstract: This paper presents a generalized 3D shape generation prior model that supports various tasks such as unconditional shape generation and point cloud completion. It employs a vector quantized variational autoencoder for local geometry indexing and a discrete diffusion generator to model structural dependencies among tokens, achieving high-fidelity and diverse shape features.

title: PartNeRF: Generating Part-Aware Editable 3D Shapes without 3D Supervision
figure: First part-aware generative model for editable 3D shape synthesis without 3D supervision
abstract: PartNeRF introduces a novel part-aware generative model that enables editable 3D shape synthesis without requiring explicit 3D supervision. By generating objects as a set of locally defined NeRFs, the model allows for local control and editing of shapes, enhancing content creation applications. Evaluations show improved fidelity in generating editable 3D objects compared to previous methods.

title: Controllable Mesh Generation Through Sparse Latent Point Diffusion Models
figure: First mesh generation method using sparse latent point diffusion models for improved efficiency and controllability.
abstract: This paper presents a novel sparse latent point diffusion model for mesh generation, treating point clouds as an intermediate representation. By encoding point clouds into sparse latent points, the method enhances efficiency and allows for explicit control over mesh structures and details. Experiments on the ShapeNet dataset demonstrate superior performance in generation quality and controllabilit

title: SDF-3DGAN: A 3D Object Generative Method Based on Implicit Signed Distance Function
figure: First 3D object generation method using implicit Signed Distance Function via GAN.
abstract: SDF-3DGAN introduces a novel method for 3D object generation using implicit Signed Distance Function (SDF) as a representation. It enhances rendering efficiency and accuracy, allowing for high-quality 3D object generation from 2D images. The method addresses sampling ambiguity and improves surface smoothness, demonstrating superior performance in benchmarks for 3D object generation and 3D-aware im

title: 3DGen: Triplane Latent Diffusion for Textured Mesh Generation
figure: First triplane latent diffusion model for generating textured meshes via a two-step pipeline
abstract: 3DGen introduces a two-step pipeline combining a triplane VAE for learning latent representations of textured meshes and a conditional diffusion model for generating triplane features. This architecture enables both conditional and unconditional generation of high-quality 3D meshes across various categories, significantly improving upon previous methods in terms of mesh and texture quality. The mo

title: 3D generation on ImageNet
figure: First 3D generator with Generic Priors that scales to diverse datasets via GAN.
abstract: This paper presents 3DGP, a 3D synthesis framework that addresses the limitations of existing 3D-from-2D generators by incorporating a depth estimator, a flexible camera model, and knowledge distillation techniques. It demonstrates improved stability and convergence in training, outperforming state-of-the-art methods on challenging datasets like ImageNet.

title: Neural Wavelet-domain Diffusion for 3D Shape Generation, Inversion, and Manipulation
figure: First approach for 3D shape generation in wavelet domain via diffusion.
abstract: This paper introduces a method for 3D shape generation, inversion, and manipulation using a continuous implicit representation in the wavelet domain. It employs a diffusion-based generator and a detail predictor to create diverse shapes and fine structures. The approach allows for rich shape manipulations and demonstrates superior performance compared to existing methods.

title: Zero3D: Semantic-Driven Multi-Category 3D Shape Generation
figure: First multi-category 3D shape generator that uses a conditional diffusion model via CLIP embeddings
abstract: Zero3D introduces a multi-category conditional diffusion model for generating 3D shapes based on text. It addresses challenges in single-category generation and data scarcity by leveraging the CLIP model to connect text, 2D images, and 3D shapes, while employing a conditional flow model to enhance feature extraction and reduce training time.

title: 3DShape2VecSet: A 3D Shape Representation for Neural Fields and Generative Diffusion Models
figure: First shape representation for neural fields tailored for generative diffusion models.
abstract: 3DShape2VecSet is a novel shape representation for neural fields aimed at generative diffusion models. It encodes 3D shapes as neural fields using a set of vectors, enhancing performance in 3D shape encoding and generative modeling. The method supports various generative applications, including unconditioned, category-conditioned, text-conditioned, and image-conditioned generation, as well as poin

title: SCARP: 3D Shape Completion in ARbitrary Poses for Improved Grasping
figure: First model for 3D shape completion in arbitrary poses via a single network
abstract: SCARP addresses the challenge of recovering full 3D shapes from partial observations by learning a disentangled feature representation of pose and shape. It performs canonicalization, pose estimation, and shape completion in one network, significantly improving grasp proposals on tabletop objects. The model shows a 45% performance increase over existing methods and a 71.2% improvement in grasp pro
